8256					n->reset_default_tblspc = false;
8257					$$ = (Node *) n;
8258				}
8259		;
8260
8261opt_unique:
8262			UNIQUE									{ $$ = true; }
8263			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
8264		;
8265
8266access_method_clause:
8267			USING name								{ $$ = $2; }
8268			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = DEFAULT_INDEX_TYPE; }
8269		;
8270
8271index_params:	index_elem							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8272			| index_params ',' index_elem			{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8273		;
8274
8275
8276index_elem_options:
8277	opt_collate opt_qualified_name opt_asc_desc opt_nulls_order
8278		{
8279			$$ = makeNode(IndexElem);
8280			$$->name = NULL;
8281			$$->expr = NULL;
8282			$$->indexcolname = NULL;
8283			$$->collation = $1;
8284			$$->opclass = $2;
8285			$$->opclassopts = NIL;
8286			$$->ordering = $3;
8287			$$->nulls_ordering = $4;
8288		}
8289	| opt_collate any_name reloptions opt_asc_desc opt_nulls_order
8290		{
8291			$$ = makeNode(IndexElem);
8292			$$->name = NULL;
8293			$$->expr = NULL;
8294			$$->indexcolname = NULL;
8295			$$->collation = $1;
8296			$$->opclass = $2;
8297			$$->opclassopts = $3;
8298			$$->ordering = $4;
8299			$$->nulls_ordering = $5;
8300		}
8301	;
8302
8303/*
8304 * Index attributes can be either simple column references, or arbitrary
8305 * expressions in parens.  For backwards-compatibility reasons, we allow
8306 * an expression that's just a function call to be written without parens.
8307 */
8308index_elem: ColId index_elem_options
8309				{
8310					$$ = $2;
8311					$$->name = $1;
8312				}
8313			| func_expr_windowless index_elem_options
8314				{
8315					$$ = $2;
8316					$$->expr = $1;
8317				}
8318			| '(' a_expr ')' index_elem_options
8319				{
8320					$$ = $4;
8321					$$->expr = $2;
8322				}
8323		;
8324
8325opt_include:		INCLUDE '(' index_including_params ')'			{ $$ = $3; }
8326			 |		/* EMPTY */						{ $$ = NIL; }
8327		;
8328
8329index_including_params:	index_elem						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8330			| index_including_params ',' index_elem		{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8331		;
8332
8333opt_collate: COLLATE any_name						{ $$ = $2; }
8334			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
8335		;
8336
8337
8338opt_asc_desc: ASC							{ $$ = SORTBY_ASC; }
8339			| DESC							{ $$ = SORTBY_DESC; }
8340			| /*EMPTY*/						{ $$ = SORTBY_DEFAULT; }
8341		;
8342
8343opt_nulls_order: NULLS_LA FIRST_P			{ $$ = SORTBY_NULLS_FIRST; }
8344			| NULLS_LA LAST_P				{ $$ = SORTBY_NULLS_LAST; }
8345			| /*EMPTY*/						{ $$ = SORTBY_NULLS_DEFAULT; }
8346		;
8347
8348
8349/*****************************************************************************
8350 *
8351 *		QUERY:
8352 *				create [or replace] function <fname>
8353 *						[(<type-1> { , <type-n>})]
8354 *						returns <type-r>
8355 *						as <filename or code in language as appropriate>
8356 *						language <lang> [with parameters]
8357 *
8358 *****************************************************************************/
8359
8360CreateFunctionStmt:
8361			CREATE opt_or_replace FUNCTION func_name func_args_with_defaults
8362			RETURNS func_return opt_createfunc_opt_list opt_routine_body
8363				{
8364					CreateFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(CreateFunctionStmt);
8365
8366					n->is_procedure = false;
8367					n->replace = $2;
8368					n->funcname = $4;
8369					n->parameters = $5;
8370					n->returnType = $7;
8371					n->options = $8;
8372					n->sql_body = $9;
8373					$$ = (Node *) n;
8374				}
8375			| CREATE opt_or_replace FUNCTION func_name func_args_with_defaults
8376			  RETURNS TABLE '(' table_func_column_list ')' opt_createfunc_opt_list opt_routine_body
8377				{
8378					CreateFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(CreateFunctionStmt);
8379
8380					n->is_procedure = false;
8381					n->replace = $2;
8382					n->funcname = $4;
8383					n->parameters = mergeTableFuncParameters($5, $9, yyscanner);
8384					n->returnType = TableFuncTypeName($9);
8385					n->returnType->location = @7;
8386					n->options = $11;
8387					n->sql_body = $12;
8388					$$ = (Node *) n;
8389				}
8390			| CREATE opt_or_replace FUNCTION func_name func_args_with_defaults
8391			  opt_createfunc_opt_list opt_routine_body
8392				{
8393					CreateFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(CreateFunctionStmt);
8394
8395					n->is_procedure = false;
8396					n->replace = $2;
8397					n->funcname = $4;
8398					n->parameters = $5;
8399					n->returnType = NULL;
8400					n->options = $6;
8401					n->sql_body = $7;
8402					$$ = (Node *) n;
8403				}
8404			| CREATE opt_or_replace PROCEDURE func_name func_args_with_defaults
8405			  opt_createfunc_opt_list opt_routine_body
8406				{
8407					CreateFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(CreateFunctionStmt);
8408
8409					n->is_procedure = true;
8410					n->replace = $2;
8411					n->funcname = $4;
8412					n->parameters = $5;
8413					n->returnType = NULL;
8414					n->options = $6;
8415					n->sql_body = $7;
8416					$$ = (Node *) n;
8417				}
8418		;
8419
8420opt_or_replace:
8421			OR REPLACE								{ $$ = true; }
8422			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
8423		;
8424
8425func_args:	'(' func_args_list ')'					{ $$ = $2; }
8426			| '(' ')'								{ $$ = NIL; }
8427		;
8428
8429func_args_list:
8430			func_arg								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8431			| func_args_list ',' func_arg			{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8432		;
8433
8434function_with_argtypes_list:
8435			function_with_argtypes					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8436			| function_with_argtypes_list ',' function_with_argtypes
8437													{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8438		;
8439
8440function_with_argtypes:
8441			func_name func_args
8442				{
8443					ObjectWithArgs *n = makeNode(ObjectWithArgs);
8444
8445					n->objname = $1;
8446					n->objargs = extractArgTypes($2);
8447					n->objfuncargs = $2;
8448					$$ = n;
8449				}
8450			/*
8451			 * Because of reduce/reduce conflicts, we can't use func_name
8452			 * below, but we can write it out the long way, which actually
8453			 * allows more cases.
8454			 */
8455			| type_func_name_keyword
8456				{
8457					ObjectWithArgs *n = makeNode(ObjectWithArgs);
8458
8459					n->objname = list_make1(makeString(pstrdup($1)));
8460					n->args_unspecified = true;
8461					$$ = n;
8462				}
8463			| ColId
8464				{
8465					ObjectWithArgs *n = makeNode(ObjectWithArgs);
8466
8467					n->objname = list_make1(makeString($1));
8468					n->args_unspecified = true;
8469					$$ = n;
8470				}
8471			| ColId indirection
8472				{
8473					ObjectWithArgs *n = makeNode(ObjectWithArgs);
8474
8475					n->objname = check_func_name(lcons(makeString($1), $2),
8476												  yyscanner);
8477					n->args_unspecified = true;
8478					$$ = n;
8479				}
8480		;
8481
8482/*
8483 * func_args_with_defaults is separate because we only want to accept
8484 * defaults in CREATE FUNCTION, not in ALTER etc.
8485 */
8486func_args_with_defaults:
8487		'(' func_args_with_defaults_list ')'		{ $$ = $2; }
8488		| '(' ')'									{ $$ = NIL; }
8489		;
8490
8491func_args_with_defaults_list:
8492		func_arg_with_default						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8493		| func_args_with_defaults_list ',' func_arg_with_default
8494													{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8495		;
8496
8497/*
8498 * The style with arg_class first is SQL99 standard, but Oracle puts
8499 * param_name first; accept both since it's likely people will try both
8500 * anyway.  Don't bother trying to save productions by letting arg_class
8501 * have an empty alternative ... you'll get shift/reduce conflicts.
8502 *
8503 * We can catch over-specified arguments here if we want to,
8504 * but for now better to silently swallow typmod, etc.
8505 * - thomas 2000-03-22
8506 */
8507func_arg:
8508			arg_class param_name func_type
8509				{
8510					FunctionParameter *n = makeNode(FunctionParameter);
8511
8512					n->name = $2;
8513					n->argType = $3;
8514					n->mode = $1;
8515					n->defexpr = NULL;
8516					n->location = @1;
8517					$$ = n;
8518				}
8519			| param_name arg_class func_type
8520				{
8521					FunctionParameter *n = makeNode(FunctionParameter);
8522
8523					n->name = $1;
8524					n->argType = $3;
8525					n->mode = $2;
8526					n->defexpr = NULL;
8527					n->location = @1;
8528					$$ = n;
8529				}
8530			| param_name func_type
8531				{
8532					FunctionParameter *n = makeNode(FunctionParameter);
8533
8534					n->name = $1;
8535					n->argType = $2;
8536					n->mode = FUNC_PARAM_DEFAULT;
8537					n->defexpr = NULL;
8538					n->location = @1;
8539					$$ = n;
8540				}
8541			| arg_class func_type
8542				{
8543					FunctionParameter *n = makeNode(FunctionParameter);
8544
8545					n->name = NULL;
8546					n->argType = $2;
8547					n->mode = $1;
8548					n->defexpr = NULL;
8549					n->location = @1;
8550					$$ = n;
8551				}
8552			| func_type
8553				{
8554					FunctionParameter *n = makeNode(FunctionParameter);
8555
8556					n->name = NULL;
8557					n->argType = $1;
8558					n->mode = FUNC_PARAM_DEFAULT;
8559					n->defexpr = NULL;
8560					n->location = @1;
8561					$$ = n;
8562				}
8563		;
8564
8565/* INOUT is SQL99 standard, IN OUT is for Oracle compatibility */
8566arg_class:	IN_P								{ $$ = FUNC_PARAM_IN; }
8567			| OUT_P								{ $$ = FUNC_PARAM_OUT; }
8568			| INOUT								{ $$ = FUNC_PARAM_INOUT; }
8569			| IN_P OUT_P						{ $$ = FUNC_PARAM_INOUT; }
8570			| VARIADIC							{ $$ = FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC; }
8571		;
8572
8573/*
8574 * Ideally param_name should be ColId, but that causes too many conflicts.
8575 */
8576param_name:	type_function_name
8577		;
8578
8579func_return:
8580			func_type
8581				{
8582					/* We can catch over-specified results here if we want to,
8583					 * but for now better to silently swallow typmod, etc.
8584					 * - thomas 2000-03-22
8585					 */
8586					$$ = $1;
8587				}
8588		;
8589
8590/*
8591 * We would like to make the %TYPE productions here be ColId attrs etc,
8592 * but that causes reduce/reduce conflicts.  type_function_name
8593 * is next best choice.
8594 */
8595func_type:	Typename								{ $$ = $1; }
8596			| type_function_name attrs '%' TYPE_P
8597				{
8598					$$ = makeTypeNameFromNameList(lcons(makeString($1), $2));
8599					$$->pct_type = true;
8600					$$->location = @1;
8601				}
8602			| SETOF type_function_name attrs '%' TYPE_P
8603				{
8604					$$ = makeTypeNameFromNameList(lcons(makeString($2), $3));
8605					$$->pct_type = true;
8606					$$->setof = true;
8607					$$->location = @2;
8608				}
8609		;
8610
8611func_arg_with_default:
8612		func_arg
8613				{
8614					$$ = $1;
8615				}
8616		| func_arg DEFAULT a_expr
8617				{
8618					$$ = $1;
8619					$$->defexpr = $3;
8620				}
8621		| func_arg '=' a_expr
8622				{
8623					$$ = $1;
8624					$$->defexpr = $3;
8625				}
8626		;
8627
8628/* Aggregate args can be most things that function args can be */
8629aggr_arg:	func_arg
8630				{
8631					if (!($1->mode == FUNC_PARAM_DEFAULT ||
8632						  $1->mode == FUNC_PARAM_IN ||
8633						  $1->mode == FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC))
8634						ereport(ERROR,
8635								(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
8636								 errmsg("aggregates cannot have output arguments"),
8637								 parser_errposition(@1)));
8638					$$ = $1;
8639				}
8640		;
8641
8642/*
8643 * The SQL standard offers no guidance on how to declare aggregate argument
8644 * lists, since it doesn't have CREATE AGGREGATE etc.  We accept these cases:
8645 *
8646 * (*)									- normal agg with no args
8647 * (aggr_arg,...)						- normal agg with args
8648 * (ORDER BY aggr_arg,...)				- ordered-set agg with no direct args
8649 * (aggr_arg,... ORDER BY aggr_arg,...)	- ordered-set agg with direct args
8650 *
8651 * The zero-argument case is spelled with '*' for consistency with COUNT(*).
8652 *
8653 * An additional restriction is that if the direct-args list ends in a
8654 * VARIADIC item, the ordered-args list must contain exactly one item that
8655 * is also VARIADIC with the same type.  This allows us to collapse the two
8656 * VARIADIC items into one, which is necessary to represent the aggregate in
8657 * pg_proc.  We check this at the grammar stage so that we can return a list
8658 * in which the second VARIADIC item is already discarded, avoiding extra work
8659 * in cases such as DROP AGGREGATE.
8660 *
8661 * The return value of this production is a two-element list, in which the
8662 * first item is a sublist of FunctionParameter nodes (with any duplicate
8663 * VARIADIC item already dropped, as per above) and the second is an Integer
8664 * node, containing -1 if there was no ORDER BY and otherwise the number
8665 * of argument declarations before the ORDER BY.  (If this number is equal
8666 * to the first sublist's length, then we dropped a duplicate VARIADIC item.)
8667 * This representation is passed as-is to CREATE AGGREGATE; for operations
8668 * on existing aggregates, we can just apply extractArgTypes to the first
8669 * sublist.
8670 */
8671aggr_args:	'(' '*' ')'
8672				{
8673					$$ = list_make2(NIL, makeInteger(-1));
8674				}
8675			| '(' aggr_args_list ')'
8676				{
8677					$$ = list_make2($2, makeInteger(-1));
8678				}
8679			| '(' ORDER BY aggr_args_list ')'
8680				{
8681					$$ = list_make2($4, makeInteger(0));
8682				}
8683			| '(' aggr_args_list ORDER BY aggr_args_list ')'
8684				{
8685					/* this is the only case requiring consistency checking */
8686					$$ = makeOrderedSetArgs($2, $5, yyscanner);
8687				}
8688		;
8689
8690aggr_args_list:
8691			aggr_arg								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8692			| aggr_args_list ',' aggr_arg			{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8693		;
8694
8695aggregate_with_argtypes:
8696			func_name aggr_args
8697				{
8698					ObjectWithArgs *n = makeNode(ObjectWithArgs);
8699
8700					n->objname = $1;
8701					n->objargs = extractAggrArgTypes($2);
8702					n->objfuncargs = (List *) linitial($2);
8703					$$ = n;
8704				}
8705		;
8706
8707aggregate_with_argtypes_list:
8708			aggregate_with_argtypes					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8709			| aggregate_with_argtypes_list ',' aggregate_with_argtypes
8710													{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
8711		;
8712
8713opt_createfunc_opt_list:
8714			createfunc_opt_list
8715			| /*EMPTY*/ { $$ = NIL; }
8716	;
8717
8718createfunc_opt_list:
8719			/* Must be at least one to prevent conflict */
8720			createfunc_opt_item						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8721			| createfunc_opt_list createfunc_opt_item { $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
8722	;
8723
8724/*
8725 * Options common to both CREATE FUNCTION and ALTER FUNCTION
8726 */
8727common_func_opt_item:
8728			CALLED ON NULL_P INPUT_P
8729				{
8730					$$ = makeDefElem("strict", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);
8731				}
8732			| RETURNS NULL_P ON NULL_P INPUT_P
8733				{
8734					$$ = makeDefElem("strict", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);
8735				}
8736			| STRICT_P
8737				{
8738					$$ = makeDefElem("strict", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);
8739				}
8740			| IMMUTABLE
8741				{
8742					$$ = makeDefElem("volatility", (Node *) makeString("immutable"), @1);
8743				}
8744			| STABLE
8745				{
8746					$$ = makeDefElem("volatility", (Node *) makeString("stable"), @1);
8747				}
8748			| VOLATILE
8749				{
8750					$$ = makeDefElem("volatility", (Node *) makeString("volatile"), @1);
8751				}
8752			| EXTERNAL SECURITY DEFINER
8753				{
8754					$$ = makeDefElem("security", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);
8755				}
8756			| EXTERNAL SECURITY INVOKER
8757				{
8758					$$ = makeDefElem("security", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);
8759				}
8760			| SECURITY DEFINER
8761				{
8762					$$ = makeDefElem("security", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);
8763				}
8764			| SECURITY INVOKER
8765				{
8766					$$ = makeDefElem("security", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);
8767				}
8768			| LEAKPROOF
8769				{
8770					$$ = makeDefElem("leakproof", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);
8771				}
8772			| NOT LEAKPROOF
8773				{
8774					$$ = makeDefElem("leakproof", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1);
8775				}
8776			| COST NumericOnly
8777				{
8778					$$ = makeDefElem("cost", (Node *) $2, @1);
8779				}
8780			| ROWS NumericOnly
8781				{
8782					$$ = makeDefElem("rows", (Node *) $2, @1);
8783				}
8784			| SUPPORT any_name
8785				{
8786					$$ = makeDefElem("support", (Node *) $2, @1);
8787				}
8788			| FunctionSetResetClause
8789				{
8790					/* we abuse the normal content of a DefElem here */
8791					$$ = makeDefElem("set", (Node *) $1, @1);
8792				}
8793			| PARALLEL ColId
8794				{
8795					$$ = makeDefElem("parallel", (Node *) makeString($2), @1);
8796				}
8797		;
8798
8799createfunc_opt_item:
8800			AS func_as
8801				{
8802					$$ = makeDefElem("as", (Node *) $2, @1);
8803				}
8804			| LANGUAGE NonReservedWord_or_Sconst
8805				{
8806					$$ = makeDefElem("language", (Node *) makeString($2), @1);
8807				}
8808			| TRANSFORM transform_type_list
8809				{
8810					$$ = makeDefElem("transform", (Node *) $2, @1);
8811				}
8812			| WINDOW
8813				{
8814					$$ = makeDefElem("window", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1);
8815				}
8816			| common_func_opt_item
8817				{
8818					$$ = $1;
8819				}
8820		;
8821
8822func_as:	Sconst						{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
8823			| Sconst ',' Sconst
8824				{
8825					$$ = list_make2(makeString($1), makeString($3));
8826				}
8827		;
8828
8829ReturnStmt:	RETURN a_expr
8830				{
8831					ReturnStmt *r = makeNode(ReturnStmt);
8832
8833					r->returnval = (Node *) $2;
8834					$$ = (Node *) r;
8835				}
8836		;
8837
8838opt_routine_body:
8839			ReturnStmt
8840				{
8841					$$ = $1;
8842				}
8843			| BEGIN_P ATOMIC routine_body_stmt_list END_P
8844				{
8845					/*
8846					 * A compound statement is stored as a single-item list
8847					 * containing the list of statements as its member.  That
8848					 * way, the parse analysis code can tell apart an empty
8849					 * body from no body at all.
8850					 */
8851					$$ = (Node *) list_make1($3);
8852				}
8853			| /*EMPTY*/
8854				{
8855					$$ = NULL;
8856				}
8857		;
8858
8859routine_body_stmt_list:
8860			routine_body_stmt_list routine_body_stmt ';'
8861				{
8862					/* As in stmtmulti, discard empty statements */
8863					if ($2 != NULL)
8864						$$ = lappend($1, $2);
8865					else
8866						$$ = $1;
8867				}
8868			| /*EMPTY*/
8869				{
8870					$$ = NIL;
8871				}
8872		;
8873
8874routine_body_stmt:
8875			stmt
8876			| ReturnStmt
8877		;
8878
8879transform_type_list:
8880			FOR TYPE_P Typename { $$ = list_make1($3); }
8881			| transform_type_list ',' FOR TYPE_P Typename { $$ = lappend($1, $5); }
8882		;
8883
8884opt_definition:
8885			WITH definition							{ $$ = $2; }
8886			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
8887		;
8888
8889table_func_column:	param_name func_type
8890				{
8891					FunctionParameter *n = makeNode(FunctionParameter);
8892
8893					n->name = $1;
8894					n->argType = $2;
8895					n->mode = FUNC_PARAM_TABLE;
8896					n->defexpr = NULL;
8897					n->location = @1;
8898					$$ = n;
8899				}
8900		;
8901
8902table_func_column_list:
8903			table_func_column
8904				{
8905					$$ = list_make1($1);
8906				}
8907			| table_func_column_list ',' table_func_column
8908				{
8909					$$ = lappend($1, $3);
8910				}
8911		;
8912
8913/*****************************************************************************
8914 * ALTER FUNCTION / ALTER PROCEDURE / ALTER ROUTINE
8915 *
8916 * RENAME and OWNER subcommands are already provided by the generic
8917 * ALTER infrastructure, here we just specify alterations that can
8918 * only be applied to functions.
8919 *
8920 *****************************************************************************/
8921AlterFunctionStmt:
8922			ALTER FUNCTION function_with_argtypes alterfunc_opt_list opt_restrict
8923				{
8924					AlterFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(AlterFunctionStmt);
8925
8926					n->objtype = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
8927					n->func = $3;
8928					n->actions = $4;
8929					$$ = (Node *) n;
8930				}
8931			| ALTER PROCEDURE function_with_argtypes alterfunc_opt_list opt_restrict
8932				{
8933					AlterFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(AlterFunctionStmt);
8934
8935					n->objtype = OBJECT_PROCEDURE;
8936					n->func = $3;
8937					n->actions = $4;
8938					$$ = (Node *) n;
8939				}
8940			| ALTER ROUTINE function_with_argtypes alterfunc_opt_list opt_restrict
8941				{
8942					AlterFunctionStmt *n = makeNode(AlterFunctionStmt);
8943
8944					n->objtype = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
8945					n->func = $3;
8946					n->actions = $4;
8947					$$ = (Node *) n;
8948				}
8949		;
8950
8951alterfunc_opt_list:
8952			/* At least one option must be specified */
8953			common_func_opt_item					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
8954			| alterfunc_opt_list common_func_opt_item { $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
8955		;
8956
8957/* Ignored, merely for SQL compliance */
8958opt_restrict:
8959			RESTRICT
8960			| /* EMPTY */
8961		;
8962
8963
8964/*****************************************************************************
8965 *
8966 *		QUERY:
8967 *
8968 *		DROP FUNCTION funcname (arg1, arg2, ...) [ RESTRICT | CASCADE ]
8969 *		DROP PROCEDURE procname (arg1, arg2, ...) [ RESTRICT | CASCADE ]
8970 *		DROP ROUTINE routname (arg1, arg2, ...) [ RESTRICT | CASCADE ]
8971 *		DROP AGGREGATE aggname (arg1, ...) [ RESTRICT | CASCADE ]
8972 *		DROP OPERATOR opname (leftoperand_typ, rightoperand_typ) [ RESTRICT | CASCADE ]
8973 *
8974 *****************************************************************************/
8975
8976RemoveFuncStmt:
8977			DROP FUNCTION function_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
8978				{
8979					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
8980
8981					n->removeType = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
8982					n->objects = $3;
8983					n->behavior = $4;
8984					n->missing_ok = false;
8985					n->concurrent = false;
8986					$$ = (Node *) n;
8987				}
8988			| DROP FUNCTION IF_P EXISTS function_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
8989				{
8990					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
8991
8992					n->removeType = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
8993					n->objects = $5;
8994					n->behavior = $6;
8995					n->missing_ok = true;
8996					n->concurrent = false;
8997					$$ = (Node *) n;
8998				}
8999			| DROP PROCEDURE function_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9000				{
9001					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9002
9003					n->removeType = OBJECT_PROCEDURE;
9004					n->objects = $3;
9005					n->behavior = $4;
9006					n->missing_ok = false;
9007					n->concurrent = false;
9008					$$ = (Node *) n;
9009				}
9010			| DROP PROCEDURE IF_P EXISTS function_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9011				{
9012					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9013
9014					n->removeType = OBJECT_PROCEDURE;
9015					n->objects = $5;
9016					n->behavior = $6;
9017					n->missing_ok = true;
9018					n->concurrent = false;
9019					$$ = (Node *) n;
9020				}
9021			| DROP ROUTINE function_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9022				{
9023					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9024
9025					n->removeType = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
9026					n->objects = $3;
9027					n->behavior = $4;
9028					n->missing_ok = false;
9029					n->concurrent = false;
9030					$$ = (Node *) n;
9031				}
9032			| DROP ROUTINE IF_P EXISTS function_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9033				{
9034					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9035
9036					n->removeType = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
9037					n->objects = $5;
9038					n->behavior = $6;
9039					n->missing_ok = true;
9040					n->concurrent = false;
9041					$$ = (Node *) n;
9042				}
9043		;
9044
9045RemoveAggrStmt:
9046			DROP AGGREGATE aggregate_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9047				{
9048					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9049
9050					n->removeType = OBJECT_AGGREGATE;
9051					n->objects = $3;
9052					n->behavior = $4;
9053					n->missing_ok = false;
9054					n->concurrent = false;
9055					$$ = (Node *) n;
9056				}
9057			| DROP AGGREGATE IF_P EXISTS aggregate_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9058				{
9059					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9060
9061					n->removeType = OBJECT_AGGREGATE;
9062					n->objects = $5;
9063					n->behavior = $6;
9064					n->missing_ok = true;
9065					n->concurrent = false;
9066					$$ = (Node *) n;
9067				}
9068		;
9069
9070RemoveOperStmt:
9071			DROP OPERATOR operator_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9072				{
9073					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9074
9075					n->removeType = OBJECT_OPERATOR;
9076					n->objects = $3;
9077					n->behavior = $4;
9078					n->missing_ok = false;
9079					n->concurrent = false;
9080					$$ = (Node *) n;
9081				}
9082			| DROP OPERATOR IF_P EXISTS operator_with_argtypes_list opt_drop_behavior
9083				{
9084					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9085
9086					n->removeType = OBJECT_OPERATOR;
9087					n->objects = $5;
9088					n->behavior = $6;
9089					n->missing_ok = true;
9090					n->concurrent = false;
9091					$$ = (Node *) n;
9092				}
9093		;
9094
9095oper_argtypes:
9096			'(' Typename ')'
9097				{
9098				   ereport(ERROR,
9099						   (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
9100							errmsg("missing argument"),
9101							errhint("Use NONE to denote the missing argument of a unary operator."),
9102							parser_errposition(@3)));
9103				}
9104			| '(' Typename ',' Typename ')'
9105					{ $$ = list_make2($2, $4); }
9106			| '(' NONE ',' Typename ')'					/* left unary */
9107					{ $$ = list_make2(NULL, $4); }
9108			| '(' Typename ',' NONE ')'					/* right unary */
9109					{ $$ = list_make2($2, NULL); }
9110		;
9111
9112any_operator:
9113			all_Op
9114					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
9115			| ColId '.' any_operator
9116					{ $$ = lcons(makeString($1), $3); }
9117		;
9118
9119operator_with_argtypes_list:
9120			operator_with_argtypes					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
9121			| operator_with_argtypes_list ',' operator_with_argtypes
9122													{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
9123		;
9124
9125operator_with_argtypes:
9126			any_operator oper_argtypes
9127				{
9128					ObjectWithArgs *n = makeNode(ObjectWithArgs);
9129
9130					n->objname = $1;
9131					n->objargs = $2;
9132					$$ = n;
9133				}
9134		;
9135
9136/*****************************************************************************
9137 *
9138 *		DO <anonymous code block> [ LANGUAGE language ]
9139 *
9140 * We use a DefElem list for future extensibility, and to allow flexibility
9141 * in the clause order.
9142 *
9143 *****************************************************************************/
9144
9145DoStmt: DO dostmt_opt_list
9146				{
9147					DoStmt *n = makeNode(DoStmt);
9148
9149					n->args = $2;
9150					$$ = (Node *) n;
9151				}
9152		;
9153
9154dostmt_opt_list:
9155			dostmt_opt_item						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
9156			| dostmt_opt_list dostmt_opt_item	{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
9157		;
9158
9159dostmt_opt_item:
9160			Sconst
9161				{
9162					$$ = makeDefElem("as", (Node *) makeString($1), @1);
9163				}
9164			| LANGUAGE NonReservedWord_or_Sconst
9165				{
9166					$$ = makeDefElem("language", (Node *) makeString($2), @1);
9167				}
9168		;
9169
9170/*****************************************************************************
9171 *
9172 *		CREATE CAST / DROP CAST
9173 *
9174 *****************************************************************************/
9175
9176CreateCastStmt: CREATE CAST '(' Typename AS Typename ')'
9177					WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes cast_context
9178				{
9179					CreateCastStmt *n = makeNode(CreateCastStmt);
9180
9181					n->sourcetype = $4;
9182					n->targettype = $6;
9183					n->func = $10;
9184					n->context = (CoercionContext) $11;
9185					n->inout = false;
9186					$$ = (Node *) n;
9187				}
9188			| CREATE CAST '(' Typename AS Typename ')'
9189					WITHOUT FUNCTION cast_context
9190				{
9191					CreateCastStmt *n = makeNode(CreateCastStmt);
9192
9193					n->sourcetype = $4;
9194					n->targettype = $6;
9195					n->func = NULL;
9196					n->context = (CoercionContext) $10;
9197					n->inout = false;
9198					$$ = (Node *) n;
9199				}
9200			| CREATE CAST '(' Typename AS Typename ')'
9201					WITH INOUT cast_context
9202				{
9203					CreateCastStmt *n = makeNode(CreateCastStmt);
9204
9205					n->sourcetype = $4;
9206					n->targettype = $6;
9207					n->func = NULL;
9208					n->context = (CoercionContext) $10;
9209					n->inout = true;
9210					$$ = (Node *) n;
9211				}
9212		;
9213
9214cast_context:  AS IMPLICIT_P					{ $$ = COERCION_IMPLICIT; }
9215		| AS ASSIGNMENT							{ $$ = COERCION_ASSIGNMENT; }
9216		| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = COERCION_EXPLICIT; }
9217		;
9218
9219
9220DropCastStmt: DROP CAST opt_if_exists '(' Typename AS Typename ')' opt_drop_behavior
9221				{
9222					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9223
9224					n->removeType = OBJECT_CAST;
9225					n->objects = list_make1(list_make2($5, $7));
9226					n->behavior = $9;
9227					n->missing_ok = $3;
9228					n->concurrent = false;
9229					$$ = (Node *) n;
9230				}
9231		;
9232
9233opt_if_exists: IF_P EXISTS						{ $$ = true; }
9234		| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
9235		;
9236
9237
9238/*****************************************************************************
9239 *
9240 *		CREATE TRANSFORM / DROP TRANSFORM
9241 *
9242 *****************************************************************************/
9243
9244CreateTransformStmt: CREATE opt_or_replace TRANSFORM FOR Typename LANGUAGE name '(' transform_element_list ')'
9245				{
9246					CreateTransformStmt *n = makeNode(CreateTransformStmt);
9247
9248					n->replace = $2;
9249					n->type_name = $5;
9250					n->lang = $7;
9251					n->fromsql = linitial($9);
9252					n->tosql = lsecond($9);
9253					$$ = (Node *) n;
9254				}
9255		;
9256
9257transform_element_list: FROM SQL_P WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes ',' TO SQL_P WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes
9258				{
9259					$$ = list_make2($5, $11);
9260				}
9261				| TO SQL_P WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes ',' FROM SQL_P WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes
9262				{
9263					$$ = list_make2($11, $5);
9264				}
9265				| FROM SQL_P WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes
9266				{
9267					$$ = list_make2($5, NULL);
9268				}
9269				| TO SQL_P WITH FUNCTION function_with_argtypes
9270				{
9271					$$ = list_make2(NULL, $5);
9272				}
9273		;
9274
9275
9276DropTransformStmt: DROP TRANSFORM opt_if_exists FOR Typename LANGUAGE name opt_drop_behavior
9277				{
9278					DropStmt *n = makeNode(DropStmt);
9279
9280					n->removeType = OBJECT_TRANSFORM;
9281					n->objects = list_make1(list_make2($5, makeString($7)));
9282					n->behavior = $8;
9283					n->missing_ok = $3;
9284					$$ = (Node *) n;
9285				}
9286		;
9287
9288
9289/*****************************************************************************
9290 *
9291 *		QUERY:
9292 *
9293 *		REINDEX [ (options) ] {INDEX | TABLE | SCHEMA} [CONCURRENTLY] <name>
9294 *		REINDEX [ (options) ] {DATABASE | SYSTEM} [CONCURRENTLY] [<name>]
9295 *****************************************************************************/
9296
9297ReindexStmt:
9298			REINDEX opt_reindex_option_list reindex_target_relation opt_concurrently qualified_name
9299				{
9300					ReindexStmt *n = makeNode(ReindexStmt);
9301
9302					n->kind = $3;
9303					n->relation = $5;
9304					n->name = NULL;
9305					n->params = $2;
9306					if ($4)
9307						n->params = lappend(n->params,
9308											makeDefElem("concurrently", NULL, @4));
9309					$$ = (Node *) n;
9310				}
9311			| REINDEX opt_reindex_option_list SCHEMA opt_concurrently name
9312				{
9313					ReindexStmt *n = makeNode(ReindexStmt);
9314
9315					n->kind = REINDEX_OBJECT_SCHEMA;
9316					n->relation = NULL;
9317					n->name = $5;
9318					n->params = $2;
9319					if ($4)
9320						n->params = lappend(n->params,
9321											makeDefElem("concurrently", NULL, @4));
9322					$$ = (Node *) n;
9323				}
9324			| REINDEX opt_reindex_option_list reindex_target_all opt_concurrently opt_single_name
9325				{
9326					ReindexStmt *n = makeNode(ReindexStmt);
9327
9328					n->kind = $3;
9329					n->relation = NULL;
9330					n->name = $5;
9331					n->params = $2;
9332					if ($4)
9333						n->params = lappend(n->params,
9334											makeDefElem("concurrently", NULL, @4));
9335					$$ = (Node *) n;
9336				}
9337		;
9338reindex_target_relation:
9339			INDEX					{ $$ = REINDEX_OBJECT_INDEX; }
9340			| TABLE					{ $$ = REINDEX_OBJECT_TABLE; }
9341		;
9342reindex_target_all:
9343			SYSTEM_P				{ $$ = REINDEX_OBJECT_SYSTEM; }
9344			| DATABASE				{ $$ = REINDEX_OBJECT_DATABASE; }
9345		;
9346opt_reindex_option_list:
9347			'(' utility_option_list ')'				{ $$ = $2; }
9348			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NULL; }
9349		;
9350
9351/*****************************************************************************
9352 *
9353 * ALTER TABLESPACE
9354 *
9355 *****************************************************************************/
9356
9357AlterTblSpcStmt:
9358			ALTER TABLESPACE name SET reloptions
9359				{
9360					AlterTableSpaceOptionsStmt *n =
9361						makeNode(AlterTableSpaceOptionsStmt);
9362
9363					n->tablespacename = $3;
9364					n->options = $5;
9365					n->isReset = false;
9366					$$ = (Node *) n;
9367				}
9368			| ALTER TABLESPACE name RESET reloptions
9369				{
9370					AlterTableSpaceOptionsStmt *n =
9371						makeNode(AlterTableSpaceOptionsStmt);
9372
9373					n->tablespacename = $3;
9374					n->options = $5;
9375					n->isReset = true;
9376					$$ = (Node *) n;
9377				}
9378		;
9379
9380/*****************************************************************************
9381 *
9382 * ALTER THING name RENAME TO newname
9383 *
9384 *****************************************************************************/
9385
9386RenameStmt: ALTER AGGREGATE aggregate_with_argtypes RENAME TO name
9387				{
9388					RenameStmt *n = makeNode(RenameStmt);
9389

9959			| ALTER TYPE_P any_name RENAME ATTRIBUTE name TO name opt_drop_behavior
9960				{

9962

9964					n->relationType = OBJECT_TYPE;
9965					n->relation = makeRangeVarFromAnyName($3, @3, yyscanner);
9966					n->subname = $6;
9967					n->newname = $8;
9968					n->behavior = $9;
9969					n->missing_ok = false;
9970					$$ = (Node *) n;
9971				}
9972		;
9973
9974opt_column: COLUMN
9975			| /*EMPTY*/
9976		;
9977
9978opt_set_data: SET DATA_P							{ $$ = 1; }
9979			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = 0; }
9980		;
9981
9982/*****************************************************************************
9983 *
9984 * ALTER THING name DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
9985 *
9986 *****************************************************************************/
9987
9988AlterObjectDependsStmt:
9989			ALTER FUNCTION function_with_argtypes opt_no DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
9990				{
9991					AlterObjectDependsStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectDependsStmt);
9992
9993					n->objectType = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
9994					n->object = (Node *) $3;
9995					n->extname = makeString($8);
9996					n->remove = $4;
9997					$$ = (Node *) n;
9998				}
9999			| ALTER PROCEDURE function_with_argtypes opt_no DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
10000				{
10001					AlterObjectDependsStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectDependsStmt);
10002
10003					n->objectType = OBJECT_PROCEDURE;
10004					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10005					n->extname = makeString($8);
10006					n->remove = $4;
10007					$$ = (Node *) n;
10008				}
10009			| ALTER ROUTINE function_with_argtypes opt_no DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
10010				{
10011					AlterObjectDependsStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectDependsStmt);
10012
10013					n->objectType = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
10014					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10015					n->extname = makeString($8);
10016					n->remove = $4;
10017					$$ = (Node *) n;
10018				}
10019			| ALTER TRIGGER name ON qualified_name opt_no DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
10020				{
10021					AlterObjectDependsStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectDependsStmt);
10022
10023					n->objectType = OBJECT_TRIGGER;
10024					n->relation = $5;
10025					n->object = (Node *) list_make1(makeString($3));
10026					n->extname = makeString($10);
10027					n->remove = $6;
10028					$$ = (Node *) n;
10029				}
10030			| ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW qualified_name opt_no DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
10031				{
10032					AlterObjectDependsStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectDependsStmt);
10033
10034					n->objectType = OBJECT_MATVIEW;
10035					n->relation = $4;
10036					n->extname = makeString($9);
10037					n->remove = $5;
10038					$$ = (Node *) n;
10039				}
10040			| ALTER INDEX qualified_name opt_no DEPENDS ON EXTENSION name
10041				{
10042					AlterObjectDependsStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectDependsStmt);
10043
10044					n->objectType = OBJECT_INDEX;
10045					n->relation = $3;
10046					n->extname = makeString($8);
10047					n->remove = $4;
10048					$$ = (Node *) n;
10049				}
10050		;
10051
10052opt_no:		NO				{ $$ = true; }
10053			| /* EMPTY */	{ $$ = false;	}
10054		;
10055
10056/*****************************************************************************
10057 *
10058 * ALTER THING name SET SCHEMA name
10059 *
10060 *****************************************************************************/
10061
10062AlterObjectSchemaStmt:
10063			ALTER AGGREGATE aggregate_with_argtypes SET SCHEMA name
10064				{
10065					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10066
10067					n->objectType = OBJECT_AGGREGATE;
10068					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10069					n->newschema = $6;
10070					n->missing_ok = false;
10071					$$ = (Node *) n;
10072				}
10073			| ALTER COLLATION any_name SET SCHEMA name
10074				{
10075					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10076
10077					n->objectType = OBJECT_COLLATION;
10078					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10079					n->newschema = $6;
10080					n->missing_ok = false;
10081					$$ = (Node *) n;
10082				}
10083			| ALTER CONVERSION_P any_name SET SCHEMA name
10084				{
10085					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10086
10087					n->objectType = OBJECT_CONVERSION;
10088					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10089					n->newschema = $6;
10090					n->missing_ok = false;
10091					$$ = (Node *) n;
10092				}
10093			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name SET SCHEMA name
10094				{
10095					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10096
10097					n->objectType = OBJECT_DOMAIN;
10098					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10099					n->newschema = $6;
10100					n->missing_ok = false;
10101					$$ = (Node *) n;
10102				}
10103			| ALTER EXTENSION name SET SCHEMA name
10104				{
10105					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10106
10107					n->objectType = OBJECT_EXTENSION;
10108					n->object = (Node *) makeString($3);
10109					n->newschema = $6;
10110					n->missing_ok = false;
10111					$$ = (Node *) n;
10112				}
10113			| ALTER FUNCTION function_with_argtypes SET SCHEMA name
10114				{
10115					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10116
10117					n->objectType = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
10118					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10119					n->newschema = $6;
10120					n->missing_ok = false;
10121					$$ = (Node *) n;
10122				}
10123			| ALTER OPERATOR operator_with_argtypes SET SCHEMA name
10124				{
10125					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10126
10127					n->objectType = OBJECT_OPERATOR;
10128					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10129					n->newschema = $6;
10130					n->missing_ok = false;
10131					$$ = (Node *) n;
10132				}
10133			| ALTER OPERATOR CLASS any_name USING name SET SCHEMA name
10134				{
10135					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10136
10137					n->objectType = OBJECT_OPCLASS;
10138					n->object = (Node *) lcons(makeString($6), $4);
10139					n->newschema = $9;
10140					n->missing_ok = false;
10141					$$ = (Node *) n;
10142				}
10143			| ALTER OPERATOR FAMILY any_name USING name SET SCHEMA name
10144				{
10145					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10146
10147					n->objectType = OBJECT_OPFAMILY;
10148					n->object = (Node *) lcons(makeString($6), $4);
10149					n->newschema = $9;
10150					n->missing_ok = false;
10151					$$ = (Node *) n;
10152				}
10153			| ALTER PROCEDURE function_with_argtypes SET SCHEMA name
10154				{
10155					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10156
10157					n->objectType = OBJECT_PROCEDURE;
10158					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10159					n->newschema = $6;
10160					n->missing_ok = false;
10161					$$ = (Node *) n;
10162				}
10163			| ALTER ROUTINE function_with_argtypes SET SCHEMA name
10164				{
10165					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10166
10167					n->objectType = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
10168					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10169					n->newschema = $6;
10170					n->missing_ok = false;
10171					$$ = (Node *) n;
10172				}
10173			| ALTER TABLE relation_expr SET SCHEMA name
10174				{
10175					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10176
10177					n->objectType = OBJECT_TABLE;
10178					n->relation = $3;
10179					n->newschema = $6;
10180					n->missing_ok = false;
10181					$$ = (Node *) n;
10182				}
10183			| ALTER TABLE IF_P EXISTS relation_expr SET SCHEMA name
10184				{
10185					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10186
10187					n->objectType = OBJECT_TABLE;
10188					n->relation = $5;
10189					n->newschema = $8;
10190					n->missing_ok = true;
10191					$$ = (Node *) n;
10192				}
10193			| ALTER STATISTICS any_name SET SCHEMA name
10194				{
10195					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10196
10197					n->objectType = OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT;
10198					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10199					n->newschema = $6;
10200					n->missing_ok = false;
10201					$$ = (Node *) n;
10202				}
10203			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH PARSER any_name SET SCHEMA name
10204				{
10205					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10206
10207					n->objectType = OBJECT_TSPARSER;
10208					n->object = (Node *) $5;
10209					n->newschema = $8;
10210					n->missing_ok = false;
10211					$$ = (Node *) n;
10212				}
10213			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH DICTIONARY any_name SET SCHEMA name
10214				{

10216

10218					n->object = (Node *) $5;

10221					$$ = (Node *) n;

10223			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH TEMPLATE any_name SET SCHEMA name
10224				{

10226

10228					n->object = (Node *) $5;

10231					$$ = (Node *) n;
10232				}
10233			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name SET SCHEMA name

10236
10237					n->objectType = OBJECT_TSCONFIGURATION;
10238					n->object = (Node *) $5;
10239					n->newschema = $8;
10240					n->missing_ok = false;
10241					$$ = (Node *) n;
10242				}
10243			| ALTER SEQUENCE qualified_name SET SCHEMA name
10244				{
10245					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10246
10247					n->objectType = OBJECT_SEQUENCE;
10248					n->relation = $3;
10249					n->newschema = $6;
10250					n->missing_ok = false;
10251					$$ = (Node *) n;
10252				}
10253			| ALTER SEQUENCE IF_P EXISTS qualified_name SET SCHEMA name
10254				{
10255					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10256
10257					n->objectType = OBJECT_SEQUENCE;
10258					n->relation = $5;
10259					n->newschema = $8;
10260					n->missing_ok = true;
10261					$$ = (Node *) n;
10262				}
10263			| ALTER VIEW qualified_name SET SCHEMA name
10264				{
10265					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10266
10267					n->objectType = OBJECT_VIEW;
10268					n->relation = $3;
10269					n->newschema = $6;
10270					n->missing_ok = false;
10271					$$ = (Node *) n;
10272				}
10273			| ALTER VIEW IF_P EXISTS qualified_name SET SCHEMA name
10274				{
10275					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10276
10277					n->objectType = OBJECT_VIEW;
10278					n->relation = $5;
10279					n->newschema = $8;
10280					n->missing_ok = true;
10281					$$ = (Node *) n;
10282				}
10283			| ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW qualified_name SET SCHEMA name
10284				{
10285					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10286
10287					n->objectType = OBJECT_MATVIEW;
10288					n->relation = $4;
10289					n->newschema = $7;
10290					n->missing_ok = false;
10291					$$ = (Node *) n;
10292				}
10293			| ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW IF_P EXISTS qualified_name SET SCHEMA name
10294				{
10295					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10296
10297					n->objectType = OBJECT_MATVIEW;
10298					n->relation = $6;
10299					n->newschema = $9;
10300					n->missing_ok = true;
10301					$$ = (Node *) n;
10302				}
10303			| ALTER FOREIGN TABLE relation_expr SET SCHEMA name
10304				{
10305					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10306
10307					n->objectType = OBJECT_FOREIGN_TABLE;
10308					n->relation = $4;
10309					n->newschema = $7;
10310					n->missing_ok = false;
10311					$$ = (Node *) n;
10312				}
10313			| ALTER FOREIGN TABLE IF_P EXISTS relation_expr SET SCHEMA name
10314				{
10315					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10316
10317					n->objectType = OBJECT_FOREIGN_TABLE;
10318					n->relation = $6;
10319					n->newschema = $9;
10320					n->missing_ok = true;
10321					$$ = (Node *) n;
10322				}
10323			| ALTER TYPE_P any_name SET SCHEMA name
10324				{
10325					AlterObjectSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(AlterObjectSchemaStmt);
10326
10327					n->objectType = OBJECT_TYPE;
10328					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10329					n->newschema = $6;
10330					n->missing_ok = false;
10331					$$ = (Node *) n;
10332				}
10333		;
10334
10335/*****************************************************************************
10336 *
10337 * ALTER OPERATOR name SET define
10338 *
10339 *****************************************************************************/
10340
10341AlterOperatorStmt:
10342			ALTER OPERATOR operator_with_argtypes SET '(' operator_def_list ')'
10343				{
10344					AlterOperatorStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOperatorStmt);
10345
10346					n->opername = $3;
10347					n->options = $6;
10348					$$ = (Node *) n;
10349				}
10350		;
10351
10352operator_def_list:	operator_def_elem								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
10353			| operator_def_list ',' operator_def_elem				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
10354		;
10355
10356operator_def_elem: ColLabel '=' NONE
10357						{ $$ = makeDefElem($1, NULL, @1); }
10358				   | ColLabel '=' operator_def_arg
10359						{ $$ = makeDefElem($1, (Node *) $3, @1); }
10360				   | ColLabel
10361						{ $$ = makeDefElem($1, NULL, @1); }
10362		;
10363
10364/* must be similar enough to def_arg to avoid reduce/reduce conflicts */
10365operator_def_arg:
10366			func_type						{ $$ = (Node *) $1; }
10367			| reserved_keyword				{ $$ = (Node *) makeString(pstrdup($1)); }
10368			| qual_all_Op					{ $$ = (Node *) $1; }
10369			| NumericOnly					{ $$ = (Node *) $1; }
10370			| Sconst						{ $$ = (Node *) makeString($1); }
10371		;
10372
10373/*****************************************************************************
10374 *
10375 * ALTER TYPE name SET define
10376 *
10377 * We repurpose ALTER OPERATOR's version of "definition" here
10378 *
10379 *****************************************************************************/
10380
10381AlterTypeStmt:
10382			ALTER TYPE_P any_name SET '(' operator_def_list ')'
10383				{
10384					AlterTypeStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTypeStmt);
10385
10386					n->typeName = $3;
10387					n->options = $6;
10388					$$ = (Node *) n;
10389				}
10390		;
10391
10392/*****************************************************************************
10393 *
10394 * ALTER THING name OWNER TO newname
10395 *
10396 *****************************************************************************/
10397
10398AlterOwnerStmt: ALTER AGGREGATE aggregate_with_argtypes OWNER TO RoleSpec
10399				{
10400					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10401
10402					n->objectType = OBJECT_AGGREGATE;
10403					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10404					n->newowner = $6;
10405					$$ = (Node *) n;
10406				}
10407			| ALTER COLLATION any_name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10408				{
10409					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10410
10411					n->objectType = OBJECT_COLLATION;
10412					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10413					n->newowner = $6;
10414					$$ = (Node *) n;
10415				}
10416			| ALTER CONVERSION_P any_name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10417				{
10418					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10419
10420					n->objectType = OBJECT_CONVERSION;
10421					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10422					n->newowner = $6;
10423					$$ = (Node *) n;
10424				}
10425			| ALTER DATABASE name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10426				{
10427					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10428
10429					n->objectType = OBJECT_DATABASE;
10430					n->object = (Node *) makeString($3);
10431					n->newowner = $6;
10432					$$ = (Node *) n;
10433				}
10434			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10435				{
10436					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10437
10438					n->objectType = OBJECT_DOMAIN;
10439					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10440					n->newowner = $6;
10441					$$ = (Node *) n;
10442				}
10443			| ALTER FUNCTION function_with_argtypes OWNER TO RoleSpec
10444				{
10445					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10446
10447					n->objectType = OBJECT_FUNCTION;
10448					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10449					n->newowner = $6;
10450					$$ = (Node *) n;
10451				}
10452			| ALTER opt_procedural LANGUAGE name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10453				{
10454					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10455
10456					n->objectType = OBJECT_LANGUAGE;
10457					n->object = (Node *) makeString($4);
10458					n->newowner = $7;
10459					$$ = (Node *) n;
10460				}
10461			| ALTER LARGE_P OBJECT_P NumericOnly OWNER TO RoleSpec
10462				{
10463					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10464
10465					n->objectType = OBJECT_LARGEOBJECT;
10466					n->object = (Node *) $4;
10467					n->newowner = $7;
10468					$$ = (Node *) n;
10469				}
10470			| ALTER OPERATOR operator_with_argtypes OWNER TO RoleSpec
10471				{
10472					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10473
10474					n->objectType = OBJECT_OPERATOR;
10475					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10476					n->newowner = $6;
10477					$$ = (Node *) n;
10478				}
10479			| ALTER OPERATOR CLASS any_name USING name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10480				{
10481					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10482
10483					n->objectType = OBJECT_OPCLASS;
10484					n->object = (Node *) lcons(makeString($6), $4);
10485					n->newowner = $9;
10486					$$ = (Node *) n;
10487				}
10488			| ALTER OPERATOR FAMILY any_name USING name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10489				{
10490					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10491
10492					n->objectType = OBJECT_OPFAMILY;
10493					n->object = (Node *) lcons(makeString($6), $4);
10494					n->newowner = $9;
10495					$$ = (Node *) n;
10496				}
10497			| ALTER PROCEDURE function_with_argtypes OWNER TO RoleSpec
10498				{
10499					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10500
10501					n->objectType = OBJECT_PROCEDURE;
10502					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10503					n->newowner = $6;
10504					$$ = (Node *) n;
10505				}
10506			| ALTER ROUTINE function_with_argtypes OWNER TO RoleSpec
10507				{
10508					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10509
10510					n->objectType = OBJECT_ROUTINE;
10511					n->object = (Node *) $3;
10512					n->newowner = $6;
10513					$$ = (Node *) n;
10514				}
10515			| ALTER SCHEMA name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10516				{
10517					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10518

10521					n->newowner = $6;

10523				}

10525				{

10527

10529					n->object = (Node *) $3;

10532				}

10536
10537					n->objectType = OBJECT_TABLESPACE;
10538					n->object = (Node *) makeString($3);
10539					n->newowner = $6;
10540					$$ = (Node *) n;
10541				}
10542			| ALTER STATISTICS any_name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10543				{

10545
10546					n->objectType = OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT;

10549					$$ = (Node *) n;
10550				}
10551			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH DICTIONARY any_name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10552				{

10554
10555					n->objectType = OBJECT_TSDICTIONARY;
10556					n->object = (Node *) $5;
10557					n->newowner = $8;
10558					$$ = (Node *) n;
10559				}
10560			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name OWNER TO RoleSpec

10567					$$ = (Node *) n;
10568				}

10572

10574					n->object = (Node *) makeString($5);
10575					n->newowner = $8;
10576					$$ = (Node *) n;
10577				}
10578			| ALTER SERVER name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10579				{
10580					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10581
10582					n->objectType = OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER;
10583					n->object = (Node *) makeString($3);
10584					n->newowner = $6;
10585					$$ = (Node *) n;
10586				}
10587			| ALTER EVENT TRIGGER name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10588				{
10589					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10590
10591					n->objectType = OBJECT_EVENT_TRIGGER;
10592					n->object = (Node *) makeString($4);
10593					n->newowner = $7;
10594					$$ = (Node *) n;
10595				}
10596			| ALTER PUBLICATION name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10597				{
10598					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10599
10600					n->objectType = OBJECT_PUBLICATION;
10601					n->object = (Node *) makeString($3);
10602					n->newowner = $6;
10603					$$ = (Node *) n;
10604				}
10605			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name OWNER TO RoleSpec
10606				{
10607					AlterOwnerStmt *n = makeNode(AlterOwnerStmt);
10608
10609					n->objectType = OBJECT_SUBSCRIPTION;
10610					n->object = (Node *) makeString($3);
10611					n->newowner = $6;
10612					$$ = (Node *) n;
10613				}
10614		;
10615
10616
10617/*****************************************************************************
10618 *
10619 * CREATE PUBLICATION name [WITH options]
10620 *
10621 * CREATE PUBLICATION FOR ALL TABLES [WITH options]
10622 *
10623 * CREATE PUBLICATION FOR pub_obj [, ...] [WITH options]
10624 *
10625 * pub_obj is one of:
10626 *
10627 *		TABLE table [, ...]
10628 *		TABLES IN SCHEMA schema [, ...]
10629 *
10630 *****************************************************************************/
10631
10632CreatePublicationStmt:
10633			CREATE PUBLICATION name opt_definition
10634				{
10635					CreatePublicationStmt *n = makeNode(CreatePublicationStmt);
10636
10637					n->pubname = $3;
10638					n->options = $4;
10639					$$ = (Node *) n;
10640				}
10641			| CREATE PUBLICATION name FOR ALL TABLES opt_definition
10642				{
10643					CreatePublicationStmt *n = makeNode(CreatePublicationStmt);
10644
10645					n->pubname = $3;
10646					n->options = $7;
10647					n->for_all_tables = true;
10648					$$ = (Node *) n;
10649				}
10650			| CREATE PUBLICATION name FOR pub_obj_list opt_definition
10651				{
10652					CreatePublicationStmt *n = makeNode(CreatePublicationStmt);
10653
10654					n->pubname = $3;
10655					n->options = $6;
10656					n->pubobjects = (List *) $5;
10657					preprocess_pubobj_list(n->pubobjects, yyscanner);
10658					$$ = (Node *) n;
10659				}
10660		;
10661
10662/*
10663 * FOR TABLE and FOR TABLES IN SCHEMA specifications
10664 *
10665 * This rule parses publication objects with and without keyword prefixes.
10666 *
10667 * The actual type of the object without keyword prefix depends on the previous
10668 * one with keyword prefix. It will be preprocessed in preprocess_pubobj_list().
10669 *
10670 * For the object without keyword prefix, we cannot just use relation_expr here,
10671 * because some extended expressions in relation_expr cannot be used as a
10672 * schemaname and we cannot differentiate it. So, we extract the rules from
10673 * relation_expr here.
10674 */
10675PublicationObjSpec:
10676			TABLE relation_expr opt_column_list OptWhereClause
10677				{
10678					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10679					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLE;
10680					$$->pubtable = makeNode(PublicationTable);
10681					$$->pubtable->relation = $2;
10682					$$->pubtable->columns = $3;
10683					$$->pubtable->whereClause = $4;
10684				}
10685			| TABLES IN_P SCHEMA ColId
10686				{
10687					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10688					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLES_IN_SCHEMA;
10689					$$->name = $4;
10690					$$->location = @4;
10691				}
10692			| TABLES IN_P SCHEMA CURRENT_SCHEMA
10693				{
10694					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10695					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLES_IN_CUR_SCHEMA;
10696					$$->location = @4;
10697				}
10698			| ColId opt_column_list OptWhereClause
10699				{
10700					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10701					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION;
10702					/*
10703					 * If either a row filter or column list is specified, create
10704					 * a PublicationTable object.
10705					 */
10706					if ($2 || $3)
10707					{
10708						/*
10709						 * The OptWhereClause must be stored here but it is
10710						 * valid only for tables. For non-table objects, an
10711						 * error will be thrown later via
10712						 * preprocess_pubobj_list().
10713						 */
10714						$$->pubtable = makeNode(PublicationTable);
10715						$$->pubtable->relation = makeRangeVar(NULL, $1, @1);
10716						$$->pubtable->columns = $2;
10717						$$->pubtable->whereClause = $3;
10718					}
10719					else
10720					{
10721						$$->name = $1;
10722					}
10723					$$->location = @1;
10724				}
10725			| ColId indirection opt_column_list OptWhereClause
10726				{
10727					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10728					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION;
10729					$$->pubtable = makeNode(PublicationTable);
10730					$$->pubtable->relation = makeRangeVarFromQualifiedName($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);
10731					$$->pubtable->columns = $3;
10732					$$->pubtable->whereClause = $4;
10733					$$->location = @1;
10734				}
10735			/* grammar like tablename * , ONLY tablename, ONLY ( tablename ) */
10736			| extended_relation_expr opt_column_list OptWhereClause
10737				{
10738					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10739					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION;
10740					$$->pubtable = makeNode(PublicationTable);
10741					$$->pubtable->relation = $1;
10742					$$->pubtable->columns = $2;
10743					$$->pubtable->whereClause = $3;
10744				}
10745			| CURRENT_SCHEMA
10746				{
10747					$$ = makeNode(PublicationObjSpec);
10748					$$->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION;
10749					$$->location = @1;
10750				}
10751				;
10752
10753pub_obj_list:	PublicationObjSpec
10754					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
10755			| pub_obj_list ',' PublicationObjSpec
10756					{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
10757	;
10758
10759/*****************************************************************************
10760 *
10761 * ALTER PUBLICATION name SET ( options )
10762 *
10763 * ALTER PUBLICATION name ADD pub_obj [, ...]
10764 *
10765 * ALTER PUBLICATION name DROP pub_obj [, ...]
10766 *
10767 * ALTER PUBLICATION name SET pub_obj [, ...]
10768 *
10769 * pub_obj is one of:
10770 *
10771 *		TABLE table_name [, ...]
10772 *		TABLES IN SCHEMA schema_name [, ...]
10773 *
10774 *****************************************************************************/
10775
10776AlterPublicationStmt:
10777			ALTER PUBLICATION name SET definition
10778				{
10779					AlterPublicationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterPublicationStmt);
10780
10781					n->pubname = $3;
10782					n->options = $5;
10783					$$ = (Node *) n;
10784				}
10785			| ALTER PUBLICATION name ADD_P pub_obj_list
10786				{
10787					AlterPublicationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterPublicationStmt);
10788
10789					n->pubname = $3;
10790					n->pubobjects = $5;
10791					preprocess_pubobj_list(n->pubobjects, yyscanner);
10792					n->action = AP_AddObjects;
10793					$$ = (Node *) n;
10794				}
10795			| ALTER PUBLICATION name SET pub_obj_list
10796				{
10797					AlterPublicationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterPublicationStmt);
10798
10799					n->pubname = $3;
10800					n->pubobjects = $5;
10801					preprocess_pubobj_list(n->pubobjects, yyscanner);
10802					n->action = AP_SetObjects;
10803					$$ = (Node *) n;
10804				}
10805			| ALTER PUBLICATION name DROP pub_obj_list
10806				{
10807					AlterPublicationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterPublicationStmt);
10808
10809					n->pubname = $3;
10810					n->pubobjects = $5;
10811					preprocess_pubobj_list(n->pubobjects, yyscanner);
10812					n->action = AP_DropObjects;
10813					$$ = (Node *) n;
10814				}
10815		;
10816
10817/*****************************************************************************
10818 *
10819 * CREATE SUBSCRIPTION name ...
10820 *
10821 *****************************************************************************/
10822
10823CreateSubscriptionStmt:
10824			CREATE SUBSCRIPTION name CONNECTION Sconst PUBLICATION name_list opt_definition
10825				{
10826					CreateSubscriptionStmt *n =
10827						makeNode(CreateSubscriptionStmt);
10828					n->subname = $3;
10829					n->conninfo = $5;
10830					n->publication = $7;
10831					n->options = $8;
10832					$$ = (Node *) n;
10833				}
10834		;
10835
10836/*****************************************************************************
10837 *
10838 * ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name ...
10839 *
10840 *****************************************************************************/
10841
10842AlterSubscriptionStmt:
10843			ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name SET definition
10844				{
10845					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10846						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10847
10848					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_OPTIONS;
10849					n->subname = $3;
10850					n->options = $5;
10851					$$ = (Node *) n;
10852				}
10853			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name CONNECTION Sconst
10854				{
10855					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10856						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10857
10858					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_CONNECTION;
10859					n->subname = $3;
10860					n->conninfo = $5;
10861					$$ = (Node *) n;
10862				}
10863			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name REFRESH PUBLICATION opt_definition
10864				{
10865					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10866						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10867
10868					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_REFRESH;
10869					n->subname = $3;
10870					n->options = $6;
10871					$$ = (Node *) n;
10872				}
10873			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name ADD_P PUBLICATION name_list opt_definition
10874				{
10875					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10876						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10877
10878					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_ADD_PUBLICATION;
10879					n->subname = $3;
10880					n->publication = $6;
10881					n->options = $7;
10882					$$ = (Node *) n;
10883				}
10884			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name DROP PUBLICATION name_list opt_definition
10885				{
10886					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10887						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10888
10889					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_DROP_PUBLICATION;
10890					n->subname = $3;
10891					n->publication = $6;
10892					n->options = $7;
10893					$$ = (Node *) n;
10894				}
10895			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name SET PUBLICATION name_list opt_definition
10896				{
10897					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10898						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10899
10900					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_SET_PUBLICATION;
10901					n->subname = $3;
10902					n->publication = $6;
10903					n->options = $7;
10904					$$ = (Node *) n;
10905				}
10906			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name ENABLE_P
10907				{
10908					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10909						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10910
10911					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_ENABLED;
10912					n->subname = $3;
10913					n->options = list_make1(makeDefElem("enabled",
10914											(Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1));
10915					$$ = (Node *) n;
10916				}
10917			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name DISABLE_P
10918				{
10919					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10920						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10921
10922					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_ENABLED;
10923					n->subname = $3;
10924					n->options = list_make1(makeDefElem("enabled",
10925											(Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1));
10926					$$ = (Node *) n;
10927				}
10928			| ALTER SUBSCRIPTION name SKIP definition
10929				{
10930					AlterSubscriptionStmt *n =
10931						makeNode(AlterSubscriptionStmt);
10932
10933					n->kind = ALTER_SUBSCRIPTION_SKIP;
10934					n->subname = $3;
10935					n->options = $5;
10936					$$ = (Node *) n;
10937				}
10938		;
10939
10940/*****************************************************************************
10941 *
10942 * DROP SUBSCRIPTION [ IF EXISTS ] name
10943 *
10944 *****************************************************************************/
10945
10946DropSubscriptionStmt: DROP SUBSCRIPTION name opt_drop_behavior
10947				{
10948					DropSubscriptionStmt *n = makeNode(DropSubscriptionStmt);
10949
10950					n->subname = $3;
10951					n->missing_ok = false;
10952					n->behavior = $4;
10953					$$ = (Node *) n;
10954				}
10955				|  DROP SUBSCRIPTION IF_P EXISTS name opt_drop_behavior
10956				{
10957					DropSubscriptionStmt *n = makeNode(DropSubscriptionStmt);
10958
10959					n->subname = $5;
10960					n->missing_ok = true;
10961					n->behavior = $6;
10962					$$ = (Node *) n;
10963				}
10964		;
10965
10966/*****************************************************************************
10967 *
10968 *		QUERY:	Define Rewrite Rule
10969 *
10970 *****************************************************************************/
10971
10972RuleStmt:	CREATE opt_or_replace RULE name AS
10973			ON event TO qualified_name where_clause
10974			DO opt_instead RuleActionList
10975				{
10976					RuleStmt   *n = makeNode(RuleStmt);
10977
10978					n->replace = $2;
10979					n->relation = $9;
10980					n->rulename = $4;
10981					n->whereClause = $10;
10982					n->event = $7;
10983					n->instead = $12;
10984					n->actions = $13;
10985					$$ = (Node *) n;
10986				}
10987		;
10988
10989RuleActionList:
10990			NOTHING									{ $$ = NIL; }
10991			| RuleActionStmt						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
10992			| '(' RuleActionMulti ')'				{ $$ = $2; }
10993		;
10994
10995/* the thrashing around here is to discard "empty" statements... */
10996RuleActionMulti:
10997			RuleActionMulti ';' RuleActionStmtOrEmpty
10998				{ if ($3 != NULL)
10999					$$ = lappend($1, $3);
11000				  else
11001					$$ = $1;
11002				}
11003			| RuleActionStmtOrEmpty
11004				{ if ($1 != NULL)
11005					$$ = list_make1($1);
11006				  else
11007					$$ = NIL;
11008				}
11009		;
11010
11011RuleActionStmt:
11012			SelectStmt
11013			| InsertStmt
11014			| UpdateStmt
11015			| DeleteStmt
11016			| NotifyStmt
11017		;
11018
11019RuleActionStmtOrEmpty:
11020			RuleActionStmt							{ $$ = $1; }
11021			|	/*EMPTY*/							{ $$ = NULL; }
11022		;
11023
11024event:		SELECT									{ $$ = CMD_SELECT; }
11025			| UPDATE								{ $$ = CMD_UPDATE; }
11026			| DELETE_P								{ $$ = CMD_DELETE; }
11027			| INSERT								{ $$ = CMD_INSERT; }
11028		 ;
11029
11030opt_instead:
11031			INSTEAD									{ $$ = true; }
11032			| ALSO									{ $$ = false; }
11033			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
11034		;
11035
11036
11037/*****************************************************************************
11038 *
11039 *		QUERY:
11040 *				NOTIFY <identifier> can appear both in rule bodies and
11041 *				as a query-level command
11042 *
11043 *****************************************************************************/
11044
11045NotifyStmt: NOTIFY ColId notify_payload
11046				{
11047					NotifyStmt *n = makeNode(NotifyStmt);
11048
11049					n->conditionname = $2;
11050					n->payload = $3;
11051					$$ = (Node *) n;
11052				}
11053		;
11054
11055notify_payload:
11056			',' Sconst							{ $$ = $2; }
11057			| /*EMPTY*/							{ $$ = NULL; }
11058		;
11059
11060ListenStmt: LISTEN ColId
11061				{
11062					ListenStmt *n = makeNode(ListenStmt);
11063
11064					n->conditionname = $2;
11065					$$ = (Node *) n;
11066				}
11067		;
11068
11069UnlistenStmt:
11070			UNLISTEN ColId
11071				{
11072					UnlistenStmt *n = makeNode(UnlistenStmt);
11073
11074					n->conditionname = $2;
11075					$$ = (Node *) n;
11076				}
11077			| UNLISTEN '*'
11078				{
11079					UnlistenStmt *n = makeNode(UnlistenStmt);
11080
11081					n->conditionname = NULL;
11082					$$ = (Node *) n;
11083				}
11084		;
11085
11086
11087/*****************************************************************************
11088 *
11089 *		Transactions:
11090 *
11091 *		BEGIN / COMMIT / ROLLBACK
11092 *		(also older versions END / ABORT)
11093 *
11094 *****************************************************************************/
11095
11096TransactionStmt:
11097			ABORT_P opt_transaction opt_transaction_chain
11098				{
11099					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11100
11101					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_ROLLBACK;
11102					n->options = NIL;
11103					n->chain = $3;
11104					n->location = -1;
11105					$$ = (Node *) n;
11106				}
11107			| START TRANSACTION transaction_mode_list_or_empty
11108				{
11109					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11110
11111					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_START;
11112					n->options = $3;
11113					n->location = -1;
11114					$$ = (Node *) n;
11115				}
11116			| COMMIT opt_transaction opt_transaction_chain
11117				{
11118					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11119
11120					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_COMMIT;
11121					n->options = NIL;
11122					n->chain = $3;
11123					n->location = -1;
11124					$$ = (Node *) n;
11125				}
11126			| ROLLBACK opt_transaction opt_transaction_chain
11127				{
11128					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11129
11130					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_ROLLBACK;
11131					n->options = NIL;
11132					n->chain = $3;
11133					n->location = -1;
11134					$$ = (Node *) n;
11135				}
11136			| SAVEPOINT ColId
11137				{
11138					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11139
11140					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_SAVEPOINT;
11141					n->savepoint_name = $2;
11142					n->location = @2;
11143					$$ = (Node *) n;
11144				}
11145			| RELEASE SAVEPOINT ColId
11146				{
11147					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11148
11149					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_RELEASE;
11150					n->savepoint_name = $3;
11151					n->location = @3;
11152					$$ = (Node *) n;
11153				}
11154			| RELEASE ColId
11155				{
11156					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11157
11158					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_RELEASE;
11159					n->savepoint_name = $2;
11160					n->location = @2;
11161					$$ = (Node *) n;
11162				}
11163			| ROLLBACK opt_transaction TO SAVEPOINT ColId
11164				{
11165					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11166
11167					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_ROLLBACK_TO;
11168					n->savepoint_name = $5;
11169					n->location = @5;
11170					$$ = (Node *) n;
11171				}
11172			| ROLLBACK opt_transaction TO ColId
11173				{
11174					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11175
11176					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_ROLLBACK_TO;
11177					n->savepoint_name = $4;
11178					n->location = @4;
11179					$$ = (Node *) n;
11180				}
11181			| PREPARE TRANSACTION Sconst
11182				{
11183					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11184
11185					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_PREPARE;
11186					n->gid = $3;
11187					n->location = @3;
11188					$$ = (Node *) n;
11189				}
11190			| COMMIT PREPARED Sconst
11191				{
11192					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11193
11194					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_COMMIT_PREPARED;
11195					n->gid = $3;
11196					n->location = @3;
11197					$$ = (Node *) n;
11198				}
11199			| ROLLBACK PREPARED Sconst
11200				{
11201					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11202
11203					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_ROLLBACK_PREPARED;
11204					n->gid = $3;
11205					n->location = @3;
11206					$$ = (Node *) n;
11207				}
11208		;
11209
11210TransactionStmtLegacy:
11211			BEGIN_P opt_transaction transaction_mode_list_or_empty
11212				{
11213					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11214
11215					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_BEGIN;
11216					n->options = $3;
11217					n->location = -1;
11218					$$ = (Node *) n;
11219				}
11220			| END_P opt_transaction opt_transaction_chain
11221				{
11222					TransactionStmt *n = makeNode(TransactionStmt);
11223
11224					n->kind = TRANS_STMT_COMMIT;
11225					n->options = NIL;
11226					n->chain = $3;
11227					n->location = -1;
11228					$$ = (Node *) n;
11229				}
11230		;
11231
11232opt_transaction:	WORK
11233			| TRANSACTION
11234			| /*EMPTY*/
11235		;
11236
11237transaction_mode_item:
11238			ISOLATION LEVEL iso_level
11239					{ $$ = makeDefElem("transaction_isolation",
11240									   makeStringConst($3, @3), @1); }
11241			| READ ONLY
11242					{ $$ = makeDefElem("transaction_read_only",
11243									   makeIntConst(true, @1), @1); }
11244			| READ WRITE
11245					{ $$ = makeDefElem("transaction_read_only",
11246									   makeIntConst(false, @1), @1); }
11247			| DEFERRABLE
11248					{ $$ = makeDefElem("transaction_deferrable",
11249									   makeIntConst(true, @1), @1); }
11250			| NOT DEFERRABLE
11251					{ $$ = makeDefElem("transaction_deferrable",
11252									   makeIntConst(false, @1), @1); }
11253		;
11254
11255/* Syntax with commas is SQL-spec, without commas is Postgres historical */
11256transaction_mode_list:
11257			transaction_mode_item
11258					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
11259			| transaction_mode_list ',' transaction_mode_item
11260					{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
11261			| transaction_mode_list transaction_mode_item
11262					{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
11263		;
11264
11265transaction_mode_list_or_empty:
11266			transaction_mode_list
11267			| /* EMPTY */
11268					{ $$ = NIL; }
11269		;
11270
11271opt_transaction_chain:
11272			AND CHAIN		{ $$ = true; }
11273			| AND NO CHAIN	{ $$ = false; }
11274			| /* EMPTY */	{ $$ = false; }
11275		;
11276
11277
11278/*****************************************************************************
11279 *
11280 *	QUERY:
11281 *		CREATE [ OR REPLACE ] [ TEMP ] VIEW <viewname> '('target-list ')'
11282 *			AS <query> [ WITH [ CASCADED | LOCAL ] CHECK OPTION ]
11283 *
11284 *****************************************************************************/
11285
11286ViewStmt: CREATE OptTemp VIEW qualified_name opt_column_list opt_reloptions
11287				AS SelectStmt opt_check_option
11288				{
11289					ViewStmt   *n = makeNode(ViewStmt);
11290
11291					n->view = $4;
11292					n->view->relpersistence = $2;
11293					n->aliases = $5;
11294					n->query = $8;
11295					n->replace = false;
11296					n->options = $6;
11297					n->withCheckOption = $9;
11298					$$ = (Node *) n;
11299				}
11300		| CREATE OR REPLACE OptTemp VIEW qualified_name opt_column_list opt_reloptions
11301				AS SelectStmt opt_check_option
11302				{
11303					ViewStmt   *n = makeNode(ViewStmt);
11304
11305					n->view = $6;
11306					n->view->relpersistence = $4;
11307					n->aliases = $7;
11308					n->query = $10;
11309					n->replace = true;
11310					n->options = $8;
11311					n->withCheckOption = $11;
11312					$$ = (Node *) n;
11313				}
11314		| CREATE OptTemp RECURSIVE VIEW qualified_name '(' columnList ')' opt_reloptions
11315				AS SelectStmt opt_check_option
11316				{
11317					ViewStmt   *n = makeNode(ViewStmt);
11318
11319					n->view = $5;
11320					n->view->relpersistence = $2;
11321					n->aliases = $7;
11322					n->query = makeRecursiveViewSelect(n->view->relname, n->aliases, $11);
11323					n->replace = false;
11324					n->options = $9;
11325					n->withCheckOption = $12;
11326					if (n->withCheckOption != NO_CHECK_OPTION)
11327						ereport(ERROR,
11328								(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
11329								 errmsg("WITH CHECK OPTION not supported on recursive views"),
11330								 parser_errposition(@12)));
11331					$$ = (Node *) n;
11332				}
11333		| CREATE OR REPLACE OptTemp RECURSIVE VIEW qualified_name '(' columnList ')' opt_reloptions
11334				AS SelectStmt opt_check_option
11335				{
11336					ViewStmt   *n = makeNode(ViewStmt);
11337
11338					n->view = $7;
11339					n->view->relpersistence = $4;
11340					n->aliases = $9;
11341					n->query = makeRecursiveViewSelect(n->view->relname, n->aliases, $13);
11342					n->replace = true;
11343					n->options = $11;
11344					n->withCheckOption = $14;
11345					if (n->withCheckOption != NO_CHECK_OPTION)
11346						ereport(ERROR,
11347								(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
11348								 errmsg("WITH CHECK OPTION not supported on recursive views"),
11349								 parser_errposition(@14)));
11350					$$ = (Node *) n;
11351				}
11352		;
11353
11354opt_check_option:
11355		WITH CHECK OPTION				{ $$ = CASCADED_CHECK_OPTION; }
11356		| WITH CASCADED CHECK OPTION	{ $$ = CASCADED_CHECK_OPTION; }
11357		| WITH LOCAL CHECK OPTION		{ $$ = LOCAL_CHECK_OPTION; }
11358		| /* EMPTY */					{ $$ = NO_CHECK_OPTION; }
11359		;
11360
11361/*****************************************************************************
11362 *
11363 *		QUERY:
11364 *				LOAD "filename"
11365 *
11366 *****************************************************************************/
11367
11368LoadStmt:	LOAD file_name
11369				{
11370					LoadStmt   *n = makeNode(LoadStmt);
11371
11372					n->filename = $2;
11373					$$ = (Node *) n;
11374				}
11375		;
11376
11377
11378/*****************************************************************************
11379 *
11380 *		CREATE DATABASE
11381 *
11382 *****************************************************************************/
11383
11384CreatedbStmt:
11385			CREATE DATABASE name opt_with createdb_opt_list
11386				{
11387					CreatedbStmt *n = makeNode(CreatedbStmt);
11388
11389					n->dbname = $3;
11390					n->options = $5;
11391					$$ = (Node *) n;
11392				}
11393		;
11394
11395createdb_opt_list:
11396			createdb_opt_items						{ $$ = $1; }
11397			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NIL; }
11398		;
11399
11400createdb_opt_items:
11401			createdb_opt_item						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
11402			| createdb_opt_items createdb_opt_item	{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
11403		;
11404
11405createdb_opt_item:
11406			createdb_opt_name opt_equal NumericOnly
11407				{
11408					$$ = makeDefElem($1, $3, @1);
11409				}
11410			| createdb_opt_name opt_equal opt_boolean_or_string
11411				{
11412					$$ = makeDefElem($1, (Node *) makeString($3), @1);
11413				}
11414			| createdb_opt_name opt_equal DEFAULT
11415				{
11416					$$ = makeDefElem($1, NULL, @1);
11417				}
11418		;
11419
11420/*
11421 * Ideally we'd use ColId here, but that causes shift/reduce conflicts against
11422 * the ALTER DATABASE SET/RESET syntaxes.  Instead call out specific keywords
11423 * we need, and allow IDENT so that database option names don't have to be
11424 * parser keywords unless they are already keywords for other reasons.
11425 *
11426 * XXX this coding technique is fragile since if someone makes a formerly
11427 * non-keyword option name into a keyword and forgets to add it here, the
11428 * option will silently break.  Best defense is to provide a regression test
11429 * exercising every such option, at least at the syntax level.
11430 */
11431createdb_opt_name:
11432			IDENT							{ $$ = $1; }
11433			| CONNECTION LIMIT				{ $$ = pstrdup("connection_limit"); }
11434			| ENCODING						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
11435			| LOCATION						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
11436			| OWNER							{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
11437			| TABLESPACE					{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
11438			| TEMPLATE						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
11439		;
11440
11441/*
11442 *	Though the equals sign doesn't match other WITH options, pg_dump uses
11443 *	equals for backward compatibility, and it doesn't seem worth removing it.
11444 */
11445opt_equal:	'='
11446			| /*EMPTY*/
11447		;
11448
11449
11450/*****************************************************************************
11451 *
11452 *		ALTER DATABASE
11453 *
11454 *****************************************************************************/
11455
11456AlterDatabaseStmt:
11457			ALTER DATABASE name WITH createdb_opt_list
11458				 {
11459					AlterDatabaseStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDatabaseStmt);
11460
11461					n->dbname = $3;
11462					n->options = $5;
11463					$$ = (Node *) n;
11464				 }
11465			| ALTER DATABASE name createdb_opt_list
11466				 {
11467					AlterDatabaseStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDatabaseStmt);
11468
11469					n->dbname = $3;
11470					n->options = $4;
11471					$$ = (Node *) n;
11472				 }
11473			| ALTER DATABASE name SET TABLESPACE name
11474				 {
11475					AlterDatabaseStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDatabaseStmt);
11476
11477					n->dbname = $3;
11478					n->options = list_make1(makeDefElem("tablespace",
11479														(Node *) makeString($6), @6));
11480					$$ = (Node *) n;
11481				 }
11482			| ALTER DATABASE name REFRESH COLLATION VERSION_P
11483				 {
11484					AlterDatabaseRefreshCollStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDatabaseRefreshCollStmt);
11485
11486					n->dbname = $3;
11487					$$ = (Node *) n;
11488				 }
11489		;
11490
