11491AlterDatabaseSetStmt:
11492			ALTER DATABASE name SetResetClause
11493				{
11494					AlterDatabaseSetStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDatabaseSetStmt);
11495
11496					n->dbname = $3;
11497					n->setstmt = $4;
11498					$$ = (Node *) n;
11499				}
11500		;
11501
11502
11503/*****************************************************************************
11504 *
11505 *		DROP DATABASE [ IF EXISTS ] dbname [ [ WITH ] ( options ) ]
11506 *
11507 * This is implicitly CASCADE, no need for drop behavior
11508 *****************************************************************************/
11509
11510DropdbStmt: DROP DATABASE name
11511				{
11512					DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
11513
11514					n->dbname = $3;
11515					n->missing_ok = false;
11516					n->options = NULL;
11517					$$ = (Node *) n;
11518				}
11519			| DROP DATABASE IF_P EXISTS name
11520				{
11521					DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
11522
11523					n->dbname = $5;
11524					n->missing_ok = true;
11525					n->options = NULL;
11526					$$ = (Node *) n;
11527				}
11528			| DROP DATABASE name opt_with '(' drop_option_list ')'
11529				{
11530					DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
11531
11532					n->dbname = $3;
11533					n->missing_ok = false;
11534					n->options = $6;
11535					$$ = (Node *) n;
11536				}
11537			| DROP DATABASE IF_P EXISTS name opt_with '(' drop_option_list ')'
11538				{
11539					DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
11540
11541					n->dbname = $5;
11542					n->missing_ok = true;
11543					n->options = $8;
11544					$$ = (Node *) n;
11545				}
11546		;
11547
11548drop_option_list:
11549			drop_option
11550				{
11551					$$ = list_make1((Node *) $1);
11552				}
11553			| drop_option_list ',' drop_option
11554				{
11555					$$ = lappend($1, (Node *) $3);
11556				}
11557		;
11558
11559/*
11560 * Currently only the FORCE option is supported, but the syntax is designed
11561 * to be extensible so that we can add more options in the future if required.
11562 */
11563drop_option:
11564			FORCE
11565				{
11566					$$ = makeDefElem("force", NULL, @1);
11567				}
11568		;
11569
11570/*****************************************************************************
11571 *
11572 *		ALTER COLLATION
11573 *
11574 *****************************************************************************/
11575
11576AlterCollationStmt: ALTER COLLATION any_name REFRESH VERSION_P
11577				{
11578					AlterCollationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterCollationStmt);
11579
11580					n->collname = $3;
11581					$$ = (Node *) n;
11582				}
11583		;
11584
11585
11586/*****************************************************************************
11587 *
11588 *		ALTER SYSTEM
11589 *
11590 * This is used to change configuration parameters persistently.
11591 *****************************************************************************/
11592
11593AlterSystemStmt:
11594			ALTER SYSTEM_P SET generic_set
11595				{
11596					AlterSystemStmt *n = makeNode(AlterSystemStmt);
11597
11598					n->setstmt = $4;
11599					$$ = (Node *) n;
11600				}
11601			| ALTER SYSTEM_P RESET generic_reset
11602				{
11603					AlterSystemStmt *n = makeNode(AlterSystemStmt);
11604
11605					n->setstmt = $4;
11606					$$ = (Node *) n;
11607				}
11608		;
11609
11610
11611/*****************************************************************************
11612 *
11613 * Manipulate a domain
11614 *
11615 *****************************************************************************/
11616
11617CreateDomainStmt:
11618			CREATE DOMAIN_P any_name opt_as Typename ColQualList
11619				{
11620					CreateDomainStmt *n = makeNode(CreateDomainStmt);
11621
11622					n->domainname = $3;
11623					n->typeName = $5;
11624					SplitColQualList($6, &n->constraints, &n->collClause,
11625									 yyscanner);
11626					$$ = (Node *) n;
11627				}
11628		;
11629
11630AlterDomainStmt:
11631			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> {SET DEFAULT <expr>|DROP DEFAULT} */
11632			ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name alter_column_default
11633				{
11634					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11635
11636					n->subtype = 'T';
11637					n->typeName = $3;
11638					n->def = $4;
11639					$$ = (Node *) n;
11640				}
11641			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> DROP NOT NULL */
11642			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name DROP NOT NULL_P
11643				{
11644					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11645
11646					n->subtype = 'N';
11647					n->typeName = $3;
11648					$$ = (Node *) n;
11649				}
11650			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> SET NOT NULL */
11651			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name SET NOT NULL_P
11652				{
11653					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11654
11655					n->subtype = 'O';
11656					n->typeName = $3;
11657					$$ = (Node *) n;
11658				}
11659			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> ADD CONSTRAINT ... */
11660			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name ADD_P DomainConstraint
11661				{
11662					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11663
11664					n->subtype = 'C';
11665					n->typeName = $3;
11666					n->def = $5;
11667					$$ = (Node *) n;
11668				}
11669			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> DROP CONSTRAINT <name> [RESTRICT|CASCADE] */
11670			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name DROP CONSTRAINT name opt_drop_behavior
11671				{
11672					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11673
11674					n->subtype = 'X';
11675					n->typeName = $3;
11676					n->name = $6;
11677					n->behavior = $7;
11678					n->missing_ok = false;
11679					$$ = (Node *) n;
11680				}
11681			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS <name> [RESTRICT|CASCADE] */
11682			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name DROP CONSTRAINT IF_P EXISTS name opt_drop_behavior
11683				{
11684					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11685
11686					n->subtype = 'X';
11687					n->typeName = $3;
11688					n->name = $8;
11689					n->behavior = $9;
11690					n->missing_ok = true;
11691					$$ = (Node *) n;
11692				}
11693			/* ALTER DOMAIN <domain> VALIDATE CONSTRAINT <name> */
11694			| ALTER DOMAIN_P any_name VALIDATE CONSTRAINT name
11695				{
11696					AlterDomainStmt *n = makeNode(AlterDomainStmt);
11697
11698					n->subtype = 'V';
11699					n->typeName = $3;
11700					n->name = $6;
11701					$$ = (Node *) n;
11702				}
11703			;
11704
11705opt_as:		AS
11706			| /* EMPTY */
11707		;
11708
11709
11710/*****************************************************************************
11711 *
11712 * Manipulate a text search dictionary or configuration
11713 *
11714 *****************************************************************************/
11715
11716AlterTSDictionaryStmt:
11717			ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH DICTIONARY any_name definition
11718				{
11719					AlterTSDictionaryStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSDictionaryStmt);
11720
11721					n->dictname = $5;
11722					n->options = $6;
11723					$$ = (Node *) n;
11724				}
11725		;
11726
11727AlterTSConfigurationStmt:
11728			ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name ADD_P MAPPING FOR name_list any_with any_name_list
11729				{
11730					AlterTSConfigurationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSConfigurationStmt);
11731
11732					n->kind = ALTER_TSCONFIG_ADD_MAPPING;
11733					n->cfgname = $5;
11734					n->tokentype = $9;
11735					n->dicts = $11;
11736					n->override = false;
11737					n->replace = false;
11738					$$ = (Node *) n;
11739				}
11740			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name ALTER MAPPING FOR name_list any_with any_name_list
11741				{
11742					AlterTSConfigurationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSConfigurationStmt);
11743
11744					n->kind = ALTER_TSCONFIG_ALTER_MAPPING_FOR_TOKEN;
11745					n->cfgname = $5;
11746					n->tokentype = $9;
11747					n->dicts = $11;
11748					n->override = true;
11749					n->replace = false;
11750					$$ = (Node *) n;
11751				}
11752			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name ALTER MAPPING REPLACE any_name any_with any_name
11753				{
11754					AlterTSConfigurationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSConfigurationStmt);
11755
11756					n->kind = ALTER_TSCONFIG_REPLACE_DICT;
11757					n->cfgname = $5;
11758					n->tokentype = NIL;
11759					n->dicts = list_make2($9,$11);
11760					n->override = false;
11761					n->replace = true;
11762					$$ = (Node *) n;
11763				}
11764			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name ALTER MAPPING FOR name_list REPLACE any_name any_with any_name
11765				{
11766					AlterTSConfigurationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSConfigurationStmt);
11767
11768					n->kind = ALTER_TSCONFIG_REPLACE_DICT_FOR_TOKEN;
11769					n->cfgname = $5;
11770					n->tokentype = $9;
11771					n->dicts = list_make2($11,$13);
11772					n->override = false;
11773					n->replace = true;
11774					$$ = (Node *) n;
11775				}
11776			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name DROP MAPPING FOR name_list
11777				{
11778					AlterTSConfigurationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSConfigurationStmt);
11779
11780					n->kind = ALTER_TSCONFIG_DROP_MAPPING;
11781					n->cfgname = $5;
11782					n->tokentype = $9;
11783					n->missing_ok = false;
11784					$$ = (Node *) n;
11785				}
11786			| ALTER TEXT_P SEARCH CONFIGURATION any_name DROP MAPPING IF_P EXISTS FOR name_list
11787				{
11788					AlterTSConfigurationStmt *n = makeNode(AlterTSConfigurationStmt);
11789
11790					n->kind = ALTER_TSCONFIG_DROP_MAPPING;
11791					n->cfgname = $5;
11792					n->tokentype = $11;
11793					n->missing_ok = true;
11794					$$ = (Node *) n;
11795				}
11796		;
11797
11798/* Use this if TIME or ORDINALITY after WITH should be taken as an identifier */
11799any_with:	WITH
11800			| WITH_LA
11801		;
11802
11803
11804/*****************************************************************************
11805 *
11806 * Manipulate a conversion
11807 *
11808 *		CREATE [DEFAULT] CONVERSION <conversion_name>
11809 *		FOR <encoding_name> TO <encoding_name> FROM <func_name>
11810 *
11811 *****************************************************************************/
11812
11813CreateConversionStmt:
11814			CREATE opt_default CONVERSION_P any_name FOR Sconst
11815			TO Sconst FROM any_name
11816			{
11817				CreateConversionStmt *n = makeNode(CreateConversionStmt);
11818
11819				n->conversion_name = $4;
11820				n->for_encoding_name = $6;
11821				n->to_encoding_name = $8;
11822				n->func_name = $10;
11823				n->def = $2;
11824				$$ = (Node *) n;
11825			}
11826		;
11827
11828/*****************************************************************************
11829 *
11830 *		QUERY:
11831 *				CLUSTER (options) [ <qualified_name> [ USING <index_name> ] ]
11832 *				CLUSTER [VERBOSE] [ <qualified_name> [ USING <index_name> ] ]
11833 *				CLUSTER [VERBOSE] <index_name> ON <qualified_name> (for pre-8.3)
11834 *
11835 *****************************************************************************/
11836
11837ClusterStmt:
11838			CLUSTER '(' utility_option_list ')' qualified_name cluster_index_specification
11839				{
11840					ClusterStmt *n = makeNode(ClusterStmt);
11841
11842					n->relation = $5;
11843					n->indexname = $6;
11844					n->params = $3;
11845					$$ = (Node *) n;
11846				}
11847			| CLUSTER '(' utility_option_list ')'
11848				{
11849					ClusterStmt *n = makeNode(ClusterStmt);
11850
11851					n->relation = NULL;
11852					n->indexname = NULL;
11853					n->params = $3;
11854					$$ = (Node *) n;
11855				}
11856			/* unparenthesized VERBOSE kept for pre-14 compatibility */
11857			| CLUSTER opt_verbose qualified_name cluster_index_specification
11858				{
11859					ClusterStmt *n = makeNode(ClusterStmt);
11860
11861					n->relation = $3;
11862					n->indexname = $4;
11863					n->params = NIL;
11864					if ($2)
11865						n->params = lappend(n->params, makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @2));
11866					$$ = (Node *) n;
11867				}
11868			/* unparenthesized VERBOSE kept for pre-17 compatibility */
11869			| CLUSTER opt_verbose
11870				{
11871					ClusterStmt *n = makeNode(ClusterStmt);
11872
11873					n->relation = NULL;
11874					n->indexname = NULL;
11875					n->params = NIL;
11876					if ($2)
11877						n->params = lappend(n->params, makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @2));
11878					$$ = (Node *) n;
11879				}
11880			/* kept for pre-8.3 compatibility */
11881			| CLUSTER opt_verbose name ON qualified_name
11882				{
11883					ClusterStmt *n = makeNode(ClusterStmt);
11884
11885					n->relation = $5;
11886					n->indexname = $3;
11887					n->params = NIL;
11888					if ($2)
11889						n->params = lappend(n->params, makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @2));
11890					$$ = (Node *) n;
11891				}
11892		;
11893
11894cluster_index_specification:
11895			USING name				{ $$ = $2; }
11896			| /*EMPTY*/				{ $$ = NULL; }
11897		;
11898
11899
11900/*****************************************************************************
11901 *
11902 *		QUERY:
11903 *				VACUUM
11904 *				ANALYZE
11905 *
11906 *****************************************************************************/
11907
11908VacuumStmt: VACUUM opt_full opt_freeze opt_verbose opt_analyze opt_vacuum_relation_list
11909				{
11910					VacuumStmt *n = makeNode(VacuumStmt);
11911
11912					n->options = NIL;
11913					if ($2)
11914						n->options = lappend(n->options,
11915											 makeDefElem("full", NULL, @2));
11916					if ($3)
11917						n->options = lappend(n->options,
11918											 makeDefElem("freeze", NULL, @3));
11919					if ($4)
11920						n->options = lappend(n->options,
11921											 makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @4));
11922					if ($5)
11923						n->options = lappend(n->options,
11924											 makeDefElem("analyze", NULL, @5));
11925					n->rels = $6;
11926					n->is_vacuumcmd = true;
11927					$$ = (Node *) n;
11928				}
11929			| VACUUM '(' utility_option_list ')' opt_vacuum_relation_list
11930				{
11931					VacuumStmt *n = makeNode(VacuumStmt);
11932
11933					n->options = $3;
11934					n->rels = $5;
11935					n->is_vacuumcmd = true;
11936					$$ = (Node *) n;
11937				}
11938		;
11939
11940AnalyzeStmt: analyze_keyword opt_verbose opt_vacuum_relation_list
11941				{
11942					VacuumStmt *n = makeNode(VacuumStmt);
11943
11944					n->options = NIL;
11945					if ($2)
11946						n->options = lappend(n->options,
11947											 makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @2));
11948					n->rels = $3;
11949					n->is_vacuumcmd = false;
11950					$$ = (Node *) n;
11951				}
11952			| analyze_keyword '(' utility_option_list ')' opt_vacuum_relation_list
11953				{
11954					VacuumStmt *n = makeNode(VacuumStmt);
11955
11956					n->options = $3;
11957					n->rels = $5;
11958					n->is_vacuumcmd = false;
11959					$$ = (Node *) n;
11960				}
11961		;
11962
11963utility_option_list:
11964			utility_option_elem
11965				{
11966					$$ = list_make1($1);
11967				}
11968			| utility_option_list ',' utility_option_elem
11969				{
11970					$$ = lappend($1, $3);
11971				}
11972		;
11973
11974analyze_keyword:
11975			ANALYZE
11976			| ANALYSE /* British */
11977		;
11978
11979utility_option_elem:
11980			utility_option_name utility_option_arg
11981				{
11982					$$ = makeDefElem($1, $2, @1);
11983				}
11984		;
11985
11986utility_option_name:
11987			NonReservedWord							{ $$ = $1; }
11988			| analyze_keyword						{ $$ = "analyze"; }
11989			| FORMAT_LA								{ $$ = "format"; }
11990		;
11991
11992utility_option_arg:
11993			opt_boolean_or_string					{ $$ = (Node *) makeString($1); }
11994			| NumericOnly							{ $$ = (Node *) $1; }
11995			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NULL; }
11996		;
11997
11998opt_analyze:
11999			analyze_keyword							{ $$ = true; }
12000			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
12001		;
12002
12003opt_verbose:
12004			VERBOSE									{ $$ = true; }
12005			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
12006		;
12007
12008opt_full:	FULL									{ $$ = true; }
12009			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
12010		;
12011
12012opt_freeze: FREEZE									{ $$ = true; }
12013			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
12014		;
12015
12016opt_name_list:
12017			'(' name_list ')'						{ $$ = $2; }
12018			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
12019		;
12020
12021vacuum_relation:
12022			relation_expr opt_name_list
12023				{
12024					$$ = (Node *) makeVacuumRelation($1, InvalidOid, $2);
12025				}
12026		;
12027
12028vacuum_relation_list:
12029			vacuum_relation
12030					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
12031			| vacuum_relation_list ',' vacuum_relation
12032					{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
12033		;
12034
12035opt_vacuum_relation_list:
12036			vacuum_relation_list					{ $$ = $1; }
12037			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
12038		;
12039
12040
12041/*****************************************************************************
12042 *
12043 *		QUERY:
12044 *				EXPLAIN [ANALYZE] [VERBOSE] query
12045 *				EXPLAIN ( options ) query
12046 *
12047 *****************************************************************************/
12048
12049ExplainStmt:
12050		EXPLAIN ExplainableStmt
12051				{
12052					ExplainStmt *n = makeNode(ExplainStmt);
12053
12054					n->query = $2;
12055					n->options = NIL;
12056					$$ = (Node *) n;
12057				}
12058		| EXPLAIN analyze_keyword opt_verbose ExplainableStmt
12059				{
12060					ExplainStmt *n = makeNode(ExplainStmt);
12061
12062					n->query = $4;
12063					n->options = list_make1(makeDefElem("analyze", NULL, @2));
12064					if ($3)
12065						n->options = lappend(n->options,
12066											 makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @3));
12067					$$ = (Node *) n;
12068				}
12069		| EXPLAIN VERBOSE ExplainableStmt
12070				{
12071					ExplainStmt *n = makeNode(ExplainStmt);
12072
12073					n->query = $3;
12074					n->options = list_make1(makeDefElem("verbose", NULL, @2));
12075					$$ = (Node *) n;
12076				}
12077		| EXPLAIN '(' utility_option_list ')' ExplainableStmt
12078				{
12079					ExplainStmt *n = makeNode(ExplainStmt);
12080
12081					n->query = $5;
12082					n->options = $3;
12083					$$ = (Node *) n;
12084				}
12085		;
12086
12087ExplainableStmt:
12088			SelectStmt
12089			| InsertStmt
12090			| UpdateStmt
12091			| DeleteStmt
12092			| MergeStmt
12093			| DeclareCursorStmt
12094			| CreateAsStmt
12095			| CreateMatViewStmt
12096			| RefreshMatViewStmt
12097			| ExecuteStmt					/* by default all are $$=$1 */
12098		;
12099
12100/*****************************************************************************
12101 *
12102 *		QUERY:
12103 *				PREPARE <plan_name> [(args, ...)] AS <query>
12104 *
12105 *****************************************************************************/
12106
12107PrepareStmt: PREPARE name prep_type_clause AS PreparableStmt
12108				{
12109					PrepareStmt *n = makeNode(PrepareStmt);
12110
12111					n->name = $2;
12112					n->argtypes = $3;
12113					n->query = $5;
12114					$$ = (Node *) n;
12115				}
12116		;
12117
12118prep_type_clause: '(' type_list ')'			{ $$ = $2; }
12119				| /* EMPTY */				{ $$ = NIL; }
12120		;
12121
12122PreparableStmt:
12123			SelectStmt
12124			| InsertStmt
12125			| UpdateStmt
12126			| DeleteStmt
12127			| MergeStmt						/* by default all are $$=$1 */
12128		;
12129
12130/*****************************************************************************
12131 *
12132 * EXECUTE <plan_name> [(params, ...)]
12133 * CREATE TABLE <name> AS EXECUTE <plan_name> [(params, ...)]
12134 *
12135 *****************************************************************************/
12136
12137ExecuteStmt: EXECUTE name execute_param_clause
12138				{
12139					ExecuteStmt *n = makeNode(ExecuteStmt);
12140
12141					n->name = $2;
12142					n->params = $3;
12143					$$ = (Node *) n;
12144				}
12145			| CREATE OptTemp TABLE create_as_target AS
12146				EXECUTE name execute_param_clause opt_with_data
12147				{
12148					CreateTableAsStmt *ctas = makeNode(CreateTableAsStmt);
12149					ExecuteStmt *n = makeNode(ExecuteStmt);
12150
12151					n->name = $7;
12152					n->params = $8;
12153					ctas->query = (Node *) n;
12154					ctas->into = $4;
12155					ctas->objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
12156					ctas->is_select_into = false;
12157					ctas->if_not_exists = false;
12158					/* cram additional flags into the IntoClause */
12159					$4->rel->relpersistence = $2;
12160					$4->skipData = !($9);
12161					$$ = (Node *) ctas;
12162				}
12163			| CREATE OptTemp TABLE IF_P NOT EXISTS create_as_target AS
12164				EXECUTE name execute_param_clause opt_with_data
12165				{
12166					CreateTableAsStmt *ctas = makeNode(CreateTableAsStmt);
12167					ExecuteStmt *n = makeNode(ExecuteStmt);
12168
12169					n->name = $10;
12170					n->params = $11;
12171					ctas->query = (Node *) n;
12172					ctas->into = $7;
12173					ctas->objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
12174					ctas->is_select_into = false;
12175					ctas->if_not_exists = true;
12176					/* cram additional flags into the IntoClause */
12177					$7->rel->relpersistence = $2;
12178					$7->skipData = !($12);
12179					$$ = (Node *) ctas;
12180				}
12181		;
12182
12183execute_param_clause: '(' expr_list ')'				{ $$ = $2; }
12184					| /* EMPTY */					{ $$ = NIL; }
12185					;
12186
12187/*****************************************************************************
12188 *
12189 *		QUERY:
12190 *				DEALLOCATE [PREPARE] <plan_name>
12191 *
12192 *****************************************************************************/
12193
12194DeallocateStmt: DEALLOCATE name
12195					{
12196						DeallocateStmt *n = makeNode(DeallocateStmt);
12197
12198						n->name = $2;
12199						n->isall = false;
12200						n->location = @2;
12201						$$ = (Node *) n;
12202					}
12203				| DEALLOCATE PREPARE name
12204					{
12205						DeallocateStmt *n = makeNode(DeallocateStmt);
12206
12207						n->name = $3;
12208						n->isall = false;
12209						n->location = @3;
12210						$$ = (Node *) n;
12211					}
12212				| DEALLOCATE ALL
12213					{
12214						DeallocateStmt *n = makeNode(DeallocateStmt);
12215
12216						n->name = NULL;
12217						n->isall = true;
12218						n->location = -1;
12219						$$ = (Node *) n;
12220					}
12221				| DEALLOCATE PREPARE ALL
12222					{
12223						DeallocateStmt *n = makeNode(DeallocateStmt);
12224
12225						n->name = NULL;
12226						n->isall = true;
12227						n->location = -1;
12228						$$ = (Node *) n;
12229					}
12230		;
12231
12232/*****************************************************************************
12233 *
12234 *		QUERY:
12235 *				INSERT STATEMENTS
12236 *
12237 *****************************************************************************/
12238
12239InsertStmt:
12240			opt_with_clause INSERT INTO insert_target insert_rest
12241			opt_on_conflict returning_clause
12242				{
12243					$5->relation = $4;
12244					$5->onConflictClause = $6;
12245					$5->returningClause = $7;
12246					$5->withClause = $1;
12247					$$ = (Node *) $5;
12248				}
12249		;
12250
12251/*
12252 * Can't easily make AS optional here, because VALUES in insert_rest would
12253 * have a shift/reduce conflict with VALUES as an optional alias.  We could
12254 * easily allow unreserved_keywords as optional aliases, but that'd be an odd
12255 * divergence from other places.  So just require AS for now.
12256 */
12257insert_target:
12258			qualified_name
12259				{
12260					$$ = $1;
12261				}
12262			| qualified_name AS ColId
12263				{
12264					$1->alias = makeAlias($3, NIL);
12265					$$ = $1;
12266				}
12267		;
12268
12269insert_rest:
12270			SelectStmt
12271				{
12272					$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);
12273					$$->cols = NIL;
12274					$$->selectStmt = $1;
12275				}
12276			| OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P SelectStmt
12277				{
12278					$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);
12279					$$->cols = NIL;
12280					$$->override = $2;
12281					$$->selectStmt = $4;
12282				}
12283			| '(' insert_column_list ')' SelectStmt
12284				{
12285					$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);
12286					$$->cols = $2;
12287					$$->selectStmt = $4;
12288				}
12289			| '(' insert_column_list ')' OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P SelectStmt
12290				{
12291					$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);
12292					$$->cols = $2;
12293					$$->override = $5;
12294					$$->selectStmt = $7;
12295				}
12296			| DEFAULT VALUES
12297				{
12298					$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);
12299					$$->cols = NIL;
12300					$$->selectStmt = NULL;
12301				}
12302		;
12303
12304override_kind:
12305			USER		{ $$ = OVERRIDING_USER_VALUE; }
12306			| SYSTEM_P	{ $$ = OVERRIDING_SYSTEM_VALUE; }
12307		;
12308
12309insert_column_list:
12310			insert_column_item
12311					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
12312			| insert_column_list ',' insert_column_item
12313					{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
12314		;
12315
12316insert_column_item:
12317			ColId opt_indirection
12318				{
12319					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
12320					$$->name = $1;
12321					$$->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);
12322					$$->val = NULL;
12323					$$->location = @1;
12324				}
12325		;
12326
12327opt_on_conflict:
12328			ON CONFLICT opt_conf_expr DO UPDATE SET set_clause_list	where_clause
12329				{
12330					$$ = makeNode(OnConflictClause);
12331					$$->action = ONCONFLICT_UPDATE;
12332					$$->infer = $3;
12333					$$->targetList = $7;
12334					$$->whereClause = $8;
12335					$$->location = @1;
12336				}
12337			|
12338			ON CONFLICT opt_conf_expr DO NOTHING
12339				{
12340					$$ = makeNode(OnConflictClause);
12341					$$->action = ONCONFLICT_NOTHING;
12342					$$->infer = $3;
12343					$$->targetList = NIL;
12344					$$->whereClause = NULL;
12345					$$->location = @1;
12346				}
12347			| /*EMPTY*/
12348				{
12349					$$ = NULL;
12350				}
12351		;
12352
12353opt_conf_expr:
12354			'(' index_params ')' where_clause
12355				{
12356					$$ = makeNode(InferClause);
12357					$$->indexElems = $2;
12358					$$->whereClause = $4;
12359					$$->conname = NULL;
12360					$$->location = @1;
12361				}
12362			|
12363			ON CONSTRAINT name
12364				{
12365					$$ = makeNode(InferClause);
12366					$$->indexElems = NIL;
12367					$$->whereClause = NULL;
12368					$$->conname = $3;
12369					$$->location = @1;
12370				}
12371			| /*EMPTY*/
12372				{
12373					$$ = NULL;
12374				}
12375		;
12376
12377returning_clause:
12378			RETURNING returning_with_clause target_list
12379				{
12380					ReturningClause *n = makeNode(ReturningClause);
12381
12382					n->options = $2;
12383					n->exprs = $3;
12384					$$ = n;
12385				}
12386			| /* EMPTY */
12387				{
12388					$$ = NULL;
12389				}
12390		;
12391
12392returning_with_clause:
12393			WITH '(' returning_options ')'		{ $$ = $3; }
12394			| /* EMPTY */						{ $$ = NIL; }
12395		;
12396
12397returning_options:
12398			returning_option							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
12399			| returning_options ',' returning_option	{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
12400		;
12401
12402returning_option:
12403			returning_option_kind AS ColId
12404				{
12405					ReturningOption *n = makeNode(ReturningOption);
12406
12407					n->option = $1;
12408					n->value = $3;
12409					n->location = @1;
12410					$$ = (Node *) n;
12411				}
12412		;
12413
12414returning_option_kind:
12415			OLD			{ $$ = RETURNING_OPTION_OLD; }
12416			| NEW		{ $$ = RETURNING_OPTION_NEW; }
12417		;
12418
12419
12420/*****************************************************************************
12421 *
12422 *		QUERY:
12423 *				DELETE STATEMENTS
12424 *
12425 *****************************************************************************/
12426
12427DeleteStmt: opt_with_clause DELETE_P FROM relation_expr_opt_alias
12428			using_clause where_or_current_clause returning_clause
12429				{
12430					DeleteStmt *n = makeNode(DeleteStmt);
12431
12432					n->relation = $4;
12433					n->usingClause = $5;
12434					n->whereClause = $6;
12435					n->returningClause = $7;
12436					n->withClause = $1;
12437					$$ = (Node *) n;
12438				}
12439		;
12440
12441using_clause:
12442				USING from_list						{ $$ = $2; }
12443			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
12444		;
12445
12446
12447/*****************************************************************************
12448 *
12449 *		QUERY:
12450 *				LOCK TABLE
12451 *
12452 *****************************************************************************/
12453
12454LockStmt:	LOCK_P opt_table relation_expr_list opt_lock opt_nowait
12455				{
12456					LockStmt   *n = makeNode(LockStmt);
12457
12458					n->relations = $3;
12459					n->mode = $4;
12460					n->nowait = $5;
12461					$$ = (Node *) n;
12462				}
12463		;
12464
12465opt_lock:	IN_P lock_type MODE				{ $$ = $2; }
12466			| /*EMPTY*/						{ $$ = AccessExclusiveLock; }
12467		;
12468
12469lock_type:	ACCESS SHARE					{ $$ = AccessShareLock; }
12470			| ROW SHARE						{ $$ = RowShareLock; }
12471			| ROW EXCLUSIVE					{ $$ = RowExclusiveLock; }
12472			| SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE		{ $$ = ShareUpdateExclusiveLock; }
12473			| SHARE							{ $$ = ShareLock; }
12474			| SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE			{ $$ = ShareRowExclusiveLock; }
12475			| EXCLUSIVE						{ $$ = ExclusiveLock; }
12476			| ACCESS EXCLUSIVE				{ $$ = AccessExclusiveLock; }
12477		;
12478
12479opt_nowait:	NOWAIT							{ $$ = true; }
12480			| /*EMPTY*/						{ $$ = false; }
12481		;
12482
12483opt_nowait_or_skip:
12484			NOWAIT							{ $$ = LockWaitError; }
12485			| SKIP LOCKED					{ $$ = LockWaitSkip; }
12486			| /*EMPTY*/						{ $$ = LockWaitBlock; }
12487		;
12488
12489
12490/*****************************************************************************
12491 *
12492 *		QUERY:
12493 *				UpdateStmt (UPDATE)
12494 *
12495 *****************************************************************************/
12496
12497UpdateStmt: opt_with_clause UPDATE relation_expr_opt_alias
12498			SET set_clause_list
12499			from_clause
12500			where_or_current_clause
12501			returning_clause
12502				{
12503					UpdateStmt *n = makeNode(UpdateStmt);
12504
12505					n->relation = $3;
12506					n->targetList = $5;
12507					n->fromClause = $6;
12508					n->whereClause = $7;
12509					n->returningClause = $8;
12510					n->withClause = $1;
12511					$$ = (Node *) n;
12512				}
12513		;
12514
12515set_clause_list:
12516			set_clause							{ $$ = $1; }
12517			| set_clause_list ',' set_clause	{ $$ = list_concat($1,$3); }
12518		;
12519
12520set_clause:
12521			set_target '=' a_expr
12522				{
12523					$1->val = (Node *) $3;
12524					$$ = list_make1($1);
12525				}
12526			| '(' set_target_list ')' '=' a_expr
12527				{
12528					int			ncolumns = list_length($2);
12529					int			i = 1;
12530					ListCell   *col_cell;
12531
12532					/* Create a MultiAssignRef source for each target */
12533					foreach(col_cell, $2)
12534					{
12535						ResTarget  *res_col = (ResTarget *) lfirst(col_cell);
12536						MultiAssignRef *r = makeNode(MultiAssignRef);
12537
12538						r->source = (Node *) $5;
12539						r->colno = i;
12540						r->ncolumns = ncolumns;
12541						res_col->val = (Node *) r;
12542						i++;
12543					}
12544
12545					$$ = $2;
12546				}
12547		;
12548
12549set_target:
12550			ColId opt_indirection
12551				{
12552					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
12553					$$->name = $1;
12554					$$->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);
12555					$$->val = NULL;	/* upper production sets this */
12556					$$->location = @1;
12557				}
12558		;
12559
12560set_target_list:
12561			set_target								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
12562			| set_target_list ',' set_target		{ $$ = lappend($1,$3); }
12563		;
12564
12565
12566/*****************************************************************************
12567 *
12568 *		QUERY:
12569 *				MERGE
12570 *
12571 *****************************************************************************/
12572
12573MergeStmt:
12574			opt_with_clause MERGE INTO relation_expr_opt_alias
12575			USING table_ref
12576			ON a_expr
12577			merge_when_list
12578			returning_clause
12579				{
12580					MergeStmt  *m = makeNode(MergeStmt);
12581
12582					m->withClause = $1;
12583					m->relation = $4;
12584					m->sourceRelation = $6;
12585					m->joinCondition = $8;
12586					m->mergeWhenClauses = $9;
12587					m->returningClause = $10;
12588
12589					$$ = (Node *) m;
12590				}
12591		;
12592
12593merge_when_list:
12594			merge_when_clause						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
12595			| merge_when_list merge_when_clause		{ $$ = lappend($1,$2); }
12596		;
12597
12598/*
12599 * A WHEN clause may be WHEN MATCHED, WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE, or WHEN NOT
12600 * MATCHED [BY TARGET]. The first two cases match target tuples, and support
12601 * UPDATE/DELETE/DO NOTHING actions. The third case does not match target
12602 * tuples, and only supports INSERT/DO NOTHING actions.
12603 */
12604merge_when_clause:
12605			merge_when_tgt_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN merge_update
12606				{
12607					$4->matchKind = $1;
12608					$4->condition = $2;
12609
12610					$$ = (Node *) $4;
12611				}
12612			| merge_when_tgt_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN merge_delete
12613				{
12614					$4->matchKind = $1;
12615					$4->condition = $2;
12616
12617					$$ = (Node *) $4;
12618				}
12619			| merge_when_tgt_not_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN merge_insert
12620				{
12621					$4->matchKind = $1;
12622					$4->condition = $2;
12623
12624					$$ = (Node *) $4;
12625				}
12626			| merge_when_tgt_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN DO NOTHING
12627				{
12628					MergeWhenClause *m = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12629
12630					m->matchKind = $1;
12631					m->commandType = CMD_NOTHING;
12632					m->condition = $2;
12633
12634					$$ = (Node *) m;
12635				}
12636			| merge_when_tgt_not_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN DO NOTHING
12637				{
12638					MergeWhenClause *m = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12639
12640					m->matchKind = $1;
12641					m->commandType = CMD_NOTHING;
12642					m->condition = $2;
12643
12644					$$ = (Node *) m;
12645				}
12646		;
12647
12648merge_when_tgt_matched:
12649			WHEN MATCHED					{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_MATCHED; }
12650			| WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE	{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_SOURCE; }
12651		;
12652
12653merge_when_tgt_not_matched:
12654			WHEN NOT MATCHED				{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_TARGET; }
12655			| WHEN NOT MATCHED BY TARGET	{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_TARGET; }
12656		;
12657
12658opt_merge_when_condition:
12659			AND a_expr				{ $$ = $2; }
12660			|						{ $$ = NULL; }
12661		;
12662
12663merge_update:
12664			UPDATE SET set_clause_list
12665				{
12666					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12667					n->commandType = CMD_UPDATE;
12668					n->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;
12669					n->targetList = $3;
12670					n->values = NIL;
12671
12672					$$ = n;
12673				}
12674		;
12675
12676merge_delete:
12677			DELETE_P
12678				{
12679					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12680					n->commandType = CMD_DELETE;
12681					n->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;
12682					n->targetList = NIL;
12683					n->values = NIL;
12684
12685					$$ = n;
12686				}
12687		;
12688
12689merge_insert:
12690			INSERT merge_values_clause
12691				{
12692					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12693					n->commandType = CMD_INSERT;
12694					n->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;
12695					n->targetList = NIL;
12696					n->values = $2;
12697					$$ = n;
12698				}
12699			| INSERT OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P merge_values_clause
12700				{
12701					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12702					n->commandType = CMD_INSERT;
12703					n->override = $3;
12704					n->targetList = NIL;
12705					n->values = $5;
12706					$$ = n;
12707				}
12708			| INSERT '(' insert_column_list ')' merge_values_clause
12709				{
12710					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12711					n->commandType = CMD_INSERT;
12712					n->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;
12713					n->targetList = $3;
12714					n->values = $5;
12715					$$ = n;
12716				}
12717			| INSERT '(' insert_column_list ')' OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P merge_values_clause
12718				{
12719					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12720					n->commandType = CMD_INSERT;
12721					n->override = $6;
12722					n->targetList = $3;
12723					n->values = $8;
12724					$$ = n;
12725				}
12726			| INSERT DEFAULT VALUES
12727				{
12728					MergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);
12729					n->commandType = CMD_INSERT;
12730					n->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;
12731					n->targetList = NIL;
12732					n->values = NIL;
12733					$$ = n;
12734				}
12735		;
12736
12737merge_values_clause:
12738			VALUES '(' expr_list ')'
12739				{
12740					$$ = $3;
12741				}
12742		;
12743
12744/*****************************************************************************
12745 *
12746 *		QUERY:
12747 *				CURSOR STATEMENTS
12748 *
12749 *****************************************************************************/
12750DeclareCursorStmt: DECLARE cursor_name cursor_options CURSOR opt_hold FOR SelectStmt
12751				{
12752					DeclareCursorStmt *n = makeNode(DeclareCursorStmt);
12753
12754					n->portalname = $2;
12755					/* currently we always set FAST_PLAN option */
12756					n->options = $3 | $5 | CURSOR_OPT_FAST_PLAN;
12757					n->query = $7;
12758					$$ = (Node *) n;
12759				}
12760		;
12761
12762cursor_name:	name						{ $$ = $1; }
12763		;
12764
12765cursor_options: /*EMPTY*/					{ $$ = 0; }
12766			| cursor_options NO SCROLL		{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_NO_SCROLL; }
12767			| cursor_options SCROLL			{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL; }
12768			| cursor_options BINARY			{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_BINARY; }
12769			| cursor_options ASENSITIVE		{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_ASENSITIVE; }
12770			| cursor_options INSENSITIVE	{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_INSENSITIVE; }
12771		;
12772
12773opt_hold: /* EMPTY */						{ $$ = 0; }
12774			| WITH HOLD						{ $$ = CURSOR_OPT_HOLD; }
12775			| WITHOUT HOLD					{ $$ = 0; }
12776		;
12777
12778/*****************************************************************************
12779 *
12780 *		QUERY:
12781 *				SELECT STATEMENTS
12782 *
12783 *****************************************************************************/
12784
12785/* A complete SELECT statement looks like this.
12786 *
12787 * The rule returns either a single SelectStmt node or a tree of them,
12788 * representing a set-operation tree.
12789 *
12790 * There is an ambiguity when a sub-SELECT is within an a_expr and there
12791 * are excess parentheses: do the parentheses belong to the sub-SELECT or
12792 * to the surrounding a_expr?  We don't really care, but bison wants to know.
12793 * To resolve the ambiguity, we are careful to define the grammar so that
12794 * the decision is staved off as long as possible: as long as we can keep
12795 * absorbing parentheses into the sub-SELECT, we will do so, and only when
12796 * it's no longer possible to do that will we decide that parens belong to
12797 * the expression.	For example, in "SELECT (((SELECT 2)) + 3)" the extra
12798 * parentheses are treated as part of the sub-select.  The necessity of doing
12799 * it that way is shown by "SELECT (((SELECT 2)) UNION SELECT 2)".	Had we
12800 * parsed "((SELECT 2))" as an a_expr, it'd be too late to go back to the
12801 * SELECT viewpoint when we see the UNION.
12802 *
12803 * This approach is implemented by defining a nonterminal select_with_parens,
12804 * which represents a SELECT with at least one outer layer of parentheses,
12805 * and being careful to use select_with_parens, never '(' SelectStmt ')',
12806 * in the expression grammar.  We will then have shift-reduce conflicts
12807 * which we can resolve in favor of always treating '(' <select> ')' as
12808 * a select_with_parens.  To resolve the conflicts, the productions that
12809 * conflict with the select_with_parens productions are manually given
12810 * precedences lower than the precedence of ')', thereby ensuring that we
12811 * shift ')' (and then reduce to select_with_parens) rather than trying to
12812 * reduce the inner <select> nonterminal to something else.  We use UMINUS
12813 * precedence for this, which is a fairly arbitrary choice.
12814 *
12815 * To be able to define select_with_parens itself without ambiguity, we need
12816 * a nonterminal select_no_parens that represents a SELECT structure with no
12817 * outermost parentheses.  This is a little bit tedious, but it works.
12818 *
12819 * In non-expression contexts, we use SelectStmt which can represent a SELECT
12820 * with or without outer parentheses.
12821 */
12822
12823SelectStmt: select_no_parens			%prec UMINUS
12824			| select_with_parens		%prec UMINUS
12825		;
12826
12827select_with_parens:
12828			'(' select_no_parens ')'				{ $$ = $2; }
12829			| '(' select_with_parens ')'			{ $$ = $2; }
12830		;
12831
12832/*
12833 * This rule parses the equivalent of the standard's <query expression>.
12834 * The duplicative productions are annoying, but hard to get rid of without
12835 * creating shift/reduce conflicts.
12836 *
12837 *	The locking clause (FOR UPDATE etc) may be before or after LIMIT/OFFSET.
12838 *	In <=7.2.X, LIMIT/OFFSET had to be after FOR UPDATE
12839 *	We now support both orderings, but prefer LIMIT/OFFSET before the locking
12840 * clause.
12841 *	2002-08-28 bjm
12842 */
12843select_no_parens:
12844			simple_select						{ $$ = $1; }
12845			| select_clause sort_clause
12846				{
12847					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, NIL,
12848										NULL, NULL,
12849										yyscanner);
12850					$$ = $1;
12851				}
12852			| select_clause opt_sort_clause for_locking_clause opt_select_limit
12853				{
12854					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, $3,
12855										$4,
12856										NULL,
12857										yyscanner);
12858					$$ = $1;
12859				}
12860			| select_clause opt_sort_clause select_limit opt_for_locking_clause
12861				{
12862					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, $4,
12863										$3,
12864										NULL,
12865										yyscanner);
12866					$$ = $1;
12867				}
12868			| with_clause select_clause
12869				{
12870					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, NULL, NIL,
12871										NULL,
12872										$1,
12873										yyscanner);
12874					$$ = $2;
12875				}
12876			| with_clause select_clause sort_clause
12877				{
12878					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, NIL,
12879										NULL,
12880										$1,
12881										yyscanner);
12882					$$ = $2;
12883				}
12884			| with_clause select_clause opt_sort_clause for_locking_clause opt_select_limit
12885				{
12886					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, $4,
12887										$5,
12888										$1,
12889										yyscanner);
12890					$$ = $2;
12891				}
12892			| with_clause select_clause opt_sort_clause select_limit opt_for_locking_clause
12893				{
12894					insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, $5,
12895										$4,
12896										$1,
12897										yyscanner);
12898					$$ = $2;
12899				}
12900		;
12901
12902select_clause:
12903			simple_select							{ $$ = $1; }
12904			| select_with_parens					{ $$ = $1; }
12905		;
12906
12907/*
12908 * This rule parses SELECT statements that can appear within set operations,
12909 * including UNION, INTERSECT and EXCEPT.  '(' and ')' can be used to specify
12910 * the ordering of the set operations.	Without '(' and ')' we want the
12911 * operations to be ordered per the precedence specs at the head of this file.
12912 *
12913 * As with select_no_parens, simple_select cannot have outer parentheses,
12914 * but can have parenthesized subclauses.
12915 *
12916 * It might appear that we could fold the first two alternatives into one
12917 * by using opt_distinct_clause.  However, that causes a shift/reduce conflict
12918 * against INSERT ... SELECT ... ON CONFLICT.  We avoid the ambiguity by
12919 * requiring SELECT DISTINCT [ON] to be followed by a non-empty target_list.
12920 *
12921 * Note that sort clauses cannot be included at this level --- SQL requires
12922 *		SELECT foo UNION SELECT bar ORDER BY baz
12923 * to be parsed as
12924 *		(SELECT foo UNION SELECT bar) ORDER BY baz
12925 * not
12926 *		SELECT foo UNION (SELECT bar ORDER BY baz)
12927 * Likewise for WITH, FOR UPDATE and LIMIT.  Therefore, those clauses are
12928 * described as part of the select_no_parens production, not simple_select.
12929 * This does not limit functionality, because you can reintroduce these
12930 * clauses inside parentheses.
12931 *
12932 * NOTE: only the leftmost component SelectStmt should have INTO.
12933 * However, this is not checked by the grammar; parse analysis must check it.
12934 */
12935simple_select:
12936			SELECT opt_all_clause opt_target_list
12937			into_clause from_clause where_clause
12938			group_clause having_clause window_clause
12939				{
12940					SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
12941
12942					n->targetList = $3;
12943					n->intoClause = $4;
12944					n->fromClause = $5;
12945					n->whereClause = $6;
12946					n->groupClause = ($7)->list;
12947					n->groupDistinct = ($7)->distinct;
12948					n->havingClause = $8;
12949					n->windowClause = $9;
12950					$$ = (Node *) n;
12951				}
12952			| SELECT distinct_clause target_list
12953			into_clause from_clause where_clause
12954			group_clause having_clause window_clause
12955				{
12956					SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
12957
12958					n->distinctClause = $2;
12959					n->targetList = $3;
12960					n->intoClause = $4;
12961					n->fromClause = $5;
12962					n->whereClause = $6;
12963					n->groupClause = ($7)->list;
12964					n->groupDistinct = ($7)->distinct;
12965					n->havingClause = $8;
12966					n->windowClause = $9;
12967					$$ = (Node *) n;
12968				}
12969			| values_clause							{ $$ = $1; }
12970			| TABLE relation_expr
12971				{
12972					/* same as SELECT * FROM relation_expr */
12973					ColumnRef  *cr = makeNode(ColumnRef);
12974					ResTarget  *rt = makeNode(ResTarget);
12975					SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
12976
12977					cr->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));
12978					cr->location = -1;
12979
12980					rt->name = NULL;
12981					rt->indirection = NIL;
12982					rt->val = (Node *) cr;
12983					rt->location = -1;
12984
12985					n->targetList = list_make1(rt);
12986					n->fromClause = list_make1($2);
12987					$$ = (Node *) n;
12988				}
12989			| select_clause UNION set_quantifier select_clause
12990				{
12991					$$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_UNION, $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL, $1, $4);
12992				}
12993			| select_clause INTERSECT set_quantifier select_clause
12994				{
12995					$$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_INTERSECT, $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL, $1, $4);
12996				}
12997			| select_clause EXCEPT set_quantifier select_clause
12998				{
12999					$$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_EXCEPT, $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL, $1, $4);
13000				}
13001		;
13002
13003/*
13004 * SQL standard WITH clause looks like:
13005 *
13006 * WITH [ RECURSIVE ] <query name> [ (<column>,...) ]
13007 *		AS (query) [ SEARCH or CYCLE clause ]
13008 *
13009 * Recognizing WITH_LA here allows a CTE to be named TIME or ORDINALITY.
13010 */
13011with_clause:
13012		WITH cte_list
13013			{
13014				$$ = makeNode(WithClause);
13015				$$->ctes = $2;
13016				$$->recursive = false;
13017				$$->location = @1;
13018			}
13019		| WITH_LA cte_list
13020			{
13021				$$ = makeNode(WithClause);
13022				$$->ctes = $2;
13023				$$->recursive = false;
13024				$$->location = @1;
13025			}
13026		| WITH RECURSIVE cte_list
13027			{
13028				$$ = makeNode(WithClause);
13029				$$->ctes = $3;
13030				$$->recursive = true;
13031				$$->location = @1;
13032			}
13033		;
13034
13035cte_list:
13036		common_table_expr						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
13037		| cte_list ',' common_table_expr		{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
13038		;
13039
13040common_table_expr:  name opt_name_list AS opt_materialized '(' PreparableStmt ')' opt_search_clause opt_cycle_clause
13041			{
13042				CommonTableExpr *n = makeNode(CommonTableExpr);
13043
13044				n->ctename = $1;
13045				n->aliascolnames = $2;
13046				n->ctematerialized = $4;
13047				n->ctequery = $6;
13048				n->search_clause = castNode(CTESearchClause, $8);
13049				n->cycle_clause = castNode(CTECycleClause, $9);
13050				n->location = @1;
13051				$$ = (Node *) n;
13052			}
13053		;
13054
13055opt_materialized:
13056		MATERIALIZED							{ $$ = CTEMaterializeAlways; }
13057		| NOT MATERIALIZED						{ $$ = CTEMaterializeNever; }
13058		| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = CTEMaterializeDefault; }
13059		;
13060
13061opt_search_clause:
13062		SEARCH DEPTH FIRST_P BY columnList SET ColId
13063			{
13064				CTESearchClause *n = makeNode(CTESearchClause);
13065
13066				n->search_col_list = $5;
13067				n->search_breadth_first = false;
13068				n->search_seq_column = $7;
13069				n->location = @1;
13070				$$ = (Node *) n;
13071			}
13072		| SEARCH BREADTH FIRST_P BY columnList SET ColId
13073			{
13074				CTESearchClause *n = makeNode(CTESearchClause);
13075
13076				n->search_col_list = $5;
13077				n->search_breadth_first = true;
13078				n->search_seq_column = $7;
13079				n->location = @1;
13080				$$ = (Node *) n;
13081			}
13082		| /*EMPTY*/
13083			{
13084				$$ = NULL;
13085			}
13086		;
13087
13088opt_cycle_clause:
13089		CYCLE columnList SET ColId TO AexprConst DEFAULT AexprConst USING ColId
13090			{
13091				CTECycleClause *n = makeNode(CTECycleClause);
13092
13093				n->cycle_col_list = $2;
13094				n->cycle_mark_column = $4;
13095				n->cycle_mark_value = $6;
13096				n->cycle_mark_default = $8;
13097				n->cycle_path_column = $10;
13098				n->location = @1;
13099				$$ = (Node *) n;
13100			}
13101		| CYCLE columnList SET ColId USING ColId
13102			{
13103				CTECycleClause *n = makeNode(CTECycleClause);
13104
13105				n->cycle_col_list = $2;
13106				n->cycle_mark_column = $4;
13107				n->cycle_mark_value = makeBoolAConst(true, -1);
13108				n->cycle_mark_default = makeBoolAConst(false, -1);
13109				n->cycle_path_column = $6;
13110				n->location = @1;
13111				$$ = (Node *) n;
13112			}
13113		| /*EMPTY*/
13114			{
13115				$$ = NULL;
13116			}
13117		;
13118
13119opt_with_clause:
13120		with_clause								{ $$ = $1; }
13121		| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
13122		;
13123
13124into_clause:
13125			INTO OptTempTableName
13126				{
13127					$$ = makeNode(IntoClause);
13128					$$->rel = $2;
13129					$$->colNames = NIL;
13130					$$->options = NIL;
13131					$$->onCommit = ONCOMMIT_NOOP;
13132					$$->tableSpaceName = NULL;
13133					$$->viewQuery = NULL;
13134					$$->skipData = false;
13135				}
13136			| /*EMPTY*/
13137				{ $$ = NULL; }
13138		;
13139
13140/*
13141 * Redundancy here is needed to avoid shift/reduce conflicts,
13142 * since TEMP is not a reserved word.  See also OptTemp.
13143 */
13144OptTempTableName:
13145			TEMPORARY opt_table qualified_name
13146				{
13147					$$ = $3;
13148					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
13149				}
13150			| TEMP opt_table qualified_name
13151				{
13152					$$ = $3;
13153					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
13154				}
13155			| LOCAL TEMPORARY opt_table qualified_name
13156				{
13157					$$ = $4;
13158					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
13159				}
13160			| LOCAL TEMP opt_table qualified_name
13161				{
13162					$$ = $4;
13163					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
13164				}
13165			| GLOBAL TEMPORARY opt_table qualified_name
13166				{
13167					ereport(WARNING,
13168							(errmsg("GLOBAL is deprecated in temporary table creation"),
13169							 parser_errposition(@1)));
13170					$$ = $4;
13171					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
13172				}
13173			| GLOBAL TEMP opt_table qualified_name
13174				{
13175					ereport(WARNING,
13176							(errmsg("GLOBAL is deprecated in temporary table creation"),
13177							 parser_errposition(@1)));
13178					$$ = $4;
13179					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
13180				}
13181			| UNLOGGED opt_table qualified_name
13182				{
13183					$$ = $3;
13184					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_UNLOGGED;
13185				}
13186			| TABLE qualified_name
13187				{
13188					$$ = $2;
13189					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT;
13190				}
13191			| qualified_name
13192				{
13193					$$ = $1;
13194					$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT;
13195				}
13196		;
13197
13198opt_table:	TABLE
13199			| /*EMPTY*/
13200		;
13201
13202set_quantifier:
13203			ALL										{ $$ = SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL; }
13204			| DISTINCT								{ $$ = SET_QUANTIFIER_DISTINCT; }
13205			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = SET_QUANTIFIER_DEFAULT; }
13206		;
13207
13208/* We use (NIL) as a placeholder to indicate that all target expressions
13209 * should be placed in the DISTINCT list during parsetree analysis.
13210 */
13211distinct_clause:
13212			DISTINCT								{ $$ = list_make1(NIL); }
13213			| DISTINCT ON '(' expr_list ')'			{ $$ = $4; }
13214		;
13215
13216opt_all_clause:
13217			ALL
13218			| /*EMPTY*/
13219		;
13220
13221opt_distinct_clause:
13222			distinct_clause							{ $$ = $1; }
13223			| opt_all_clause						{ $$ = NIL; }
13224		;
13225
13226opt_sort_clause:
13227			sort_clause								{ $$ = $1; }
13228			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
13229		;
13230
13231sort_clause:
13232			ORDER BY sortby_list					{ $$ = $3; }
13233		;
13234
13235sortby_list:
13236			sortby									{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
13237			| sortby_list ',' sortby				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
13238		;
13239
13240sortby:		a_expr USING qual_all_Op opt_nulls_order
13241				{
13242					$$ = makeNode(SortBy);
13243					$$->node = $1;
13244					$$->sortby_dir = SORTBY_USING;
13245					$$->sortby_nulls = $4;
13246					$$->useOp = $3;
13247					$$->location = @3;
13248				}
13249			| a_expr opt_asc_desc opt_nulls_order
13250				{
13251					$$ = makeNode(SortBy);
13252					$$->node = $1;
13253					$$->sortby_dir = $2;
13254					$$->sortby_nulls = $3;
13255					$$->useOp = NIL;
13256					$$->location = -1;		/* no operator */
13257				}
13258		;
13259
13260
13261select_limit:
13262			limit_clause offset_clause
13263				{
13264					$$ = $1;
13265					($$)->limitOffset = $2;
13266					($$)->offsetLoc = @2;
13267				}
13268			| offset_clause limit_clause
13269				{
13270					$$ = $2;
13271					($$)->limitOffset = $1;
13272					($$)->offsetLoc = @1;
13273				}
13274			| limit_clause
13275				{
13276					$$ = $1;
13277				}
13278			| offset_clause
13279				{
13280					SelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));
13281
13282					n->limitOffset = $1;
13283					n->limitCount = NULL;
13284					n->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;
13285					n->offsetLoc = @1;
13286					n->countLoc = -1;
13287					n->optionLoc = -1;
13288					$$ = n;
13289				}
13290		;
13291
13292opt_select_limit:
13293			select_limit						{ $$ = $1; }
13294			| /* EMPTY */						{ $$ = NULL; }
13295		;
13296
13297limit_clause:
13298			LIMIT select_limit_value
13299				{
13300					SelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));
13301
13302					n->limitOffset = NULL;
13303					n->limitCount = $2;
13304					n->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;
13305					n->offsetLoc = -1;
13306					n->countLoc = @1;
13307					n->optionLoc = -1;
13308					$$ = n;
13309				}
13310			| LIMIT select_limit_value ',' select_offset_value
13311				{
13312					/* Disabled because it was too confusing, bjm 2002-02-18 */
13313					ereport(ERROR,
13314							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
13315							 errmsg("LIMIT #,# syntax is not supported"),
13316							 errhint("Use separate LIMIT and OFFSET clauses."),
13317							 parser_errposition(@1)));
13318				}
13319			/* SQL:2008 syntax */
13320			/* to avoid shift/reduce conflicts, handle the optional value with
13321			 * a separate production rather than an opt_ expression.  The fact
13322			 * that ONLY is fully reserved means that this way, we defer any
13323			 * decision about what rule reduces ROW or ROWS to the point where
13324			 * we can see the ONLY token in the lookahead slot.
13325			 */
13326			| FETCH first_or_next select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows ONLY
13327				{
13328					SelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));
13329
13330					n->limitOffset = NULL;
13331					n->limitCount = $3;
13332					n->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;
13333					n->offsetLoc = -1;
13334					n->countLoc = @1;
13335					n->optionLoc = -1;
13336					$$ = n;
13337				}
13338			| FETCH first_or_next select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows WITH TIES
13339				{
13340					SelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));
13341
13342					n->limitOffset = NULL;
13343					n->limitCount = $3;
13344					n->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES;
13345					n->offsetLoc = -1;
13346					n->countLoc = @1;
13347					n->optionLoc = @5;
13348					$$ = n;
13349				}
13350			| FETCH first_or_next row_or_rows ONLY
13351				{
13352					SelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));
13353
13354					n->limitOffset = NULL;
13355					n->limitCount = makeIntConst(1, -1);
13356					n->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;
13357					n->offsetLoc = -1;
13358					n->countLoc = @1;
13359					n->optionLoc = -1;
13360					$$ = n;
13361				}
13362			| FETCH first_or_next row_or_rows WITH TIES
13363				{
13364					SelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));
13365
13366					n->limitOffset = NULL;
13367					n->limitCount = makeIntConst(1, -1);
13368					n->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES;
13369					n->offsetLoc = -1;
13370					n->countLoc = @1;
13371					n->optionLoc = @4;
13372					$$ = n;
13373				}
13374		;
13375
13376offset_clause:
13377			OFFSET select_offset_value
13378				{ $$ = $2; }
13379			/* SQL:2008 syntax */
13380			| OFFSET select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows
13381				{ $$ = $2; }
13382		;
13383
13384select_limit_value:
13385			a_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
13386			| ALL
13387				{
13388					/* LIMIT ALL is represented as a NULL constant */
13389					$$ = makeNullAConst(@1);
13390				}
13391		;
13392
13393select_offset_value:
13394			a_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
13395		;
13396
13397/*
13398 * Allowing full expressions without parentheses causes various parsing
13399 * problems with the trailing ROW/ROWS key words.  SQL spec only calls for
13400 * <simple value specification>, which is either a literal or a parameter (but
13401 * an <SQL parameter reference> could be an identifier, bringing up conflicts
13402 * with ROW/ROWS). We solve this by leveraging the presence of ONLY (see above)
13403 * to determine whether the expression is missing rather than trying to make it
13404 * optional in this rule.
13405 *
13406 * c_expr covers almost all the spec-required cases (and more), but it doesn't
13407 * cover signed numeric literals, which are allowed by the spec. So we include
13408 * those here explicitly. We need FCONST as well as ICONST because values that
13409 * don't fit in the platform's "long", but do fit in bigint, should still be
13410 * accepted here. (This is possible in 64-bit Windows as well as all 32-bit
13411 * builds.)
13412 */
13413select_fetch_first_value:
13414			c_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
13415			| '+' I_or_F_const
13416				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", NULL, $2, @1); }
13417			| '-' I_or_F_const
13418				{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }
13419		;
13420
13421I_or_F_const:
13422			Iconst									{ $$ = makeIntConst($1,@1); }
13423			| FCONST								{ $$ = makeFloatConst($1,@1); }
13424		;
13425
13426/* noise words */
13427row_or_rows: ROW									{ $$ = 0; }
13428			| ROWS									{ $$ = 0; }
13429		;
13430
13431first_or_next: FIRST_P								{ $$ = 0; }
13432			| NEXT									{ $$ = 0; }
13433		;
13434
13435
13436/*
13437 * This syntax for group_clause tries to follow the spec quite closely.
13438 * However, the spec allows only column references, not expressions,
13439 * which introduces an ambiguity between implicit row constructors
13440 * (a,b) and lists of column references.
13441 *
13442 * We handle this by using the a_expr production for what the spec calls
13443 * <ordinary grouping set>, which in the spec represents either one column
13444 * reference or a parenthesized list of column references. Then, we check the
13445 * top node of the a_expr to see if it's an implicit RowExpr, and if so, just
13446 * grab and use the list, discarding the node. (this is done in parse analysis,
13447 * not here)
13448 *
13449 * (we abuse the row_format field of RowExpr to distinguish implicit and
13450 * explicit row constructors; it's debatable if anyone sanely wants to use them
13451 * in a group clause, but if they have a reason to, we make it possible.)
13452 *
13453 * Each item in the group_clause list is either an expression tree or a
13454 * GroupingSet node of some type.
13455 */
13456group_clause:
13457			GROUP_P BY set_quantifier group_by_list
13458				{
13459					GroupClause *n = (GroupClause *) palloc(sizeof(GroupClause));
13460
13461					n->distinct = $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_DISTINCT;
13462					n->list = $4;
13463					$$ = n;
13464				}
13465			| /*EMPTY*/
13466				{
13467					GroupClause *n = (GroupClause *) palloc(sizeof(GroupClause));
13468
13469					n->distinct = false;
13470					n->list = NIL;
13471					$$ = n;
13472				}
13473		;
13474
13475group_by_list:
13476			group_by_item							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
13477			| group_by_list ',' group_by_item		{ $$ = lappend($1,$3); }
13478		;
13479
13480group_by_item:
13481			a_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
13482			| empty_grouping_set					{ $$ = $1; }
13483			| cube_clause							{ $$ = $1; }
13484			| rollup_clause							{ $$ = $1; }
13485			| grouping_sets_clause					{ $$ = $1; }
13486		;
13487
13488empty_grouping_set:
13489			'(' ')'
13490				{
13491					$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_EMPTY, NIL, @1);
13492				}
13493		;
13494
13495/*
13496 * These hacks rely on setting precedence of CUBE and ROLLUP below that of '(',
13497 * so that they shift in these rules rather than reducing the conflicting
13498 * unreserved_keyword rule.
13499 */
13500
13501rollup_clause:
13502			ROLLUP '(' expr_list ')'
13503				{
13504					$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_ROLLUP, $3, @1);
13505				}
13506		;
13507
13508cube_clause:
13509			CUBE '(' expr_list ')'
13510				{
13511					$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_CUBE, $3, @1);
13512				}
13513		;
13514
13515grouping_sets_clause:
13516			GROUPING SETS '(' group_by_list ')'
13517				{
13518					$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_SETS, $4, @1);
13519				}
13520		;
13521
13522having_clause:
13523			HAVING a_expr							{ $$ = $2; }
13524			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
13525		;
13526
13527for_locking_clause:
13528			for_locking_items						{ $$ = $1; }
13529			| FOR READ ONLY							{ $$ = NIL; }
13530		;
13531
13532opt_for_locking_clause:
13533			for_locking_clause						{ $$ = $1; }
13534			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NIL; }
13535		;
13536
13537for_locking_items:
13538			for_locking_item						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
13539			| for_locking_items for_locking_item	{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
13540		;
13541
13542for_locking_item:
13543			for_locking_strength locked_rels_list opt_nowait_or_skip
13544				{
13545					LockingClause *n = makeNode(LockingClause);
13546
13547					n->lockedRels = $2;
13548					n->strength = $1;
13549					n->waitPolicy = $3;
13550					$$ = (Node *) n;
13551				}
13552		;
13553
13554for_locking_strength:
13555			FOR UPDATE							{ $$ = LCS_FORUPDATE; }
13556			| FOR NO KEY UPDATE					{ $$ = LCS_FORNOKEYUPDATE; }
13557			| FOR SHARE							{ $$ = LCS_FORSHARE; }
13558			| FOR KEY SHARE						{ $$ = LCS_FORKEYSHARE; }
13559		;
13560
13561locked_rels_list:
13562			OF qualified_name_list					{ $$ = $2; }
13563			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NIL; }
13564		;
13565
13566
13567/*
13568 * We should allow ROW '(' expr_list ')' too, but that seems to require
13569 * making VALUES a fully reserved word, which will probably break more apps
13570 * than allowing the noise-word is worth.
13571 */
13572values_clause:
13573			VALUES '(' expr_list ')'
13574				{
13575					SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
13576
13577					n->valuesLists = list_make1($3);
13578					$$ = (Node *) n;
13579				}
13580			| values_clause ',' '(' expr_list ')'
13581				{
13582					SelectStmt *n = (SelectStmt *) $1;
13583
13584					n->valuesLists = lappend(n->valuesLists, $4);
13585					$$ = (Node *) n;
13586				}
13587		;
13588
13589
13590/*****************************************************************************
13591 *
13592 *	clauses common to all Optimizable Stmts:
13593 *		from_clause		- allow list of both JOIN expressions and table names
13594 *		where_clause	- qualifications for joins or restrictions
13595 *
13596 *****************************************************************************/
13597
13598from_clause:
13599			FROM from_list							{ $$ = $2; }
13600			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
13601		;
13602
13603from_list:
13604			table_ref								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
13605			| from_list ',' table_ref				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
13606		;
13607
13608/*
13609 * table_ref is where an alias clause can be attached.
13610 */
13611table_ref:	relation_expr opt_alias_clause
13612				{
13613					$1->alias = $2;
13614					$$ = (Node *) $1;
13615				}
13616			| relation_expr opt_alias_clause tablesample_clause
13617				{
13618					RangeTableSample *n = (RangeTableSample *) $3;
13619
13620					$1->alias = $2;
13621					/* relation_expr goes inside the RangeTableSample node */
13622					n->relation = (Node *) $1;
13623					$$ = (Node *) n;
13624				}
13625			| func_table func_alias_clause
13626				{
13627					RangeFunction *n = (RangeFunction *) $1;
13628
13629					n->alias = linitial($2);
13630					n->coldeflist = lsecond($2);
13631					$$ = (Node *) n;
13632				}
13633			| LATERAL_P func_table func_alias_clause
13634				{
13635					RangeFunction *n = (RangeFunction *) $2;
13636
13637					n->lateral = true;
13638					n->alias = linitial($3);
13639					n->coldeflist = lsecond($3);
13640					$$ = (Node *) n;
13641				}
13642			| xmltable opt_alias_clause
13643				{
13644					RangeTableFunc *n = (RangeTableFunc *) $1;
13645
13646					n->alias = $2;
13647					$$ = (Node *) n;
13648				}
13649			| LATERAL_P xmltable opt_alias_clause
13650				{
13651					RangeTableFunc *n = (RangeTableFunc *) $2;
13652
13653					n->lateral = true;
13654					n->alias = $3;
13655					$$ = (Node *) n;
13656				}
13657			| select_with_parens opt_alias_clause
13658				{
13659					RangeSubselect *n = makeNode(RangeSubselect);
13660
13661					n->lateral = false;
13662					n->subquery = $1;
13663					n->alias = $2;
13664					$$ = (Node *) n;
13665				}
13666			| LATERAL_P select_with_parens opt_alias_clause
13667				{
13668					RangeSubselect *n = makeNode(RangeSubselect);
13669
13670					n->lateral = true;
13671					n->subquery = $2;
13672					n->alias = $3;
13673					$$ = (Node *) n;
13674				}
13675			| joined_table
13676				{
13677					$$ = (Node *) $1;
13678				}
13679			| '(' joined_table ')' alias_clause
13680				{
13681					$2->alias = $4;
13682					$$ = (Node *) $2;
13683				}
13684			| json_table opt_alias_clause
13685				{
13686					JsonTable  *jt = castNode(JsonTable, $1);
13687
13688					jt->alias = $2;
13689					$$ = (Node *) jt;
13690				}
13691			| LATERAL_P json_table opt_alias_clause
13692				{
13693					JsonTable  *jt = castNode(JsonTable, $2);
13694
13695					jt->alias = $3;
13696					jt->lateral = true;
13697					$$ = (Node *) jt;
13698				}
13699		;
13700
13701
13702/*
13703 * It may seem silly to separate joined_table from table_ref, but there is
13704 * method in SQL's madness: if you don't do it this way you get reduce-
13705 * reduce conflicts, because it's not clear to the parser generator whether
13706 * to expect alias_clause after ')' or not.  For the same reason we must
13707 * treat 'JOIN' and 'join_type JOIN' separately, rather than allowing
13708 * join_type to expand to empty; if we try it, the parser generator can't
13709 * figure out when to reduce an empty join_type right after table_ref.
13710 *
13711 * Note that a CROSS JOIN is the same as an unqualified
13712 * INNER JOIN, and an INNER JOIN/ON has the same shape
13713 * but a qualification expression to limit membership.
13714 * A NATURAL JOIN implicitly matches column names between
13715 * tables and the shape is determined by which columns are
13716 * in common. We'll collect columns during the later transformations.
13717 */
13718
13719joined_table:
13720			'(' joined_table ')'
13721				{
13722					$$ = $2;
13723				}
13724			| table_ref CROSS JOIN table_ref
13725				{
13726					/* CROSS JOIN is same as unqualified inner join */
13727					JoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
13728
13729					n->jointype = JOIN_INNER;
13730					n->isNatural = false;
13731					n->larg = $1;
13732					n->rarg = $4;
13733					n->usingClause = NIL;
13734					n->join_using_alias = NULL;
13735					n->quals = NULL;
13736					$$ = n;
13737				}
13738			| table_ref join_type JOIN table_ref join_qual
13739				{
13740					JoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
13741
13742					n->jointype = $2;
13743					n->isNatural = false;
13744					n->larg = $1;
13745					n->rarg = $4;
13746					if ($5 != NULL && IsA($5, List))
13747					{
13748						 /* USING clause */
13749						n->usingClause = linitial_node(List, castNode(List, $5));
13750						n->join_using_alias = lsecond_node(Alias, castNode(List, $5));
13751					}
13752					else
13753					{
13754						/* ON clause */
13755						n->quals = $5;
13756					}
13757					$$ = n;
13758				}
13759			| table_ref JOIN table_ref join_qual
13760				{
13761					/* letting join_type reduce to empty doesn't work */
13762					JoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
13763
13764					n->jointype = JOIN_INNER;
13765					n->isNatural = false;
13766					n->larg = $1;
13767					n->rarg = $3;
13768					if ($4 != NULL && IsA($4, List))
13769					{
13770						/* USING clause */
13771						n->usingClause = linitial_node(List, castNode(List, $4));
13772						n->join_using_alias = lsecond_node(Alias, castNode(List, $4));
13773					}
13774					else
13775					{
13776						/* ON clause */
13777						n->quals = $4;
13778					}
13779					$$ = n;
13780				}
13781			| table_ref NATURAL join_type JOIN table_ref
13782				{
13783					JoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
13784
13785					n->jointype = $3;
13786					n->isNatural = true;
13787					n->larg = $1;
13788					n->rarg = $5;
13789					n->usingClause = NIL; /* figure out which columns later... */
13790					n->join_using_alias = NULL;
13791					n->quals = NULL; /* fill later */
13792					$$ = n;
13793				}
13794			| table_ref NATURAL JOIN table_ref
13795				{
13796					/* letting join_type reduce to empty doesn't work */
13797					JoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
13798
13799					n->jointype = JOIN_INNER;
13800					n->isNatural = true;
13801					n->larg = $1;
13802					n->rarg = $4;
13803					n->usingClause = NIL; /* figure out which columns later... */
13804					n->join_using_alias = NULL;
13805					n->quals = NULL; /* fill later */
13806					$$ = n;
13807				}
13808		;
13809
13810alias_clause:
13811			AS ColId '(' name_list ')'
13812				{
13813					$$ = makeNode(Alias);
13814					$$->aliasname = $2;
13815					$$->colnames = $4;
13816				}
13817			| AS ColId
13818				{
13819					$$ = makeNode(Alias);
13820					$$->aliasname = $2;
13821				}
13822			| ColId '(' name_list ')'
13823				{
13824					$$ = makeNode(Alias);
13825					$$->aliasname = $1;
13826					$$->colnames = $3;
13827				}
13828			| ColId
13829				{
13830					$$ = makeNode(Alias);
13831					$$->aliasname = $1;
13832				}
13833		;
13834
13835opt_alias_clause: alias_clause						{ $$ = $1; }
13836			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
13837		;
13838
13839/*
13840 * The alias clause after JOIN ... USING only accepts the AS ColId spelling,
13841 * per SQL standard.  (The grammar could parse the other variants, but they
13842 * don't seem to be useful, and it might lead to parser problems in the
13843 * future.)
13844 */
13845opt_alias_clause_for_join_using:
13846			AS ColId
13847				{
13848					$$ = makeNode(Alias);
13849					$$->aliasname = $2;
13850					/* the column name list will be inserted later */
13851				}
13852			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
13853		;
13854
13855/*
13856 * func_alias_clause can include both an Alias and a coldeflist, so we make it
13857 * return a 2-element list that gets disassembled by calling production.
13858 */
13859func_alias_clause:
13860			alias_clause
13861				{
13862					$$ = list_make2($1, NIL);
13863				}
13864			| AS '(' TableFuncElementList ')'
13865				{
13866					$$ = list_make2(NULL, $3);
13867				}
13868			| AS ColId '(' TableFuncElementList ')'
13869				{
13870					Alias	   *a = makeNode(Alias);
13871
13872					a->aliasname = $2;
13873					$$ = list_make2(a, $4);
13874				}
13875			| ColId '(' TableFuncElementList ')'
13876				{
13877					Alias	   *a = makeNode(Alias);
13878
13879					a->aliasname = $1;
13880					$$ = list_make2(a, $3);
13881				}
13882			| /*EMPTY*/
13883				{
13884					$$ = list_make2(NULL, NIL);
13885				}
13886		;
13887
13888join_type:	FULL opt_outer							{ $$ = JOIN_FULL; }
13889			| LEFT opt_outer						{ $$ = JOIN_LEFT; }
13890			| RIGHT opt_outer						{ $$ = JOIN_RIGHT; }
13891			| INNER_P								{ $$ = JOIN_INNER; }
13892		;
13893
13894/* OUTER is just noise... */
13895opt_outer: OUTER_P
13896			| /*EMPTY*/
13897		;
13898
13899/* JOIN qualification clauses
13900 * Possibilities are:
13901 *	USING ( column list ) [ AS alias ]
13902 *						  allows only unqualified column names,
13903 *						  which must match between tables.
13904 *	ON expr allows more general qualifications.
13905 *
13906 * We return USING as a two-element List (the first item being a sub-List
13907 * of the common column names, and the second either an Alias item or NULL).
13908 * An ON-expr will not be a List, so it can be told apart that way.
13909 */
13910
13911join_qual: USING '(' name_list ')' opt_alias_clause_for_join_using
13912				{
13913					$$ = (Node *) list_make2($3, $5);
13914				}
13915			| ON a_expr
13916				{
13917					$$ = $2;
13918				}
13919		;
13920
13921
13922relation_expr:
13923			qualified_name
13924				{
13925					/* inheritance query, implicitly */
13926					$$ = $1;
13927					$$->inh = true;
13928					$$->alias = NULL;
13929				}
13930			| extended_relation_expr
13931				{
13932					$$ = $1;
13933				}
13934		;
13935
13936extended_relation_expr:
13937			qualified_name '*'
13938				{
13939					/* inheritance query, explicitly */
13940					$$ = $1;
13941					$$->inh = true;
13942					$$->alias = NULL;
13943				}
13944			| ONLY qualified_name
13945				{
13946					/* no inheritance */
13947					$$ = $2;
13948					$$->inh = false;
13949					$$->alias = NULL;
13950				}
13951			| ONLY '(' qualified_name ')'
13952				{
13953					/* no inheritance, SQL99-style syntax */
13954					$$ = $3;
13955					$$->inh = false;
13956					$$->alias = NULL;
13957				}
13958		;
13959
13960
13961relation_expr_list:
13962			relation_expr							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
13963			| relation_expr_list ',' relation_expr	{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
13964		;
13965
13966
13967/*
13968 * Given "UPDATE foo set set ...", we have to decide without looking any
13969 * further ahead whether the first "set" is an alias or the UPDATE's SET
13970 * keyword.  Since "set" is allowed as a column name both interpretations
13971 * are feasible.  We resolve the shift/reduce conflict by giving the first
13972 * relation_expr_opt_alias production a higher precedence than the SET token
13973 * has, causing the parser to prefer to reduce, in effect assuming that the
13974 * SET is not an alias.
13975 */
13976relation_expr_opt_alias: relation_expr					%prec UMINUS
13977				{
13978					$$ = $1;
13979				}
13980			| relation_expr ColId
13981				{
13982					Alias	   *alias = makeNode(Alias);
13983
13984					alias->aliasname = $2;
13985					$1->alias = alias;
13986					$$ = $1;
13987				}
13988			| relation_expr AS ColId
13989				{
13990					Alias	   *alias = makeNode(Alias);
13991
13992					alias->aliasname = $3;
13993					$1->alias = alias;
13994					$$ = $1;
13995				}
13996		;
13997
13998/*
13999 * TABLESAMPLE decoration in a FROM item
14000 */
14001tablesample_clause:
14002			TABLESAMPLE func_name '(' expr_list ')' opt_repeatable_clause
14003				{
14004					RangeTableSample *n = makeNode(RangeTableSample);
14005
14006					/* n->relation will be filled in later */
14007					n->method = $2;
14008					n->args = $4;
14009					n->repeatable = $6;
14010					n->location = @2;
14011					$$ = (Node *) n;
14012				}
14013		;
14014
14015opt_repeatable_clause:
14016			REPEATABLE '(' a_expr ')'	{ $$ = (Node *) $3; }
14017			| /*EMPTY*/					{ $$ = NULL; }
14018		;
14019
14020/*
14021 * func_table represents a function invocation in a FROM list. It can be
14022 * a plain function call, like "foo(...)", or a ROWS FROM expression with
14023 * one or more function calls, "ROWS FROM (foo(...), bar(...))",
14024 * optionally with WITH ORDINALITY attached.
14025 * In the ROWS FROM syntax, a column definition list can be given for each
14026 * function, for example:
14027 *     ROWS FROM (foo() AS (foo_res_a text, foo_res_b text),
14028 *                bar() AS (bar_res_a text, bar_res_b text))
14029 * It's also possible to attach a column definition list to the RangeFunction
14030 * as a whole, but that's handled by the table_ref production.
14031 */
14032func_table: func_expr_windowless opt_ordinality
14033				{
14034					RangeFunction *n = makeNode(RangeFunction);
14035
14036					n->lateral = false;
14037					n->ordinality = $2;
14038					n->is_rowsfrom = false;
14039					n->functions = list_make1(list_make2($1, NIL));
14040					/* alias and coldeflist are set by table_ref production */
14041					$$ = (Node *) n;
14042				}
14043			| ROWS FROM '(' rowsfrom_list ')' opt_ordinality
14044				{
14045					RangeFunction *n = makeNode(RangeFunction);
14046
14047					n->lateral = false;
14048					n->ordinality = $6;
14049					n->is_rowsfrom = true;
14050					n->functions = $4;
14051					/* alias and coldeflist are set by table_ref production */
14052					$$ = (Node *) n;
14053				}
14054		;
14055
14056rowsfrom_item: func_expr_windowless opt_col_def_list
14057				{ $$ = list_make2($1, $2); }
14058		;
14059
14060rowsfrom_list:
14061			rowsfrom_item						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
14062			| rowsfrom_list ',' rowsfrom_item	{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
14063		;
14064
14065opt_col_def_list: AS '(' TableFuncElementList ')'	{ $$ = $3; }
14066			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
14067		;
14068
14069opt_ordinality: WITH_LA ORDINALITY					{ $$ = true; }
14070			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
14071		;
14072
14073
14074where_clause:
14075			WHERE a_expr							{ $$ = $2; }
14076			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
14077		;
14078
14079/* variant for UPDATE and DELETE */
14080where_or_current_clause:
14081			WHERE a_expr							{ $$ = $2; }
14082			| WHERE CURRENT_P OF cursor_name
14083				{
14084					CurrentOfExpr *n = makeNode(CurrentOfExpr);
14085
14086					/* cvarno is filled in by parse analysis */
14087					n->cursor_name = $4;
14088					n->cursor_param = 0;
14089					$$ = (Node *) n;
14090				}
14091			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
14092		;
14093
14094
14095OptTableFuncElementList:
14096			TableFuncElementList				{ $$ = $1; }
14097			| /*EMPTY*/							{ $$ = NIL; }
14098		;
14099
14100TableFuncElementList:
14101			TableFuncElement
14102				{
14103					$$ = list_make1($1);
14104				}
14105			| TableFuncElementList ',' TableFuncElement
14106				{
14107					$$ = lappend($1, $3);
14108				}
14109		;
14110
14111TableFuncElement:	ColId Typename opt_collate_clause
14112				{
14113					ColumnDef *n = makeNode(ColumnDef);
14114
14115					n->colname = $1;
14116					n->typeName = $2;
14117					n->inhcount = 0;
14118					n->is_local = true;
14119					n->is_not_null = false;
14120					n->is_from_type = false;
14121					n->storage = 0;
14122					n->raw_default = NULL;
14123					n->cooked_default = NULL;
14124					n->collClause = (CollateClause *) $3;
14125					n->collOid = InvalidOid;
14126					n->constraints = NIL;
14127					n->location = @1;
14128					$$ = (Node *) n;
14129				}
14130		;
14131
14132/*
14133 * XMLTABLE
14134 */
14135xmltable:
14136			XMLTABLE '(' c_expr xmlexists_argument COLUMNS xmltable_column_list ')'
14137				{
14138					RangeTableFunc *n = makeNode(RangeTableFunc);
14139
14140					n->rowexpr = $3;
14141					n->docexpr = $4;
14142					n->columns = $6;
14143					n->namespaces = NIL;
14144					n->location = @1;
14145					$$ = (Node *) n;
14146				}
14147			| XMLTABLE '(' XMLNAMESPACES '(' xml_namespace_list ')' ','
14148				c_expr xmlexists_argument COLUMNS xmltable_column_list ')'
14149				{
14150					RangeTableFunc *n = makeNode(RangeTableFunc);
14151
14152					n->rowexpr = $8;
14153					n->docexpr = $9;
14154					n->columns = $11;
