14155					n->namespaces = $5;
14156					n->location = @1;
14157					$$ = (Node *) n;
14158				}
14159		;
14160
14161xmltable_column_list: xmltable_column_el					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
14162			| xmltable_column_list ',' xmltable_column_el	{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
14163		;
14164
14165xmltable_column_el:
14166			ColId Typename
14167				{
14168					RangeTableFuncCol *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);
14169
14170					fc->colname = $1;
14171					fc->for_ordinality = false;
14172					fc->typeName = $2;
14173					fc->is_not_null = false;
14174					fc->colexpr = NULL;
14175					fc->coldefexpr = NULL;
14176					fc->location = @1;
14177
14178					$$ = (Node *) fc;
14179				}
14180			| ColId Typename xmltable_column_option_list
14181				{
14182					RangeTableFuncCol *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);
14183					ListCell   *option;
14184					bool		nullability_seen = false;
14185
14186					fc->colname = $1;
14187					fc->typeName = $2;
14188					fc->for_ordinality = false;
14189					fc->is_not_null = false;
14190					fc->colexpr = NULL;
14191					fc->coldefexpr = NULL;
14192					fc->location = @1;
14193
14194					foreach(option, $3)
14195					{
14196						DefElem   *defel = (DefElem *) lfirst(option);
14197
14198						if (strcmp(defel->defname, "default") == 0)
14199						{
14200							if (fc->coldefexpr != NULL)
14201								ereport(ERROR,
14202										(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
14203										 errmsg("only one DEFAULT value is allowed"),
14204										 parser_errposition(defel->location)));
14205							fc->coldefexpr = defel->arg;
14206						}
14207						else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "path") == 0)
14208						{
14209							if (fc->colexpr != NULL)
14210								ereport(ERROR,
14211										(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
14212										 errmsg("only one PATH value per column is allowed"),
14213										 parser_errposition(defel->location)));
14214							fc->colexpr = defel->arg;
14215						}
14216						else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "__pg__is_not_null") == 0)
14217						{
14218							if (nullability_seen)
14219								ereport(ERROR,
14220										(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
14221										 errmsg("conflicting or redundant NULL / NOT NULL declarations for column \"%s\"", fc->colname),
14222										 parser_errposition(defel->location)));
14223							fc->is_not_null = boolVal(defel->arg);
14224							nullability_seen = true;
14225						}
14226						else
14227						{
14228							ereport(ERROR,
14229									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
14230									 errmsg("unrecognized column option \"%s\"",
14231											defel->defname),
14232									 parser_errposition(defel->location)));
14233						}
14234					}
14235					$$ = (Node *) fc;
14236				}
14237			| ColId FOR ORDINALITY
14238				{
14239					RangeTableFuncCol *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);
14240
14241					fc->colname = $1;
14242					fc->for_ordinality = true;
14243					/* other fields are ignored, initialized by makeNode */
14244					fc->location = @1;
14245
14246					$$ = (Node *) fc;
14247				}
14248		;
14249
14250xmltable_column_option_list:
14251			xmltable_column_option_el
14252				{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
14253			| xmltable_column_option_list xmltable_column_option_el
14254				{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
14255		;
14256
14257xmltable_column_option_el:
14258			IDENT b_expr
14259				{
14260					if (strcmp($1, "__pg__is_not_null") == 0)
14261						ereport(ERROR,
14262								(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
14263								 errmsg("option name \"%s\" cannot be used in XMLTABLE", $1),
14264								 parser_errposition(@1)));
14265					$$ = makeDefElem($1, $2, @1);
14266				}
14267			| DEFAULT b_expr
14268				{ $$ = makeDefElem("default", $2, @1); }
14269			| NOT NULL_P
14270				{ $$ = makeDefElem("__pg__is_not_null", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1); }
14271			| NULL_P
14272				{ $$ = makeDefElem("__pg__is_not_null", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1); }
14273			| PATH b_expr
14274				{ $$ = makeDefElem("path", $2, @1); }
14275		;
14276
14277xml_namespace_list:
14278			xml_namespace_el
14279				{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
14280			| xml_namespace_list ',' xml_namespace_el
14281				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
14282		;
14283
14284xml_namespace_el:
14285			b_expr AS ColLabel
14286				{
14287					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
14288					$$->name = $3;
14289					$$->indirection = NIL;
14290					$$->val = $1;
14291					$$->location = @1;
14292				}
14293			| DEFAULT b_expr
14294				{
14295					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
14296					$$->name = NULL;
14297					$$->indirection = NIL;
14298					$$->val = $2;
14299					$$->location = @1;
14300				}
14301		;
14302
14303json_table:
14304			JSON_TABLE '('
14305				json_value_expr ',' a_expr json_table_path_name_opt
14306				json_passing_clause_opt
14307				COLUMNS '(' json_table_column_definition_list ')'
14308				json_on_error_clause_opt
14309			')'
14310				{
14311					JsonTable *n = makeNode(JsonTable);
14312					char	  *pathstring;
14313
14314					n->context_item = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
14315					if (!IsA($5, A_Const) ||
14316						castNode(A_Const, $5)->val.node.type != T_String)
14317						ereport(ERROR,
14318								errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
14319								errmsg("only string constants are supported in JSON_TABLE path specification"),
14320								parser_errposition(@5));
14321					pathstring = castNode(A_Const, $5)->val.sval.sval;
14322					n->pathspec = makeJsonTablePathSpec(pathstring, $6, @5, @6);
14323					n->passing = $7;
14324					n->columns = $10;
14325					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) $12;
14326					n->location = @1;
14327					$$ = (Node *) n;
14328				}
14329		;
14330
14331json_table_path_name_opt:
14332			AS name			{ $$ = $2; }
14333			| /* empty */	{ $$ = NULL; }
14334		;
14335
14336json_table_column_definition_list:
14337			json_table_column_definition
14338				{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
14339			| json_table_column_definition_list ',' json_table_column_definition
14340				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
14341		;
14342
14343json_table_column_definition:
14344			ColId FOR ORDINALITY
14345				{
14346					JsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);
14347
14348					n->coltype = JTC_FOR_ORDINALITY;
14349					n->name = $1;
14350					n->location = @1;
14351					$$ = (Node *) n;
14352				}
14353			| ColId Typename
14354				json_table_column_path_clause_opt
14355				json_wrapper_behavior
14356				json_quotes_clause_opt
14357				json_behavior_clause_opt
14358				{
14359					JsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);
14360
14361					n->coltype = JTC_REGULAR;
14362					n->name = $1;
14363					n->typeName = $2;
14364					n->format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);
14365					n->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *) $3;
14366					n->wrapper = $4;
14367					n->quotes = $5;
14368					n->on_empty = (JsonBehavior *) linitial($6);
14369					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) lsecond($6);
14370					n->location = @1;
14371					$$ = (Node *) n;
14372				}
14373			| ColId Typename json_format_clause
14374				json_table_column_path_clause_opt
14375				json_wrapper_behavior
14376				json_quotes_clause_opt
14377				json_behavior_clause_opt
14378				{
14379					JsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);
14380
14381					n->coltype = JTC_FORMATTED;
14382					n->name = $1;
14383					n->typeName = $2;
14384					n->format = (JsonFormat *) $3;
14385					n->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *) $4;
14386					n->wrapper = $5;
14387					n->quotes = $6;
14388					n->on_empty = (JsonBehavior *) linitial($7);
14389					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) lsecond($7);
14390					n->location = @1;
14391					$$ = (Node *) n;
14392				}
14393			| ColId Typename
14394				EXISTS json_table_column_path_clause_opt
14395				json_on_error_clause_opt
14396				{
14397					JsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);
14398
14399					n->coltype = JTC_EXISTS;
14400					n->name = $1;
14401					n->typeName = $2;
14402					n->format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);
14403					n->wrapper = JSW_NONE;
14404					n->quotes = JS_QUOTES_UNSPEC;
14405					n->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *) $4;
14406					n->on_empty = NULL;
14407					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) $5;
14408					n->location = @1;
14409					$$ = (Node *) n;
14410				}
14411			| NESTED path_opt Sconst
14412				COLUMNS '(' json_table_column_definition_list ')'
14413				{
14414					JsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);
14415
14416					n->coltype = JTC_NESTED;
14417					n->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *)
14418						makeJsonTablePathSpec($3, NULL, @3, -1);
14419					n->columns = $6;
14420					n->location = @1;
14421					$$ = (Node *) n;
14422				}
14423			| NESTED path_opt Sconst AS name
14424				COLUMNS '(' json_table_column_definition_list ')'
14425				{
14426					JsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);
14427
14428					n->coltype = JTC_NESTED;
14429					n->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *)
14430						makeJsonTablePathSpec($3, $5, @3, @5);
14431					n->columns = $8;
14432					n->location = @1;
14433					$$ = (Node *) n;
14434				}
14435		;
14436
14437path_opt:
14438			PATH
14439			| /* EMPTY */
14440		;
14441
14442json_table_column_path_clause_opt:
14443			PATH Sconst
14444				{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonTablePathSpec($2, NULL, @2, -1); }
14445			| /* EMPTY */
14446				{ $$ = NULL; }
14447		;
14448
14449/*****************************************************************************
14450 *
14451 *	Type syntax
14452 *		SQL introduces a large amount of type-specific syntax.
14453 *		Define individual clauses to handle these cases, and use
14454 *		 the generic case to handle regular type-extensible Postgres syntax.
14455 *		- thomas 1997-10-10
14456 *
14457 *****************************************************************************/
14458
14459Typename:	SimpleTypename opt_array_bounds
14460				{
14461					$$ = $1;
14462					$$->arrayBounds = $2;
14463				}
14464			| SETOF SimpleTypename opt_array_bounds
14465				{
14466					$$ = $2;
14467					$$->arrayBounds = $3;
14468					$$->setof = true;
14469				}
14470			/* SQL standard syntax, currently only one-dimensional */
14471			| SimpleTypename ARRAY '[' Iconst ']'
14472				{
14473					$$ = $1;
14474					$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger($4));
14475				}
14476			| SETOF SimpleTypename ARRAY '[' Iconst ']'
14477				{
14478					$$ = $2;
14479					$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger($5));
14480					$$->setof = true;
14481				}
14482			| SimpleTypename ARRAY
14483				{
14484					$$ = $1;
14485					$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger(-1));
14486				}
14487			| SETOF SimpleTypename ARRAY
14488				{
14489					$$ = $2;
14490					$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger(-1));
14491					$$->setof = true;
14492				}
14493		;
14494
14495opt_array_bounds:
14496			opt_array_bounds '[' ']'
14497					{  $$ = lappend($1, makeInteger(-1)); }
14498			| opt_array_bounds '[' Iconst ']'
14499					{  $$ = lappend($1, makeInteger($3)); }
14500			| /*EMPTY*/
14501					{  $$ = NIL; }
14502		;
14503
14504SimpleTypename:
14505			GenericType								{ $$ = $1; }
14506			| Numeric								{ $$ = $1; }
14507			| Bit									{ $$ = $1; }
14508			| Character								{ $$ = $1; }
14509			| ConstDatetime							{ $$ = $1; }
14510			| ConstInterval opt_interval
14511				{
14512					$$ = $1;
14513					$$->typmods = $2;
14514				}
14515			| ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')'
14516				{
14517					$$ = $1;
14518					$$->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),
14519											 makeIntConst($3, @3));
14520				}
14521			| JsonType								{ $$ = $1; }
14522		;
14523
14524/* We have a separate ConstTypename to allow defaulting fixed-length
14525 * types such as CHAR() and BIT() to an unspecified length.
14526 * SQL9x requires that these default to a length of one, but this
14527 * makes no sense for constructs like CHAR 'hi' and BIT '0101',
14528 * where there is an obvious better choice to make.
14529 * Note that ConstInterval is not included here since it must
14530 * be pushed up higher in the rules to accommodate the postfix
14531 * options (e.g. INTERVAL '1' YEAR). Likewise, we have to handle
14532 * the generic-type-name case in AexprConst to avoid premature
14533 * reduce/reduce conflicts against function names.
14534 */
14535ConstTypename:
14536			Numeric									{ $$ = $1; }
14537			| ConstBit								{ $$ = $1; }
14538			| ConstCharacter						{ $$ = $1; }
14539			| ConstDatetime							{ $$ = $1; }
14540			| JsonType								{ $$ = $1; }
14541		;
14542
14543/*
14544 * GenericType covers all type names that don't have special syntax mandated
14545 * by the standard, including qualified names.  We also allow type modifiers.
14546 * To avoid parsing conflicts against function invocations, the modifiers
14547 * have to be shown as expr_list here, but parse analysis will only accept
14548 * constants for them.
14549 */
14550GenericType:
14551			type_function_name opt_type_modifiers
14552				{
14553					$$ = makeTypeName($1);
14554					$$->typmods = $2;
14555					$$->location = @1;
14556				}
14557			| type_function_name attrs opt_type_modifiers
14558				{
14559					$$ = makeTypeNameFromNameList(lcons(makeString($1), $2));
14560					$$->typmods = $3;
14561					$$->location = @1;
14562				}
14563		;
14564
14565opt_type_modifiers: '(' expr_list ')'				{ $$ = $2; }
14566					| /* EMPTY */					{ $$ = NIL; }
14567		;
14568
14569/*
14570 * SQL numeric data types
14571 */
14572Numeric:	INT_P
14573				{
14574					$$ = SystemTypeName("int4");
14575					$$->location = @1;
14576				}
14577			| INTEGER
14578				{
14579					$$ = SystemTypeName("int4");
14580					$$->location = @1;
14581				}
14582			| SMALLINT
14583				{
14584					$$ = SystemTypeName("int2");
14585					$$->location = @1;
14586				}
14587			| BIGINT
14588				{
14589					$$ = SystemTypeName("int8");
14590					$$->location = @1;
14591				}
14592			| REAL
14593				{
14594					$$ = SystemTypeName("float4");
14595					$$->location = @1;
14596				}
14597			| FLOAT_P opt_float
14598				{
14599					$$ = $2;
14600					$$->location = @1;
14601				}
14602			| DOUBLE_P PRECISION
14603				{
14604					$$ = SystemTypeName("float8");
14605					$$->location = @1;
14606				}
14607			| DECIMAL_P opt_type_modifiers
14608				{
14609					$$ = SystemTypeName("numeric");
14610					$$->typmods = $2;
14611					$$->location = @1;
14612				}
14613			| DEC opt_type_modifiers
14614				{
14615					$$ = SystemTypeName("numeric");
14616					$$->typmods = $2;
14617					$$->location = @1;
14618				}
14619			| NUMERIC opt_type_modifiers
14620				{
14621					$$ = SystemTypeName("numeric");
14622					$$->typmods = $2;
14623					$$->location = @1;
14624				}
14625			| BOOLEAN_P
14626				{
14627					$$ = SystemTypeName("bool");
14628					$$->location = @1;
14629				}
14630		;
14631
14632opt_float:	'(' Iconst ')'
14633				{
14634					/*
14635					 * Check FLOAT() precision limits assuming IEEE floating
14636					 * types - thomas 1997-09-18
14637					 */
14638					if ($2 < 1)
14639						ereport(ERROR,
14640								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
14641								 errmsg("precision for type float must be at least 1 bit"),
14642								 parser_errposition(@2)));
14643					else if ($2 <= 24)
14644						$$ = SystemTypeName("float4");
14645					else if ($2 <= 53)
14646						$$ = SystemTypeName("float8");
14647					else
14648						ereport(ERROR,
14649								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
14650								 errmsg("precision for type float must be less than 54 bits"),
14651								 parser_errposition(@2)));
14652				}
14653			| /*EMPTY*/
14654				{
14655					$$ = SystemTypeName("float8");
14656				}
14657		;
14658
14659/*
14660 * SQL bit-field data types
14661 * The following implements BIT() and BIT VARYING().
14662 */
14663Bit:		BitWithLength
14664				{
14665					$$ = $1;
14666				}
14667			| BitWithoutLength
14668				{
14669					$$ = $1;
14670				}
14671		;
14672
14673/* ConstBit is like Bit except "BIT" defaults to unspecified length */
14674/* See notes for ConstCharacter, which addresses same issue for "CHAR" */
14675ConstBit:	BitWithLength
14676				{
14677					$$ = $1;
14678				}
14679			| BitWithoutLength
14680				{
14681					$$ = $1;
14682					$$->typmods = NIL;
14683				}
14684		;
14685
14686BitWithLength:
14687			BIT opt_varying '(' expr_list ')'
14688				{
14689					char *typname;
14690
14691					typname = $2 ? "varbit" : "bit";
14692					$$ = SystemTypeName(typname);
14693					$$->typmods = $4;
14694					$$->location = @1;
14695				}
14696		;
14697
14698BitWithoutLength:
14699			BIT opt_varying
14700				{
14701					/* bit defaults to bit(1), varbit to no limit */
14702					if ($2)
14703					{
14704						$$ = SystemTypeName("varbit");
14705					}
14706					else
14707					{
14708						$$ = SystemTypeName("bit");
14709						$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst(1, -1));
14710					}
14711					$$->location = @1;
14712				}
14713		;
14714
14715
14716/*
14717 * SQL character data types
14718 * The following implements CHAR() and VARCHAR().
14719 */
14720Character:  CharacterWithLength
14721				{
14722					$$ = $1;
14723				}
14724			| CharacterWithoutLength
14725				{
14726					$$ = $1;
14727				}
14728		;
14729
14730ConstCharacter:  CharacterWithLength
14731				{
14732					$$ = $1;
14733				}
14734			| CharacterWithoutLength
14735				{
14736					/* Length was not specified so allow to be unrestricted.
14737					 * This handles problems with fixed-length (bpchar) strings
14738					 * which in column definitions must default to a length
14739					 * of one, but should not be constrained if the length
14740					 * was not specified.
14741					 */
14742					$$ = $1;
14743					$$->typmods = NIL;
14744				}
14745		;
14746
14747CharacterWithLength:  character '(' Iconst ')'
14748				{
14749					$$ = SystemTypeName($1);
14750					$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));
14751					$$->location = @1;
14752				}
14753		;
14754
14755CharacterWithoutLength:	 character
14756				{
14757					$$ = SystemTypeName($1);
14758					/* char defaults to char(1), varchar to no limit */
14759					if (strcmp($1, "bpchar") == 0)
14760						$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst(1, -1));
14761					$$->location = @1;
14762				}
14763		;
14764
14765character:	CHARACTER opt_varying
14766										{ $$ = $2 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
14767			| CHAR_P opt_varying
14768										{ $$ = $2 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
14769			| VARCHAR
14770										{ $$ = "varchar"; }
14771			| NATIONAL CHARACTER opt_varying
14772										{ $$ = $3 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
14773			| NATIONAL CHAR_P opt_varying
14774										{ $$ = $3 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
14775			| NCHAR opt_varying
14776										{ $$ = $2 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
14777		;
14778
14779opt_varying:
14780			VARYING									{ $$ = true; }
14781			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
14782		;
14783
14784/*
14785 * SQL date/time types
14786 */
14787ConstDatetime:
14788			TIMESTAMP '(' Iconst ')' opt_timezone
14789				{
14790					if ($5)
14791						$$ = SystemTypeName("timestamptz");
14792					else
14793						$$ = SystemTypeName("timestamp");
14794					$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));
14795					$$->location = @1;
14796				}
14797			| TIMESTAMP opt_timezone
14798				{
14799					if ($2)
14800						$$ = SystemTypeName("timestamptz");
14801					else
14802						$$ = SystemTypeName("timestamp");
14803					$$->location = @1;
14804				}
14805			| TIME '(' Iconst ')' opt_timezone
14806				{
14807					if ($5)
14808						$$ = SystemTypeName("timetz");
14809					else
14810						$$ = SystemTypeName("time");
14811					$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));
14812					$$->location = @1;
14813				}
14814			| TIME opt_timezone
14815				{
14816					if ($2)
14817						$$ = SystemTypeName("timetz");
14818					else
14819						$$ = SystemTypeName("time");
14820					$$->location = @1;
14821				}
14822		;
14823
14824ConstInterval:
14825			INTERVAL
14826				{
14827					$$ = SystemTypeName("interval");
14828					$$->location = @1;
14829				}
14830		;
14831
14832opt_timezone:
14833			WITH_LA TIME ZONE						{ $$ = true; }
14834			| WITHOUT_LA TIME ZONE					{ $$ = false; }
14835			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
14836		;
14837
14838opt_interval:
14839			YEAR_P
14840				{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(YEAR), @1)); }
14841			| MONTH_P
14842				{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MONTH), @1)); }
14843			| DAY_P
14844				{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY), @1)); }
14845			| HOUR_P
14846				{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR), @1)); }
14847			| MINUTE_P
14848				{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1)); }
14849			| interval_second
14850				{ $$ = $1; }
14851			| YEAR_P TO MONTH_P
14852				{
14853					$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(YEAR) |
14854												 INTERVAL_MASK(MONTH), @1));
14855				}
14856			| DAY_P TO HOUR_P
14857				{
14858					$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |
14859												 INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR), @1));
14860				}
14861			| DAY_P TO MINUTE_P
14862				{
14863					$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |
14864												 INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |
14865												 INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1));
14866				}
14867			| DAY_P TO interval_second
14868				{
14869					$$ = $3;
14870					linitial($$) = makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |
14871												INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |
14872												INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE) |
14873												INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1);
14874				}
14875			| HOUR_P TO MINUTE_P
14876				{
14877					$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |
14878												 INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1));
14879				}
14880			| HOUR_P TO interval_second
14881				{
14882					$$ = $3;
14883					linitial($$) = makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |
14884												INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE) |
14885												INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1);
14886				}
14887			| MINUTE_P TO interval_second
14888				{
14889					$$ = $3;
14890					linitial($$) = makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE) |
14891												INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1);
14892				}
14893			| /*EMPTY*/
14894				{ $$ = NIL; }
14895		;
14896
14897interval_second:
14898			SECOND_P
14899				{
14900					$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1));
14901				}
14902			| SECOND_P '(' Iconst ')'
14903				{
14904					$$ = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1),
14905									makeIntConst($3, @3));
14906				}
14907		;
14908
14909JsonType:
14910			JSON
14911				{
14912					$$ = SystemTypeName("json");
14913					$$->location = @1;
14914				}
14915		;
14916
14917/*****************************************************************************
14918 *
14919 *	expression grammar
14920 *
14921 *****************************************************************************/
14922
14923/*
14924 * General expressions
14925 * This is the heart of the expression syntax.
14926 *
14927 * We have two expression types: a_expr is the unrestricted kind, and
14928 * b_expr is a subset that must be used in some places to avoid shift/reduce
14929 * conflicts.  For example, we can't do BETWEEN as "BETWEEN a_expr AND a_expr"
14930 * because that use of AND conflicts with AND as a boolean operator.  So,
14931 * b_expr is used in BETWEEN and we remove boolean keywords from b_expr.
14932 *
14933 * Note that '(' a_expr ')' is a b_expr, so an unrestricted expression can
14934 * always be used by surrounding it with parens.
14935 *
14936 * c_expr is all the productions that are common to a_expr and b_expr;
14937 * it's factored out just to eliminate redundant coding.
14938 *
14939 * Be careful of productions involving more than one terminal token.
14940 * By default, bison will assign such productions the precedence of their
14941 * last terminal, but in nearly all cases you want it to be the precedence
14942 * of the first terminal instead; otherwise you will not get the behavior
14943 * you expect!  So we use %prec annotations freely to set precedences.
14944 */
14945a_expr:		c_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
14946			| a_expr TYPECAST Typename
14947					{ $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }
14948			| a_expr COLLATE any_name
14949				{
14950					CollateClause *n = makeNode(CollateClause);
14951
14952					n->arg = $1;
14953					n->collname = $3;
14954					n->location = @2;
14955					$$ = (Node *) n;
14956				}
14957			| a_expr AT TIME ZONE a_expr			%prec AT
14958				{
14959					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("timezone"),
14960											   list_make2($5, $1),
14961											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
14962											   @2);
14963				}
14964			| a_expr AT LOCAL						%prec AT
14965				{
14966					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("timezone"),
14967											   list_make1($1),
14968											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
14969											   -1);
14970				}
14971		/*
14972		 * These operators must be called out explicitly in order to make use
14973		 * of bison's automatic operator-precedence handling.  All other
14974		 * operator names are handled by the generic productions using "Op",
14975		 * below; and all those operators will have the same precedence.
14976		 *
14977		 * If you add more explicitly-known operators, be sure to add them
14978		 * also to b_expr and to the MathOp list below.
14979		 */
14980			| '+' a_expr					%prec UMINUS
14981				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", NULL, $2, @1); }
14982			| '-' a_expr					%prec UMINUS
14983				{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }
14984			| a_expr '+' a_expr
14985				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", $1, $3, @2); }
14986			| a_expr '-' a_expr
14987				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "-", $1, $3, @2); }
14988			| a_expr '*' a_expr
14989				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "*", $1, $3, @2); }
14990			| a_expr '/' a_expr
14991				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "/", $1, $3, @2); }
14992			| a_expr '%' a_expr
14993				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "%", $1, $3, @2); }
14994			| a_expr '^' a_expr
14995				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "^", $1, $3, @2); }
14996			| a_expr '<' a_expr
14997				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<", $1, $3, @2); }
14998			| a_expr '>' a_expr
14999				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, ">", $1, $3, @2); }
15000			| a_expr '=' a_expr
15001				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "=", $1, $3, @2); }
15002			| a_expr LESS_EQUALS a_expr
15003				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<=", $1, $3, @2); }
15004			| a_expr GREATER_EQUALS a_expr
15005				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, ">=", $1, $3, @2); }
15006			| a_expr NOT_EQUALS a_expr
15007				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<>", $1, $3, @2); }
15008
15009			| a_expr qual_Op a_expr				%prec Op
15010				{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }
15011			| qual_Op a_expr					%prec Op
15012				{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }
15013
15014			| a_expr AND a_expr
15015				{ $$ = makeAndExpr($1, $3, @2); }
15016			| a_expr OR a_expr
15017				{ $$ = makeOrExpr($1, $3, @2); }
15018			| NOT a_expr
15019				{ $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }
15020			| NOT_LA a_expr						%prec NOT
15021				{ $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }
15022
15023			| a_expr LIKE a_expr
15024				{
15025					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, "~~",
15026												   $1, $3, @2);
15027				}
15028			| a_expr LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr					%prec LIKE
15029				{
15030					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("like_escape"),
15031												 list_make2($3, $5),
15032												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15033												 @2);
15034					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, "~~",
15035												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15036				}
15037			| a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr							%prec NOT_LA
15038				{
15039					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, "!~~",
15040												   $1, $4, @2);
15041				}
15042			| a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr			%prec NOT_LA
15043				{
15044					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("like_escape"),
15045												 list_make2($4, $6),
15046												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15047												 @2);
15048					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, "!~~",
15049												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15050				}
15051			| a_expr ILIKE a_expr
15052				{
15053					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, "~~*",
15054												   $1, $3, @2);
15055				}
15056			| a_expr ILIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr					%prec ILIKE
15057				{
15058					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("like_escape"),
15059												 list_make2($3, $5),
15060												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15061												 @2);
15062					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, "~~*",
15063												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15064				}
15065			| a_expr NOT_LA ILIKE a_expr						%prec NOT_LA
15066				{
15067					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, "!~~*",
15068												   $1, $4, @2);
15069				}
15070			| a_expr NOT_LA ILIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr			%prec NOT_LA
15071				{
15072					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("like_escape"),
15073												 list_make2($4, $6),
15074												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15075												 @2);
15076					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, "!~~*",
15077												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15078				}
15079
15080			| a_expr SIMILAR TO a_expr							%prec SIMILAR
15081				{
15082					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("similar_to_escape"),
15083												 list_make1($4),
15084												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15085												 @2);
15086					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_SIMILAR, "~",
15087												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15088				}
15089			| a_expr SIMILAR TO a_expr ESCAPE a_expr			%prec SIMILAR
15090				{
15091					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("similar_to_escape"),
15092												 list_make2($4, $6),
15093												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15094												 @2);
15095					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_SIMILAR, "~",
15096												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15097				}
15098			| a_expr NOT_LA SIMILAR TO a_expr					%prec NOT_LA
15099				{
15100					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("similar_to_escape"),
15101												 list_make1($5),
15102												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15103												 @2);
15104					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_SIMILAR, "!~",
15105												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15106				}
15107			| a_expr NOT_LA SIMILAR TO a_expr ESCAPE a_expr		%prec NOT_LA
15108				{
15109					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("similar_to_escape"),
15110												 list_make2($5, $7),
15111												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15112												 @2);
15113					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_SIMILAR, "!~",
15114												   $1, (Node *) n, @2);
15115				}
15116
15117			/* NullTest clause
15118			 * Define SQL-style Null test clause.
15119			 * Allow two forms described in the standard:
15120			 *	a IS NULL
15121			 *	a IS NOT NULL
15122			 * Allow two SQL extensions
15123			 *	a ISNULL
15124			 *	a NOTNULL
15125			 */
15126			| a_expr IS NULL_P							%prec IS
15127				{
15128					NullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);
15129
15130					n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15131					n->nulltesttype = IS_NULL;
15132					n->location = @2;
15133					$$ = (Node *) n;
15134				}
15135			| a_expr ISNULL
15136				{
15137					NullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);
15138
15139					n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15140					n->nulltesttype = IS_NULL;
15141					n->location = @2;
15142					$$ = (Node *) n;
15143				}
15144			| a_expr IS NOT NULL_P						%prec IS
15145				{
15146					NullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);
15147
15148					n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15149					n->nulltesttype = IS_NOT_NULL;
15150					n->location = @2;
15151					$$ = (Node *) n;
15152				}
15153			| a_expr NOTNULL
15154				{
15155					NullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);
15156
15157					n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15158					n->nulltesttype = IS_NOT_NULL;
15159					n->location = @2;
15160					$$ = (Node *) n;
15161				}
15162			| row OVERLAPS row
15163				{
15164					if (list_length($1) != 2)
15165						ereport(ERROR,
15166								(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
15167								 errmsg("wrong number of parameters on left side of OVERLAPS expression"),
15168								 parser_errposition(@1)));
15169					if (list_length($3) != 2)
15170						ereport(ERROR,
15171								(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
15172								 errmsg("wrong number of parameters on right side of OVERLAPS expression"),
15173								 parser_errposition(@3)));
15174					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("overlaps"),
15175											   list_concat($1, $3),
15176											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15177											   @2);
15178				}
15179			| a_expr IS TRUE_P							%prec IS
15180				{
15181					BooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);
15182
15183					b->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15184					b->booltesttype = IS_TRUE;
15185					b->location = @2;
15186					$$ = (Node *) b;
15187				}
15188			| a_expr IS NOT TRUE_P						%prec IS
15189				{
15190					BooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);
15191
15192					b->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15193					b->booltesttype = IS_NOT_TRUE;
15194					b->location = @2;
15195					$$ = (Node *) b;
15196				}
15197			| a_expr IS FALSE_P							%prec IS
15198				{
15199					BooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);
15200
15201					b->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15202					b->booltesttype = IS_FALSE;
15203					b->location = @2;
15204					$$ = (Node *) b;
15205				}
15206			| a_expr IS NOT FALSE_P						%prec IS
15207				{
15208					BooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);
15209
15210					b->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15211					b->booltesttype = IS_NOT_FALSE;
15212					b->location = @2;
15213					$$ = (Node *) b;
15214				}
15215			| a_expr IS UNKNOWN							%prec IS
15216				{
15217					BooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);
15218
15219					b->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15220					b->booltesttype = IS_UNKNOWN;
15221					b->location = @2;
15222					$$ = (Node *) b;
15223				}
15224			| a_expr IS NOT UNKNOWN						%prec IS
15225				{
15226					BooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);
15227
15228					b->arg = (Expr *) $1;
15229					b->booltesttype = IS_NOT_UNKNOWN;
15230					b->location = @2;
15231					$$ = (Node *) b;
15232				}
15233			| a_expr IS DISTINCT FROM a_expr			%prec IS
15234				{
15235					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_DISTINCT, "=", $1, $5, @2);
15236				}
15237			| a_expr IS NOT DISTINCT FROM a_expr		%prec IS
15238				{
15239					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_DISTINCT, "=", $1, $6, @2);
15240				}
15241			| a_expr BETWEEN opt_asymmetric b_expr AND a_expr		%prec BETWEEN
15242				{
15243					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_BETWEEN,
15244												   "BETWEEN",
15245												   $1,
15246												   (Node *) list_make2($4, $6),
15247												   @2);
15248				}
15249			| a_expr NOT_LA BETWEEN opt_asymmetric b_expr AND a_expr %prec NOT_LA
15250				{
15251					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_BETWEEN,
15252												   "NOT BETWEEN",
15253												   $1,
15254												   (Node *) list_make2($5, $7),
15255												   @2);
15256				}
15257			| a_expr BETWEEN SYMMETRIC b_expr AND a_expr			%prec BETWEEN
15258				{
15259					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_BETWEEN_SYM,
15260												   "BETWEEN SYMMETRIC",
15261												   $1,
15262												   (Node *) list_make2($4, $6),
15263												   @2);
15264				}
15265			| a_expr NOT_LA BETWEEN SYMMETRIC b_expr AND a_expr		%prec NOT_LA
15266				{
15267					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_BETWEEN_SYM,
15268												   "NOT BETWEEN SYMMETRIC",
15269												   $1,
15270												   (Node *) list_make2($5, $7),
15271												   @2);
15272				}
15273			| a_expr IN_P select_with_parens
15274				{
15275					/* generate foo = ANY (subquery) */
15276					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15277
15278					n->subselect = $3;
15279					n->subLinkType = ANY_SUBLINK;
15280					n->subLinkId = 0;
15281					n->testexpr = $1;
15282					n->operName = NIL;		/* show it's IN not = ANY */
15283					n->location = @2;
15284					$$ = (Node *) n;
15285				}
15286			| a_expr IN_P '(' expr_list ')'
15287				{
15288					/* generate scalar IN expression */
15289					A_Expr *n = makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_IN, "=", $1, (Node *) $4, @2);
15290
15291					n->rexpr_list_start = @3;
15292					n->rexpr_list_end = @5;
15293					$$ = (Node *) n;
15294				}
15295			| a_expr NOT_LA IN_P select_with_parens			%prec NOT_LA
15296				{
15297					/* generate NOT (foo = ANY (subquery)) */
15298					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15299
15300					n->subselect = $4;
15301					n->subLinkType = ANY_SUBLINK;
15302					n->subLinkId = 0;
15303					n->testexpr = $1;
15304					n->operName = NIL;		/* show it's IN not = ANY */
15305					n->location = @2;
15306					/* Stick a NOT on top; must have same parse location */
15307					$$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) n, @2);
15308				}
15309			| a_expr NOT_LA IN_P '(' expr_list ')'
15310				{
15311					/* generate scalar NOT IN expression */
15312					A_Expr *n = makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_IN, "<>", $1, (Node *) $5, @2);
15313
15314					n->rexpr_list_start = @4;
15315					n->rexpr_list_end = @6;
15316					$$ = (Node *) n;
15317				}
15318			| a_expr subquery_Op sub_type select_with_parens	%prec Op
15319				{
15320					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15321
15322					n->subLinkType = $3;
15323					n->subLinkId = 0;
15324					n->testexpr = $1;
15325					n->operName = $2;
15326					n->subselect = $4;
15327					n->location = @2;
15328					$$ = (Node *) n;
15329				}
15330			| a_expr subquery_Op sub_type '(' a_expr ')'		%prec Op
15331				{
15332					if ($3 == ANY_SUBLINK)
15333						$$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP_ANY, $2, $1, $5, @2);
15334					else
15335						$$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP_ALL, $2, $1, $5, @2);
15336				}
15337			| UNIQUE opt_unique_null_treatment select_with_parens
15338				{
15339					/* Not sure how to get rid of the parentheses
15340					 * but there are lots of shift/reduce errors without them.
15341					 *
15342					 * Should be able to implement this by plopping the entire
15343					 * select into a node, then transforming the target expressions
15344					 * from whatever they are into count(*), and testing the
15345					 * entire result equal to one.
15346					 * But, will probably implement a separate node in the executor.
15347					 */
15348					ereport(ERROR,
15349							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
15350							 errmsg("UNIQUE predicate is not yet implemented"),
15351							 parser_errposition(@1)));
15352				}
15353			| a_expr IS DOCUMENT_P					%prec IS
15354				{
15355					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,
15356									 list_make1($1), @2);
15357				}
15358			| a_expr IS NOT DOCUMENT_P				%prec IS
15359				{
15360					$$ = makeNotExpr(makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,
15361												 list_make1($1), @2),
15362									 @2);
15363				}
15364			| a_expr IS NORMALIZED								%prec IS
15365				{
15366					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("is_normalized"),
15367											   list_make1($1),
15368											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15369											   @2);
15370				}
15371			| a_expr IS unicode_normal_form NORMALIZED			%prec IS
15372				{
15373					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("is_normalized"),
15374											   list_make2($1, makeStringConst($3, @3)),
15375											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15376											   @2);
15377				}
15378			| a_expr IS NOT NORMALIZED							%prec IS
15379				{
15380					$$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("is_normalized"),
15381														   list_make1($1),
15382														   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15383														   @2),
15384									 @2);
15385				}
15386			| a_expr IS NOT unicode_normal_form NORMALIZED		%prec IS
15387				{
15388					$$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("is_normalized"),
15389														   list_make2($1, makeStringConst($4, @4)),
15390														   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15391														   @2),
15392									 @2);
15393				}
15394			| a_expr IS json_predicate_type_constraint
15395					json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt		%prec IS
15396				{
15397					JsonFormat *format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);
15398
15399					$$ = makeJsonIsPredicate($1, format, $3, $4, @1);
15400				}
15401			/*
15402			 * Required by SQL/JSON, but there are conflicts
15403			| a_expr
15404				json_format_clause
15405				IS  json_predicate_type_constraint
15406					json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt		%prec IS
15407				{
15408					$$ = makeJsonIsPredicate($1, $2, $4, $5, @1);
15409				}
15410			*/
15411			| a_expr IS NOT
15412					json_predicate_type_constraint
15413					json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt		%prec IS
15414				{
15415					JsonFormat *format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);
15416
15417					$$ = makeNotExpr(makeJsonIsPredicate($1, format, $4, $5, @1), @1);
15418				}
15419			/*
15420			 * Required by SQL/JSON, but there are conflicts
15421			| a_expr
15422				json_format_clause
15423				IS NOT
15424					json_predicate_type_constraint
15425					json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt		%prec IS
15426				{
15427					$$ = makeNotExpr(makeJsonIsPredicate($1, $2, $5, $6, @1), @1);
15428				}
15429			*/
15430			| DEFAULT
15431				{
15432					/*
15433					 * The SQL spec only allows DEFAULT in "contextually typed
15434					 * expressions", but for us, it's easier to allow it in
15435					 * any a_expr and then throw error during parse analysis
15436					 * if it's in an inappropriate context.  This way also
15437					 * lets us say something smarter than "syntax error".
15438					 */
15439					SetToDefault *n = makeNode(SetToDefault);
15440
15441					/* parse analysis will fill in the rest */
15442					n->location = @1;
15443					$$ = (Node *) n;
15444				}
15445		;
15446
15447/*
15448 * Restricted expressions
15449 *
15450 * b_expr is a subset of the complete expression syntax defined by a_expr.
15451 *
15452 * Presently, AND, NOT, IS, and IN are the a_expr keywords that would
15453 * cause trouble in the places where b_expr is used.  For simplicity, we
15454 * just eliminate all the boolean-keyword-operator productions from b_expr.
15455 */
15456b_expr:		c_expr
15457				{ $$ = $1; }
15458			| b_expr TYPECAST Typename
15459				{ $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }
15460			| '+' b_expr					%prec UMINUS
15461				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", NULL, $2, @1); }
15462			| '-' b_expr					%prec UMINUS
15463				{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }
15464			| b_expr '+' b_expr
15465				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", $1, $3, @2); }
15466			| b_expr '-' b_expr
15467				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "-", $1, $3, @2); }
15468			| b_expr '*' b_expr
15469				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "*", $1, $3, @2); }
15470			| b_expr '/' b_expr
15471				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "/", $1, $3, @2); }
15472			| b_expr '%' b_expr
15473				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "%", $1, $3, @2); }
15474			| b_expr '^' b_expr
15475				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "^", $1, $3, @2); }
15476			| b_expr '<' b_expr
15477				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<", $1, $3, @2); }
15478			| b_expr '>' b_expr
15479				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, ">", $1, $3, @2); }
15480			| b_expr '=' b_expr
15481				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "=", $1, $3, @2); }
15482			| b_expr LESS_EQUALS b_expr
15483				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<=", $1, $3, @2); }
15484			| b_expr GREATER_EQUALS b_expr
15485				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, ">=", $1, $3, @2); }
15486			| b_expr NOT_EQUALS b_expr
15487				{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<>", $1, $3, @2); }
15488			| b_expr qual_Op b_expr				%prec Op
15489				{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }
15490			| qual_Op b_expr					%prec Op
15491				{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }
15492			| b_expr IS DISTINCT FROM b_expr		%prec IS
15493				{
15494					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_DISTINCT, "=", $1, $5, @2);
15495				}
15496			| b_expr IS NOT DISTINCT FROM b_expr	%prec IS
15497				{
15498					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_DISTINCT, "=", $1, $6, @2);
15499				}
15500			| b_expr IS DOCUMENT_P					%prec IS
15501				{
15502					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,
15503									 list_make1($1), @2);
15504				}
15505			| b_expr IS NOT DOCUMENT_P				%prec IS
15506				{
15507					$$ = makeNotExpr(makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,
15508												 list_make1($1), @2),
15509									 @2);
15510				}
15511		;
15512
15513/*
15514 * Productions that can be used in both a_expr and b_expr.
15515 *
15516 * Note: productions that refer recursively to a_expr or b_expr mostly
15517 * cannot appear here.	However, it's OK to refer to a_exprs that occur
15518 * inside parentheses, such as function arguments; that cannot introduce
15519 * ambiguity to the b_expr syntax.
15520 */
15521c_expr:		columnref								{ $$ = $1; }
15522			| AexprConst							{ $$ = $1; }
15523			| PARAM opt_indirection
15524				{
15525					ParamRef   *p = makeNode(ParamRef);
15526
15527					p->number = $1;
15528					p->location = @1;
15529					if ($2)
15530					{
15531						A_Indirection *n = makeNode(A_Indirection);
15532
15533						n->arg = (Node *) p;
15534						n->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);
15535						$$ = (Node *) n;
15536					}
15537					else
15538						$$ = (Node *) p;
15539				}
15540			| '(' a_expr ')' opt_indirection
15541				{
15542					if ($4)
15543					{
15544						A_Indirection *n = makeNode(A_Indirection);
15545
15546						n->arg = $2;
15547						n->indirection = check_indirection($4, yyscanner);
15548						$$ = (Node *) n;
15549					}
15550					else
15551						$$ = $2;
15552				}
15553			| case_expr
15554				{ $$ = $1; }
15555			| func_expr
15556				{ $$ = $1; }
15557			| select_with_parens			%prec UMINUS
15558				{
15559					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15560
15561					n->subLinkType = EXPR_SUBLINK;
15562					n->subLinkId = 0;
15563					n->testexpr = NULL;
15564					n->operName = NIL;
15565					n->subselect = $1;
15566					n->location = @1;
15567					$$ = (Node *) n;
15568				}
15569			| select_with_parens indirection
15570				{
15571					/*
15572					 * Because the select_with_parens nonterminal is designed
15573					 * to "eat" as many levels of parens as possible, the
15574					 * '(' a_expr ')' opt_indirection production above will
15575					 * fail to match a sub-SELECT with indirection decoration;
15576					 * the sub-SELECT won't be regarded as an a_expr as long
15577					 * as there are parens around it.  To support applying
15578					 * subscripting or field selection to a sub-SELECT result,
15579					 * we need this redundant-looking production.
15580					 */
15581					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15582					A_Indirection *a = makeNode(A_Indirection);
15583
15584					n->subLinkType = EXPR_SUBLINK;
15585					n->subLinkId = 0;
15586					n->testexpr = NULL;
15587					n->operName = NIL;
15588					n->subselect = $1;
15589					n->location = @1;
15590					a->arg = (Node *) n;
15591					a->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);
15592					$$ = (Node *) a;
15593				}
15594			| EXISTS select_with_parens
15595				{
15596					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15597
15598					n->subLinkType = EXISTS_SUBLINK;
15599					n->subLinkId = 0;
15600					n->testexpr = NULL;
15601					n->operName = NIL;
15602					n->subselect = $2;
15603					n->location = @1;
15604					$$ = (Node *) n;
15605				}
15606			| ARRAY select_with_parens
15607				{
15608					SubLink	   *n = makeNode(SubLink);
15609
15610					n->subLinkType = ARRAY_SUBLINK;
15611					n->subLinkId = 0;
15612					n->testexpr = NULL;
15613					n->operName = NIL;
15614					n->subselect = $2;
15615					n->location = @1;
15616					$$ = (Node *) n;
15617				}
15618			| ARRAY array_expr
15619				{
15620					A_ArrayExpr *n = castNode(A_ArrayExpr, $2);
15621
15622					/* point outermost A_ArrayExpr to the ARRAY keyword */
15623					n->location = @1;
15624					$$ = (Node *) n;
15625				}
15626			| explicit_row
15627				{
15628					RowExpr	   *r = makeNode(RowExpr);
15629
15630					r->args = $1;
15631					r->row_typeid = InvalidOid;	/* not analyzed yet */
15632					r->colnames = NIL;	/* to be filled in during analysis */
15633					r->row_format = COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL; /* abuse */
15634					r->location = @1;
15635					$$ = (Node *) r;
15636				}
15637			| implicit_row
15638				{
15639					RowExpr	   *r = makeNode(RowExpr);
15640
15641					r->args = $1;
15642					r->row_typeid = InvalidOid;	/* not analyzed yet */
15643					r->colnames = NIL;	/* to be filled in during analysis */
15644					r->row_format = COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST; /* abuse */
15645					r->location = @1;
15646					$$ = (Node *) r;
15647				}
15648			| GROUPING '(' expr_list ')'
15649			  {
15650				  GroupingFunc *g = makeNode(GroupingFunc);
15651
15652				  g->args = $3;
15653				  g->location = @1;
15654				  $$ = (Node *) g;
15655			  }
15656		;
15657
15658func_application: func_name '(' ')'
15659				{
15660					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall($1, NIL,
15661											   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15662											   @1);
15663				}
15664			| func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'
15665				{
15666					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $3,
15667												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15668												 @1);
15669
15670					n->agg_order = $4;
15671					$$ = (Node *) n;
15672				}
15673			| func_name '(' VARIADIC func_arg_expr opt_sort_clause ')'
15674				{
15675					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, list_make1($4),
15676												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15677												 @1);
15678
15679					n->func_variadic = true;
15680					n->agg_order = $5;
15681					$$ = (Node *) n;
15682				}
15683			| func_name '(' func_arg_list ',' VARIADIC func_arg_expr opt_sort_clause ')'
15684				{
15685					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, lappend($3, $6),
15686												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15687												 @1);
15688
15689					n->func_variadic = true;
15690					n->agg_order = $7;
15691					$$ = (Node *) n;
15692				}
15693			| func_name '(' ALL func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'
15694				{
15695					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $4,
15696												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15697												 @1);
15698
15699					n->agg_order = $5;
15700					/* Ideally we'd mark the FuncCall node to indicate
15701					 * "must be an aggregate", but there's no provision
15702					 * for that in FuncCall at the moment.
15703					 */
15704					$$ = (Node *) n;
15705				}
15706			| func_name '(' DISTINCT func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'
15707				{
15708					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $4,
15709												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15710												 @1);
15711
15712					n->agg_order = $5;
15713					n->agg_distinct = true;
15714					$$ = (Node *) n;
15715				}
15716			| func_name '(' '*' ')'
15717				{
15718					/*
15719					 * We consider AGGREGATE(*) to invoke a parameterless
15720					 * aggregate.  This does the right thing for COUNT(*),
15721					 * and there are no other aggregates in SQL that accept
15722					 * '*' as parameter.
15723					 *
15724					 * The FuncCall node is also marked agg_star = true,
15725					 * so that later processing can detect what the argument
15726					 * really was.
15727					 */
15728					FuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, NIL,
15729												 COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15730												 @1);
15731
15732					n->agg_star = true;
15733					$$ = (Node *) n;
15734				}
15735		;
15736
15737
15738/*
15739 * func_expr and its cousin func_expr_windowless are split out from c_expr just
15740 * so that we have classifications for "everything that is a function call or
15741 * looks like one".  This isn't very important, but it saves us having to
15742 * document which variants are legal in places like "FROM function()" or the
15743 * backwards-compatible functional-index syntax for CREATE INDEX.
15744 * (Note that many of the special SQL functions wouldn't actually make any
15745 * sense as functional index entries, but we ignore that consideration here.)
15746 */
15747func_expr: func_application within_group_clause filter_clause over_clause
15748				{
15749					FuncCall   *n = (FuncCall *) $1;
15750
15751					/*
15752					 * The order clause for WITHIN GROUP and the one for
15753					 * plain-aggregate ORDER BY share a field, so we have to
15754					 * check here that at most one is present.  We also check
15755					 * for DISTINCT and VARIADIC here to give a better error
15756					 * location.  Other consistency checks are deferred to
15757					 * parse analysis.
15758					 */
15759					if ($2 != NIL)
15760					{
15761						if (n->agg_order != NIL)
15762							ereport(ERROR,
15763									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
15764									 errmsg("cannot use multiple ORDER BY clauses with WITHIN GROUP"),
15765									 parser_errposition(@2)));
15766						if (n->agg_distinct)
15767							ereport(ERROR,
15768									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
15769									 errmsg("cannot use DISTINCT with WITHIN GROUP"),
15770									 parser_errposition(@2)));
15771						if (n->func_variadic)
15772							ereport(ERROR,
15773									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
15774									 errmsg("cannot use VARIADIC with WITHIN GROUP"),
15775									 parser_errposition(@2)));
15776						n->agg_order = $2;
15777						n->agg_within_group = true;
15778					}
15779					n->agg_filter = $3;
15780					n->over = $4;
15781					$$ = (Node *) n;
15782				}
15783			| json_aggregate_func filter_clause over_clause
15784				{
15785					JsonAggConstructor *n = IsA($1, JsonObjectAgg) ?
15786						((JsonObjectAgg *) $1)->constructor :
15787						((JsonArrayAgg *) $1)->constructor;
15788
15789					n->agg_filter = $2;
15790					n->over = $3;
15791					$$ = (Node *) $1;
15792				}
15793			| func_expr_common_subexpr
15794				{ $$ = $1; }
15795		;
15796
15797/*
15798 * Like func_expr but does not accept WINDOW functions directly
15799 * (but they can still be contained in arguments for functions etc).
15800 * Use this when window expressions are not allowed, where needed to
15801 * disambiguate the grammar (e.g. in CREATE INDEX).
15802 */
15803func_expr_windowless:
15804			func_application						{ $$ = $1; }
15805			| func_expr_common_subexpr				{ $$ = $1; }
15806			| json_aggregate_func					{ $$ = $1; }
15807		;
15808
15809/*
15810 * Special expressions that are considered to be functions.
15811 */
15812func_expr_common_subexpr:
15813			COLLATION FOR '(' a_expr ')'
15814				{
15815					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("pg_collation_for"),
15816											   list_make1($4),
15817											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15818											   @1);
15819				}
15820			| CURRENT_DATE
15821				{
15822					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_DATE, -1, @1);
15823				}
15824			| CURRENT_TIME
15825				{
15826					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIME, -1, @1);
15827				}
15828			| CURRENT_TIME '(' Iconst ')'
15829				{
15830					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIME_N, $3, @1);
15831				}
15832			| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
15833				{
15834					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, -1, @1);
15835				}
15836			| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP '(' Iconst ')'
15837				{
15838					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_TIMESTAMP_N, $3, @1);
15839				}
15840			| LOCALTIME
15841				{
15842					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIME, -1, @1);
15843				}
15844			| LOCALTIME '(' Iconst ')'
15845				{
15846					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIME_N, $3, @1);
15847				}
15848			| LOCALTIMESTAMP
15849				{
15850					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIMESTAMP, -1, @1);
15851				}
15852			| LOCALTIMESTAMP '(' Iconst ')'
15853				{
15854					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_LOCALTIMESTAMP_N, $3, @1);
15855				}
15856			| CURRENT_ROLE
15857				{
15858					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_ROLE, -1, @1);
15859				}
15860			| CURRENT_USER
15861				{
15862					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_USER, -1, @1);
15863				}
15864			| SESSION_USER
15865				{
15866					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_SESSION_USER, -1, @1);
15867				}
15868			| SYSTEM_USER
15869				{
15870					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("system_user"),
15871											   NIL,
15872											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15873											   @1);
15874				}
15875			| USER
15876				{
15877					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_USER, -1, @1);
15878				}
15879			| CURRENT_CATALOG
15880				{
15881					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_CATALOG, -1, @1);
15882				}
15883			| CURRENT_SCHEMA
15884				{
15885					$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_SCHEMA, -1, @1);
15886				}
15887			| CAST '(' a_expr AS Typename ')'
15888				{ $$ = makeTypeCast($3, $5, @1); }
15889			| EXTRACT '(' extract_list ')'
15890				{
15891					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("extract"),
15892											   $3,
15893											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15894											   @1);
15895				}
15896			| NORMALIZE '(' a_expr ')'
15897				{
15898					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("normalize"),
15899											   list_make1($3),
15900											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15901											   @1);
15902				}
15903			| NORMALIZE '(' a_expr ',' unicode_normal_form ')'
15904				{
15905					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("normalize"),
15906											   list_make2($3, makeStringConst($5, @5)),
15907											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15908											   @1);
15909				}
15910			| OVERLAY '(' overlay_list ')'
15911				{
15912					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("overlay"),
15913											   $3,
15914											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15915											   @1);
15916				}
15917			| OVERLAY '(' func_arg_list_opt ')'
15918				{
15919					/*
15920					 * allow functions named overlay() to be called without
15921					 * special syntax
15922					 */
15923					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(list_make1(makeString("overlay")),
15924											   $3,
15925											   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15926											   @1);
15927				}
15928			| POSITION '(' position_list ')'
15929				{
15930					/*
15931					 * position(A in B) is converted to position(B, A)
15932					 *
15933					 * We deliberately don't offer a "plain syntax" option
15934					 * for position(), because the reversal of the arguments
15935					 * creates too much risk of confusion.
15936					 */
15937					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("position"),
15938											   $3,
15939											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15940											   @1);
15941				}
15942			| SUBSTRING '(' substr_list ')'
15943				{
15944					/* substring(A from B for C) is converted to
15945					 * substring(A, B, C) - thomas 2000-11-28
15946					 */
15947					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("substring"),
15948											   $3,
15949											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15950											   @1);
15951				}
15952			| SUBSTRING '(' func_arg_list_opt ')'
15953				{
15954					/*
15955					 * allow functions named substring() to be called without
15956					 * special syntax
15957					 */
15958					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(list_make1(makeString("substring")),
15959											   $3,
15960											   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15961											   @1);
15962				}
15963			| TREAT '(' a_expr AS Typename ')'
15964				{
15965					/* TREAT(expr AS target) converts expr of a particular type to target,
15966					 * which is defined to be a subtype of the original expression.
15967					 * In SQL99, this is intended for use with structured UDTs,
15968					 * but let's make this a generally useful form allowing stronger
15969					 * coercions than are handled by implicit casting.
15970					 *
15971					 * Convert SystemTypeName() to SystemFuncName() even though
15972					 * at the moment they result in the same thing.
15973					 */
15974					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(strVal(llast($5->names))),
15975											   list_make1($3),
15976											   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,
15977											   @1);
15978				}
15979			| TRIM '(' BOTH trim_list ')'
15980				{
15981					/* various trim expressions are defined in SQL
15982					 * - thomas 1997-07-19
15983					 */
15984					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("btrim"),
15985											   $4,
15986											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15987											   @1);
15988				}
15989			| TRIM '(' LEADING trim_list ')'
15990				{
15991					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("ltrim"),
15992											   $4,
15993											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
15994											   @1);
15995				}
15996			| TRIM '(' TRAILING trim_list ')'
15997				{
15998					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("rtrim"),
15999											   $4,
16000											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
16001											   @1);
16002				}
16003			| TRIM '(' trim_list ')'
16004				{
16005					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("btrim"),
16006											   $3,
16007											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
16008											   @1);
16009				}
16010			| NULLIF '(' a_expr ',' a_expr ')'
16011				{
16012					$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NULLIF, "=", $3, $5, @1);
16013				}
16014			| COALESCE '(' expr_list ')'
16015				{
16016					CoalesceExpr *c = makeNode(CoalesceExpr);
16017
16018					c->args = $3;
16019					c->location = @1;
16020					$$ = (Node *) c;
16021				}
16022			| GREATEST '(' expr_list ')'
16023				{
16024					MinMaxExpr *v = makeNode(MinMaxExpr);
16025
16026					v->args = $3;
16027					v->op = IS_GREATEST;
16028					v->location = @1;
16029					$$ = (Node *) v;
16030				}
16031			| LEAST '(' expr_list ')'
16032				{
16033					MinMaxExpr *v = makeNode(MinMaxExpr);
16034
16035					v->args = $3;
16036					v->op = IS_LEAST;
16037					v->location = @1;
16038					$$ = (Node *) v;
16039				}
16040			| XMLCONCAT '(' expr_list ')'
16041				{
16042					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLCONCAT, NULL, NIL, $3, @1);
16043				}
16044			| XMLELEMENT '(' NAME_P ColLabel ')'
16045				{
16046					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLELEMENT, $4, NIL, NIL, @1);
16047				}
16048			| XMLELEMENT '(' NAME_P ColLabel ',' xml_attributes ')'
16049				{
16050					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLELEMENT, $4, $6, NIL, @1);
16051				}
16052			| XMLELEMENT '(' NAME_P ColLabel ',' expr_list ')'
16053				{
16054					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLELEMENT, $4, NIL, $6, @1);
16055				}
16056			| XMLELEMENT '(' NAME_P ColLabel ',' xml_attributes ',' expr_list ')'
16057				{
16058					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLELEMENT, $4, $6, $8, @1);
16059				}
16060			| XMLEXISTS '(' c_expr xmlexists_argument ')'
16061				{
16062					/* xmlexists(A PASSING [BY REF] B [BY REF]) is
16063					 * converted to xmlexists(A, B)*/
16064					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("xmlexists"),
16065											   list_make2($3, $4),
16066											   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,
16067											   @1);
16068				}
16069			| XMLFOREST '(' xml_attribute_list ')'
16070				{
16071					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLFOREST, NULL, $3, NIL, @1);
16072				}
16073			| XMLPARSE '(' document_or_content a_expr xml_whitespace_option ')'
16074				{
16075					XmlExpr *x = (XmlExpr *)
16076						makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLPARSE, NULL, NIL,
16077									list_make2($4, makeBoolAConst($5, -1)),
16078									@1);
16079
16080					x->xmloption = $3;
16081					$$ = (Node *) x;
16082				}
16083			| XMLPI '(' NAME_P ColLabel ')'
16084				{
16085					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLPI, $4, NULL, NIL, @1);
16086				}
16087			| XMLPI '(' NAME_P ColLabel ',' a_expr ')'
16088				{
16089					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLPI, $4, NULL, list_make1($6), @1);
16090				}
16091			| XMLROOT '(' a_expr ',' xml_root_version opt_xml_root_standalone ')'
16092				{
16093					$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_XMLROOT, NULL, NIL,
16094									 list_make3($3, $5, $6), @1);
16095				}
16096			| XMLSERIALIZE '(' document_or_content a_expr AS SimpleTypename xml_indent_option ')'
16097				{
16098					XmlSerialize *n = makeNode(XmlSerialize);
16099
16100					n->xmloption = $3;
16101					n->expr = $4;
16102					n->typeName = $6;
16103					n->indent = $7;
16104					n->location = @1;
16105					$$ = (Node *) n;
16106				}
16107			| JSON_OBJECT '(' func_arg_list ')'
16108				{
16109					/* Support for legacy (non-standard) json_object() */
16110					$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("json_object"),
16111											   $3, COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL, @1);
16112				}
16113			| JSON_OBJECT '(' json_name_and_value_list
16114				json_object_constructor_null_clause_opt
16115				json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt
16116				json_returning_clause_opt ')'
16117				{
16118					JsonObjectConstructor *n = makeNode(JsonObjectConstructor);
16119
16120					n->exprs = $3;
16121					n->absent_on_null = $4;
16122					n->unique = $5;
16123					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $6;
16124					n->location = @1;
16125					$$ = (Node *) n;
16126				}
16127			| JSON_OBJECT '(' json_returning_clause_opt ')'
16128				{
16129					JsonObjectConstructor *n = makeNode(JsonObjectConstructor);
16130
16131					n->exprs = NULL;
16132					n->absent_on_null = false;
16133					n->unique = false;
16134					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $3;
16135					n->location = @1;
16136					$$ = (Node *) n;
16137				}
16138			| JSON_ARRAY '('
16139				json_value_expr_list
16140				json_array_constructor_null_clause_opt
16141				json_returning_clause_opt
16142			')'
16143				{
16144					JsonArrayConstructor *n = makeNode(JsonArrayConstructor);
16145
16146					n->exprs = $3;
16147					n->absent_on_null = $4;
16148					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $5;
16149					n->location = @1;
16150					$$ = (Node *) n;
16151				}
16152			| JSON_ARRAY '('
16153				select_no_parens
16154				json_format_clause_opt
16155				/* json_array_constructor_null_clause_opt */
16156				json_returning_clause_opt
16157			')'
16158				{
16159					JsonArrayQueryConstructor *n = makeNode(JsonArrayQueryConstructor);
16160
16161					n->query = $3;
16162					n->format = (JsonFormat *) $4;
16163					n->absent_on_null = true;	/* XXX */
16164					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $5;
16165					n->location = @1;
16166					$$ = (Node *) n;
16167				}
16168			| JSON_ARRAY '('
16169				json_returning_clause_opt
16170			')'
16171				{
16172					JsonArrayConstructor *n = makeNode(JsonArrayConstructor);
16173
16174					n->exprs = NIL;
16175					n->absent_on_null = true;
16176					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $3;
16177					n->location = @1;
16178					$$ = (Node *) n;
16179				}
16180			| JSON '(' json_value_expr json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt ')'
16181				{
16182					JsonParseExpr *n = makeNode(JsonParseExpr);
16183
16184					n->expr = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
16185					n->unique_keys = $4;
16186					n->output = NULL;
16187					n->location = @1;
16188					$$ = (Node *) n;
16189				}
16190			| JSON_SCALAR '(' a_expr ')'
16191				{
16192					JsonScalarExpr *n = makeNode(JsonScalarExpr);
16193
16194					n->expr = (Expr *) $3;
16195					n->output = NULL;
16196					n->location = @1;
16197					$$ = (Node *) n;
16198				}
16199			| JSON_SERIALIZE '(' json_value_expr json_returning_clause_opt ')'
16200				{
16201					JsonSerializeExpr *n = makeNode(JsonSerializeExpr);
16202
16203					n->expr = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
16204					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $4;
16205					n->location = @1;
16206					$$ = (Node *) n;
16207				}
16208			| MERGE_ACTION '(' ')'
16209				{
16210					MergeSupportFunc *m = makeNode(MergeSupportFunc);
16211
16212					m->msftype = TEXTOID;
16213					m->location = @1;
16214					$$ = (Node *) m;
16215				}
16216			| JSON_QUERY '('
16217				json_value_expr ',' a_expr json_passing_clause_opt
16218				json_returning_clause_opt
16219				json_wrapper_behavior
16220				json_quotes_clause_opt
16221				json_behavior_clause_opt
16222			')'
16223				{
16224					JsonFuncExpr *n = makeNode(JsonFuncExpr);
16225
16226					n->op = JSON_QUERY_OP;
16227					n->context_item = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
16228					n->pathspec = $5;
16229					n->passing = $6;
16230					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $7;
16231					n->wrapper = $8;
16232					n->quotes = $9;
16233					n->on_empty = (JsonBehavior *) linitial($10);
16234					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) lsecond($10);
16235					n->location = @1;
16236					$$ = (Node *) n;
16237				}
16238			| JSON_EXISTS '('
16239				json_value_expr ',' a_expr json_passing_clause_opt
16240				json_on_error_clause_opt
16241			')'
16242				{
16243					JsonFuncExpr *n = makeNode(JsonFuncExpr);
16244
16245					n->op = JSON_EXISTS_OP;
16246					n->context_item = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
16247					n->pathspec = $5;
16248					n->passing = $6;
16249					n->output = NULL;
16250					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) $7;
16251					n->location = @1;
16252					$$ = (Node *) n;
16253				}
16254			| JSON_VALUE '('
16255				json_value_expr ',' a_expr json_passing_clause_opt
16256				json_returning_clause_opt
16257				json_behavior_clause_opt
16258			')'
16259				{
16260					JsonFuncExpr *n = makeNode(JsonFuncExpr);
16261
16262					n->op = JSON_VALUE_OP;
16263					n->context_item = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
16264					n->pathspec = $5;
16265					n->passing = $6;
16266					n->output = (JsonOutput *) $7;
16267					n->on_empty = (JsonBehavior *) linitial($8);
16268					n->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) lsecond($8);
16269					n->location = @1;
16270					$$ = (Node *) n;
16271				}
16272			;
16273
16274
16275/*
16276 * SQL/XML support
16277 */
16278xml_root_version: VERSION_P a_expr
16279				{ $$ = $2; }
16280			| VERSION_P NO VALUE_P
16281				{ $$ = makeNullAConst(-1); }
16282		;
16283
16284opt_xml_root_standalone: ',' STANDALONE_P YES_P
16285				{ $$ = makeIntConst(XML_STANDALONE_YES, -1); }
16286			| ',' STANDALONE_P NO
16287				{ $$ = makeIntConst(XML_STANDALONE_NO, -1); }
16288			| ',' STANDALONE_P NO VALUE_P
16289				{ $$ = makeIntConst(XML_STANDALONE_NO_VALUE, -1); }
16290			| /*EMPTY*/
16291				{ $$ = makeIntConst(XML_STANDALONE_OMITTED, -1); }
16292		;
16293
16294xml_attributes: XMLATTRIBUTES '(' xml_attribute_list ')'	{ $$ = $3; }
16295		;
16296
16297xml_attribute_list:	xml_attribute_el					{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16298			| xml_attribute_list ',' xml_attribute_el	{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
16299		;
16300
16301xml_attribute_el: a_expr AS ColLabel
16302				{
16303					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
16304					$$->name = $3;
16305					$$->indirection = NIL;
16306					$$->val = (Node *) $1;
16307					$$->location = @1;
16308				}
16309			| a_expr
16310				{
16311					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
16312					$$->name = NULL;
16313					$$->indirection = NIL;
16314					$$->val = (Node *) $1;
16315					$$->location = @1;
16316				}
16317		;
16318
16319document_or_content: DOCUMENT_P						{ $$ = XMLOPTION_DOCUMENT; }
16320			| CONTENT_P								{ $$ = XMLOPTION_CONTENT; }
16321		;
16322
16323xml_indent_option: INDENT							{ $$ = true; }
16324			| NO INDENT								{ $$ = false; }
16325			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
16326		;
16327
16328xml_whitespace_option: PRESERVE WHITESPACE_P		{ $$ = true; }
16329			| STRIP_P WHITESPACE_P					{ $$ = false; }
16330			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = false; }
16331		;
16332
16333/* We allow several variants for SQL and other compatibility. */
16334xmlexists_argument:
16335			PASSING c_expr
16336				{
16337					$$ = $2;
16338				}
16339			| PASSING c_expr xml_passing_mech
16340				{
16341					$$ = $2;
16342				}
16343			| PASSING xml_passing_mech c_expr
16344				{
16345					$$ = $3;
16346				}
16347			| PASSING xml_passing_mech c_expr xml_passing_mech
16348				{
16349					$$ = $3;
16350				}
16351		;
16352
16353xml_passing_mech:
16354			BY REF_P
16355			| BY VALUE_P
16356		;
16357
16358
16359/*
16360 * Aggregate decoration clauses
16361 */
16362within_group_clause:
16363			WITHIN GROUP_P '(' sort_clause ')'		{ $$ = $4; }
16364			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
16365		;
16366
16367filter_clause:
16368			FILTER '(' WHERE a_expr ')'				{ $$ = $4; }
16369			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
16370		;
16371
16372
16373/*
16374 * Window Definitions
16375 */
16376window_clause:
16377			WINDOW window_definition_list			{ $$ = $2; }
16378			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
16379		;
16380
16381window_definition_list:
16382			window_definition						{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16383			| window_definition_list ',' window_definition
16384													{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
16385		;
16386
16387window_definition:
16388			ColId AS window_specification
16389				{
16390					WindowDef  *n = $3;
16391
16392					n->name = $1;
16393					$$ = n;
16394				}
16395		;
16396
16397over_clause: OVER window_specification
16398				{ $$ = $2; }
16399			| OVER ColId
16400				{
16401					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16402
16403					n->name = $2;
16404					n->refname = NULL;
16405					n->partitionClause = NIL;
16406					n->orderClause = NIL;
16407					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;
16408					n->startOffset = NULL;
16409					n->endOffset = NULL;
16410					n->location = @2;
16411					$$ = n;
16412				}
16413			| /*EMPTY*/
16414				{ $$ = NULL; }
16415		;
16416
16417window_specification: '(' opt_existing_window_name opt_partition_clause
16418						opt_sort_clause opt_frame_clause ')'
16419				{
16420					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16421
16422					n->name = NULL;
16423					n->refname = $2;
16424					n->partitionClause = $3;
16425					n->orderClause = $4;
16426					/* copy relevant fields of opt_frame_clause */
16427					n->frameOptions = $5->frameOptions;
16428					n->startOffset = $5->startOffset;
16429					n->endOffset = $5->endOffset;
16430					n->location = @1;
16431					$$ = n;
16432				}
16433		;
16434
16435/*
16436 * If we see PARTITION, RANGE, ROWS or GROUPS as the first token after the '('
16437 * of a window_specification, we want the assumption to be that there is
16438 * no existing_window_name; but those keywords are unreserved and so could
16439 * be ColIds.  We fix this by making them have the same precedence as IDENT
16440 * and giving the empty production here a slightly higher precedence, so
16441 * that the shift/reduce conflict is resolved in favor of reducing the rule.
16442 * These keywords are thus precluded from being an existing_window_name but
16443 * are not reserved for any other purpose.
16444 */
16445opt_existing_window_name: ColId						{ $$ = $1; }
16446			| /*EMPTY*/				%prec Op		{ $$ = NULL; }
16447		;
16448
16449opt_partition_clause: PARTITION BY expr_list		{ $$ = $3; }
16450			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
16451		;
16452
16453/*
16454 * For frame clauses, we return a WindowDef, but only some fields are used:
16455 * frameOptions, startOffset, and endOffset.
16456 */
16457opt_frame_clause:
16458			RANGE frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause
16459				{
16460					WindowDef  *n = $2;
16461
16462					n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_RANGE;
16463					n->frameOptions |= $3;
16464					$$ = n;
16465				}
16466			| ROWS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause
16467				{
16468					WindowDef  *n = $2;
16469
16470					n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_ROWS;
16471					n->frameOptions |= $3;
16472					$$ = n;
16473				}
16474			| GROUPS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause
16475				{
16476					WindowDef  *n = $2;
16477
16478					n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_GROUPS;
16479					n->frameOptions |= $3;
16480					$$ = n;
16481				}
16482			| /*EMPTY*/
16483				{
16484					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16485
16486					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;
16487					n->startOffset = NULL;
16488					n->endOffset = NULL;
16489					$$ = n;
16490				}
16491		;
16492
16493frame_extent: frame_bound
16494				{
16495					WindowDef  *n = $1;
16496
16497					/* reject invalid cases */
16498					if (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)
16499						ereport(ERROR,
16500								(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
16501								 errmsg("frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING"),
16502								 parser_errposition(@1)));
16503					if (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING)
16504						ereport(ERROR,
16505								(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
16506								 errmsg("frame starting from following row cannot end with current row"),
16507								 parser_errposition(@1)));
16508					n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW;
16509					$$ = n;
16510				}
16511			| BETWEEN frame_bound AND frame_bound
16512				{
16513					WindowDef  *n1 = $2;
16514					WindowDef  *n2 = $4;
16515
16516					/* form merged options */
16517					int		frameOptions = n1->frameOptions;
16518					/* shift converts START_ options to END_ options */
16519					frameOptions |= n2->frameOptions << 1;
16520					frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_BETWEEN;
16521					/* reject invalid cases */
16522					if (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)
16523						ereport(ERROR,
16524								(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
16525								 errmsg("frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING"),
16526								 parser_errposition(@2)));
16527					if (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING)
16528						ereport(ERROR,
16529								(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
16530								 errmsg("frame end cannot be UNBOUNDED PRECEDING"),
16531								 parser_errposition(@4)));
16532					if ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW) &&
16533						(frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING))
16534						ereport(ERROR,
16535								(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
16536								 errmsg("frame starting from current row cannot have preceding rows"),
16537								 parser_errposition(@4)));
16538					if ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING) &&
16539						(frameOptions & (FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING |
16540										 FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW)))
16541						ereport(ERROR,
16542								(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
16543								 errmsg("frame starting from following row cannot have preceding rows"),
16544								 parser_errposition(@4)));
16545					n1->frameOptions = frameOptions;
16546					n1->endOffset = n2->startOffset;
16547					$$ = n1;
16548				}
16549		;
16550
16551/*
16552 * This is used for both frame start and frame end, with output set up on
16553 * the assumption it's frame start; the frame_extent productions must reject
16554 * invalid cases.
16555 */
16556frame_bound:
16557			UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
16558				{
16559					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16560
16561					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING;
16562					n->startOffset = NULL;
16563					n->endOffset = NULL;
16564					$$ = n;
16565				}
16566			| UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
16567				{
16568					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16569
16570					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING;
16571					n->startOffset = NULL;
16572					n->endOffset = NULL;
16573					$$ = n;
16574				}
16575			| CURRENT_P ROW
16576				{
16577					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16578
16579					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW;
16580					n->startOffset = NULL;
16581					n->endOffset = NULL;
16582					$$ = n;
16583				}
16584			| a_expr PRECEDING
16585				{
16586					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16587
16588					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_PRECEDING;
16589					n->startOffset = $1;
16590					n->endOffset = NULL;
16591					$$ = n;
16592				}
16593			| a_expr FOLLOWING
16594				{
16595					WindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
16596
16597					n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING;
16598					n->startOffset = $1;
16599					n->endOffset = NULL;
16600					$$ = n;
16601				}
16602		;
16603
16604opt_window_exclusion_clause:
16605			EXCLUDE CURRENT_P ROW	{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_CURRENT_ROW; }
16606			| EXCLUDE GROUP_P		{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_GROUP; }
16607			| EXCLUDE TIES			{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_TIES; }
16608			| EXCLUDE NO OTHERS		{ $$ = 0; }
16609			| /*EMPTY*/				{ $$ = 0; }
16610		;
16611
16612
16613/*
16614 * Supporting nonterminals for expressions.
16615 */
16616
16617/* Explicit row production.
16618 *
16619 * SQL99 allows an optional ROW keyword, so we can now do single-element rows
16620 * without conflicting with the parenthesized a_expr production.  Without the
16621 * ROW keyword, there must be more than one a_expr inside the parens.
16622 */
16623row:		ROW '(' expr_list ')'					{ $$ = $3; }
16624			| ROW '(' ')'							{ $$ = NIL; }
16625			| '(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'			{ $$ = lappend($2, $4); }
16626		;
16627
16628explicit_row:	ROW '(' expr_list ')'				{ $$ = $3; }
16629			| ROW '(' ')'							{ $$ = NIL; }
16630		;
16631
16632implicit_row:	'(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'		{ $$ = lappend($2, $4); }
16633		;
16634
16635sub_type:	ANY										{ $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }
16636			| SOME									{ $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }
16637			| ALL									{ $$ = ALL_SUBLINK; }
16638		;
16639
