16640all_Op:		Op										{ $$ = $1; }
16641			| MathOp								{ $$ = $1; }
16642		;
16643
16644MathOp:		 '+'									{ $$ = "+"; }
16645			| '-'									{ $$ = "-"; }
16646			| '*'									{ $$ = "*"; }
16647			| '/'									{ $$ = "/"; }
16648			| '%'									{ $$ = "%"; }
16649			| '^'									{ $$ = "^"; }
16650			| '<'									{ $$ = "<"; }
16651			| '>'									{ $$ = ">"; }
16652			| '='									{ $$ = "="; }
16653			| LESS_EQUALS							{ $$ = "<="; }
16654			| GREATER_EQUALS						{ $$ = ">="; }
16655			| NOT_EQUALS							{ $$ = "<>"; }
16656		;
16657
16658qual_Op:	Op
16659					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
16660			| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'
16661					{ $$ = $3; }
16662		;
16663
16664qual_all_Op:
16665			all_Op
16666					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
16667			| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'
16668					{ $$ = $3; }
16669		;
16670
16671subquery_Op:
16672			all_Op
16673					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
16674			| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'
16675					{ $$ = $3; }
16676			| LIKE
16677					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString("~~")); }
16678			| NOT_LA LIKE
16679					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString("!~~")); }
16680			| ILIKE
16681					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString("~~*")); }
16682			| NOT_LA ILIKE
16683					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString("!~~*")); }
16684/* cannot put SIMILAR TO here, because SIMILAR TO is a hack.
16685 * the regular expression is preprocessed by a function (similar_to_escape),
16686 * and the ~ operator for posix regular expressions is used.
16687 *        x SIMILAR TO y     ->    x ~ similar_to_escape(y)
16688 * this transformation is made on the fly by the parser upwards.
16689 * however the SubLink structure which handles any/some/all stuff
16690 * is not ready for such a thing.
16691 */
16692			;
16693
16694expr_list:	a_expr
16695				{
16696					$$ = list_make1($1);
16697				}
16698			| expr_list ',' a_expr
16699				{
16700					$$ = lappend($1, $3);
16701				}
16702		;
16703
16704/* function arguments can have names */
16705func_arg_list:  func_arg_expr
16706				{
16707					$$ = list_make1($1);
16708				}
16709			| func_arg_list ',' func_arg_expr
16710				{
16711					$$ = lappend($1, $3);
16712				}
16713		;
16714
16715func_arg_expr:  a_expr
16716				{
16717					$$ = $1;
16718				}
16719			| param_name COLON_EQUALS a_expr
16720				{
16721					NamedArgExpr *na = makeNode(NamedArgExpr);
16722
16723					na->name = $1;
16724					na->arg = (Expr *) $3;
16725					na->argnumber = -1;		/* until determined */
16726					na->location = @1;
16727					$$ = (Node *) na;
16728				}
16729			| param_name EQUALS_GREATER a_expr
16730				{
16731					NamedArgExpr *na = makeNode(NamedArgExpr);
16732
16733					na->name = $1;
16734					na->arg = (Expr *) $3;
16735					na->argnumber = -1;		/* until determined */
16736					na->location = @1;
16737					$$ = (Node *) na;
16738				}
16739		;
16740
16741func_arg_list_opt:	func_arg_list					{ $$ = $1; }
16742			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
16743		;
16744
16745type_list:	Typename								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16746			| type_list ',' Typename				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
16747		;
16748
16749array_expr: '[' expr_list ']'
16750				{
16751					$$ = makeAArrayExpr($2, @1, @3);
16752				}
16753			| '[' array_expr_list ']'
16754				{
16755					$$ = makeAArrayExpr($2, @1, @3);
16756				}
16757			| '[' ']'
16758				{
16759					$$ = makeAArrayExpr(NIL, @1, @2);
16760				}
16761		;
16762
16763array_expr_list: array_expr							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16764			| array_expr_list ',' array_expr		{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
16765		;
16766
16767
16768extract_list:
16769			extract_arg FROM a_expr
16770				{
16771					$$ = list_make2(makeStringConst($1, @1), $3);
16772				}
16773		;
16774
16775/* Allow delimited string Sconst in extract_arg as an SQL extension.
16776 * - thomas 2001-04-12
16777 */
16778extract_arg:
16779			IDENT									{ $$ = $1; }
16780			| YEAR_P								{ $$ = "year"; }
16781			| MONTH_P								{ $$ = "month"; }
16782			| DAY_P									{ $$ = "day"; }
16783			| HOUR_P								{ $$ = "hour"; }
16784			| MINUTE_P								{ $$ = "minute"; }
16785			| SECOND_P								{ $$ = "second"; }
16786			| Sconst								{ $$ = $1; }
16787		;
16788
16789unicode_normal_form:
16790			NFC										{ $$ = "NFC"; }
16791			| NFD									{ $$ = "NFD"; }
16792			| NFKC									{ $$ = "NFKC"; }
16793			| NFKD									{ $$ = "NFKD"; }
16794		;
16795
16796/* OVERLAY() arguments */
16797overlay_list:
16798			a_expr PLACING a_expr FROM a_expr FOR a_expr
16799				{
16800					/* overlay(A PLACING B FROM C FOR D) is converted to overlay(A, B, C, D) */
16801					$$ = list_make4($1, $3, $5, $7);
16802				}
16803			| a_expr PLACING a_expr FROM a_expr
16804				{
16805					/* overlay(A PLACING B FROM C) is converted to overlay(A, B, C) */
16806					$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);
16807				}
16808		;
16809
16810/* position_list uses b_expr not a_expr to avoid conflict with general IN */
16811position_list:
16812			b_expr IN_P b_expr						{ $$ = list_make2($3, $1); }
16813		;
16814
16815/*
16816 * SUBSTRING() arguments
16817 *
16818 * Note that SQL:1999 has both
16819 *     text FROM int FOR int
16820 * and
16821 *     text FROM pattern FOR escape
16822 *
16823 * In the parser we map them both to a call to the substring() function and
16824 * rely on type resolution to pick the right one.
16825 *
16826 * In SQL:2003, the second variant was changed to
16827 *     text SIMILAR pattern ESCAPE escape
16828 * We could in theory map that to a different function internally, but
16829 * since we still support the SQL:1999 version, we don't.  However,
16830 * ruleutils.c will reverse-list the call in the newer style.
16831 */
16832substr_list:
16833			a_expr FROM a_expr FOR a_expr
16834				{
16835					$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);
16836				}
16837			| a_expr FOR a_expr FROM a_expr
16838				{
16839					/* not legal per SQL, but might as well allow it */
16840					$$ = list_make3($1, $5, $3);
16841				}
16842			| a_expr FROM a_expr
16843				{
16844					/*
16845					 * Because we aren't restricting data types here, this
16846					 * syntax can end up resolving to textregexsubstr().
16847					 * We've historically allowed that to happen, so continue
16848					 * to accept it.  However, ruleutils.c will reverse-list
16849					 * such a call in regular function call syntax.
16850					 */
16851					$$ = list_make2($1, $3);
16852				}
16853			| a_expr FOR a_expr
16854				{
16855					/* not legal per SQL */
16856
16857					/*
16858					 * Since there are no cases where this syntax allows
16859					 * a textual FOR value, we forcibly cast the argument
16860					 * to int4.  The possible matches in pg_proc are
16861					 * substring(text,int4) and substring(text,text),
16862					 * and we don't want the parser to choose the latter,
16863					 * which it is likely to do if the second argument
16864					 * is unknown or doesn't have an implicit cast to int4.
16865					 */
16866					$$ = list_make3($1, makeIntConst(1, -1),
16867									makeTypeCast($3,
16868												 SystemTypeName("int4"), -1));
16869				}
16870			| a_expr SIMILAR a_expr ESCAPE a_expr
16871				{
16872					$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);
16873				}
16874		;
16875
16876trim_list:	a_expr FROM expr_list					{ $$ = lappend($3, $1); }
16877			| FROM expr_list						{ $$ = $2; }
16878			| expr_list								{ $$ = $1; }
16879		;
16880
16881/*
16882 * Define SQL-style CASE clause.
16883 * - Full specification
16884 *	CASE WHEN a = b THEN c ... ELSE d END
16885 * - Implicit argument
16886 *	CASE a WHEN b THEN c ... ELSE d END
16887 */
16888case_expr:	CASE case_arg when_clause_list case_default END_P
16889				{
16890					CaseExpr   *c = makeNode(CaseExpr);
16891
16892					c->casetype = InvalidOid; /* not analyzed yet */
16893					c->arg = (Expr *) $2;
16894					c->args = $3;
16895					c->defresult = (Expr *) $4;
16896					c->location = @1;
16897					$$ = (Node *) c;
16898				}
16899		;
16900
16901when_clause_list:
16902			/* There must be at least one */
16903			when_clause								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16904			| when_clause_list when_clause			{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
16905		;
16906
16907when_clause:
16908			WHEN a_expr THEN a_expr
16909				{
16910					CaseWhen   *w = makeNode(CaseWhen);
16911
16912					w->expr = (Expr *) $2;
16913					w->result = (Expr *) $4;
16914					w->location = @1;
16915					$$ = (Node *) w;
16916				}
16917		;
16918
16919case_default:
16920			ELSE a_expr								{ $$ = $2; }
16921			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
16922		;
16923
16924case_arg:	a_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
16925			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
16926		;
16927
16928columnref:	ColId
16929				{
16930					$$ = makeColumnRef($1, NIL, @1, yyscanner);
16931				}
16932			| ColId indirection
16933				{
16934					$$ = makeColumnRef($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);
16935				}
16936		;
16937
16938indirection_el:
16939			'.' attr_name
16940				{
16941					$$ = (Node *) makeString($2);
16942				}
16943			| '.' '*'
16944				{
16945					$$ = (Node *) makeNode(A_Star);
16946				}
16947			| '[' a_expr ']'
16948				{
16949					A_Indices *ai = makeNode(A_Indices);
16950
16951					ai->is_slice = false;
16952					ai->lidx = NULL;
16953					ai->uidx = $2;
16954					$$ = (Node *) ai;
16955				}
16956			| '[' opt_slice_bound ':' opt_slice_bound ']'
16957				{
16958					A_Indices *ai = makeNode(A_Indices);
16959
16960					ai->is_slice = true;
16961					ai->lidx = $2;
16962					ai->uidx = $4;
16963					$$ = (Node *) ai;
16964				}
16965		;
16966
16967opt_slice_bound:
16968			a_expr									{ $$ = $1; }
16969			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NULL; }
16970		;
16971
16972indirection:
16973			indirection_el							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16974			| indirection indirection_el			{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
16975		;
16976
16977opt_indirection:
16978			/*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
16979			| opt_indirection indirection_el		{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
16980		;
16981
16982opt_asymmetric: ASYMMETRIC
16983			| /*EMPTY*/
16984		;
16985
16986/* SQL/JSON support */
16987json_passing_clause_opt:
16988			PASSING json_arguments					{ $$ = $2; }
16989			| /*EMPTY*/								{ $$ = NIL; }
16990		;
16991
16992json_arguments:
16993			json_argument							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
16994			| json_arguments ',' json_argument		{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
16995		;
16996
16997json_argument:
16998			json_value_expr AS ColLabel
16999			{
17000				JsonArgument *n = makeNode(JsonArgument);
17001
17002				n->val = (JsonValueExpr *) $1;
17003				n->name = $3;
17004				$$ = (Node *) n;
17005			}
17006		;
17007
17008/* ARRAY is a noise word */
17009json_wrapper_behavior:
17010			  WITHOUT WRAPPER					{ $$ = JSW_NONE; }
17011			| WITHOUT ARRAY	WRAPPER				{ $$ = JSW_NONE; }
17012			| WITH WRAPPER						{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }
17013			| WITH ARRAY WRAPPER				{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }
17014			| WITH CONDITIONAL ARRAY WRAPPER	{ $$ = JSW_CONDITIONAL; }
17015			| WITH UNCONDITIONAL ARRAY WRAPPER	{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }
17016			| WITH CONDITIONAL WRAPPER			{ $$ = JSW_CONDITIONAL; }
17017			| WITH UNCONDITIONAL WRAPPER		{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }
17018			| /* empty */						{ $$ = JSW_UNSPEC; }
17019		;
17020
17021json_behavior:
17022			DEFAULT a_expr
17023				{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonBehavior(JSON_BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT, $2, @1); }
17024			| json_behavior_type
17025				{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonBehavior($1, NULL, @1); }
17026		;
17027
17028json_behavior_type:
17029			ERROR_P		{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_ERROR; }
17030			| NULL_P	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_NULL; }
17031			| TRUE_P	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_TRUE; }
17032			| FALSE_P	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_FALSE; }
17033			| UNKNOWN	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_UNKNOWN; }
17034			| EMPTY_P ARRAY	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_EMPTY_ARRAY; }
17035			| EMPTY_P OBJECT_P	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_EMPTY_OBJECT; }
17036			/* non-standard, for Oracle compatibility only */
17037			| EMPTY_P	{ $$ = JSON_BEHAVIOR_EMPTY_ARRAY; }
17038		;
17039
17040json_behavior_clause_opt:
17041			json_behavior ON EMPTY_P
17042				{ $$ = list_make2($1, NULL); }
17043			| json_behavior ON ERROR_P
17044				{ $$ = list_make2(NULL, $1); }
17045			| json_behavior ON EMPTY_P json_behavior ON ERROR_P
17046				{ $$ = list_make2($1, $4); }
17047			| /* EMPTY */
17048				{ $$ = list_make2(NULL, NULL); }
17049		;
17050
17051json_on_error_clause_opt:
17052			json_behavior ON ERROR_P
17053				{ $$ = $1; }
17054			| /* EMPTY */
17055				{ $$ = NULL; }
17056		;
17057
17058json_value_expr:
17059			a_expr json_format_clause_opt
17060			{
17061				/* formatted_expr will be set during parse-analysis. */
17062				$$ = (Node *) makeJsonValueExpr((Expr *) $1, NULL,
17063												castNode(JsonFormat, $2));
17064			}
17065		;
17066
17067json_format_clause:
17068			FORMAT_LA JSON ENCODING name
17069				{
17070					int		encoding;
17071
17072					if (!pg_strcasecmp($4, "utf8"))
17073						encoding = JS_ENC_UTF8;
17074					else if (!pg_strcasecmp($4, "utf16"))
17075						encoding = JS_ENC_UTF16;
17076					else if (!pg_strcasecmp($4, "utf32"))
17077						encoding = JS_ENC_UTF32;
17078					else
17079						ereport(ERROR,
17080								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
17081								 errmsg("unrecognized JSON encoding: %s", $4),
17082								 parser_errposition(@4)));
17083
17084					$$ = (Node *) makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_JSON, encoding, @1);
17085				}
17086			| FORMAT_LA JSON
17087				{
17088					$$ = (Node *) makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_JSON, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, @1);
17089				}
17090		;
17091
17092json_format_clause_opt:
17093			json_format_clause
17094				{
17095					$$ = $1;
17096				}
17097			| /* EMPTY */
17098				{
17099					$$ = (Node *) makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);
17100				}
17101		;
17102
17103json_quotes_clause_opt:
17104			KEEP QUOTES ON SCALAR STRING_P		{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_KEEP; }
17105			| KEEP QUOTES						{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_KEEP; }
17106			| OMIT QUOTES ON SCALAR STRING_P	{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_OMIT; }
17107			| OMIT QUOTES						{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_OMIT; }
17108			| /* EMPTY */						{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_UNSPEC; }
17109		;
17110
17111json_returning_clause_opt:
17112			RETURNING Typename json_format_clause_opt
17113				{
17114					JsonOutput *n = makeNode(JsonOutput);
17115
17116					n->typeName = $2;
17117					n->returning = makeNode(JsonReturning);
17118					n->returning->format = (JsonFormat *) $3;
17119					$$ = (Node *) n;
17120				}
17121			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NULL; }
17122		;
17123
17124/*
17125 * We must assign the only-JSON production a precedence less than IDENT in
17126 * order to favor shifting over reduction when JSON is followed by VALUE_P,
17127 * OBJECT_P, or SCALAR.  (ARRAY doesn't need that treatment, because it's a
17128 * fully reserved word.)  Because json_predicate_type_constraint is always
17129 * followed by json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt, we also need the only-JSON
17130 * production to have precedence less than WITH and WITHOUT.  UNBOUNDED isn't
17131 * really related to this syntax, but it's a convenient choice because it
17132 * already has a precedence less than IDENT for other reasons.
17133 */
17134json_predicate_type_constraint:
17135			JSON					%prec UNBOUNDED	{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ANY; }
17136			| JSON VALUE_P							{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ANY; }
17137			| JSON ARRAY							{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ARRAY; }
17138			| JSON OBJECT_P							{ $$ = JS_TYPE_OBJECT; }
17139			| JSON SCALAR							{ $$ = JS_TYPE_SCALAR; }
17140		;
17141
17142/*
17143 * KEYS is a noise word here.  To avoid shift/reduce conflicts, assign the
17144 * KEYS-less productions a precedence less than IDENT (i.e., less than KEYS).
17145 * This prevents reducing them when the next token is KEYS.
17146 */
17147json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt:
17148			WITH UNIQUE KEYS							{ $$ = true; }
17149			| WITH UNIQUE				%prec UNBOUNDED	{ $$ = true; }
17150			| WITHOUT UNIQUE KEYS						{ $$ = false; }
17151			| WITHOUT UNIQUE			%prec UNBOUNDED	{ $$ = false; }
17152			| /* EMPTY */ 				%prec UNBOUNDED	{ $$ = false; }
17153		;
17154
17155json_name_and_value_list:
17156			json_name_and_value
17157				{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
17158			| json_name_and_value_list ',' json_name_and_value
17159				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
17160		;
17161
17162json_name_and_value:
17163/* Supporting this syntax seems to require major surgery
17164			KEY c_expr VALUE_P json_value_expr
17165				{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($2, $4); }
17166			|
17167*/
17168			c_expr VALUE_P json_value_expr
17169				{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($1, $3); }
17170			|
17171			a_expr ':' json_value_expr
17172				{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($1, $3); }
17173		;
17174
17175/* empty means false for objects, true for arrays */
17176json_object_constructor_null_clause_opt:
17177			NULL_P ON NULL_P					{ $$ = false; }
17178			| ABSENT ON NULL_P					{ $$ = true; }
17179			| /* EMPTY */						{ $$ = false; }
17180		;
17181
17182json_array_constructor_null_clause_opt:
17183			NULL_P ON NULL_P						{ $$ = false; }
17184			| ABSENT ON NULL_P						{ $$ = true; }
17185			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = true; }
17186		;
17187
17188json_value_expr_list:
17189			json_value_expr								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
17190			| json_value_expr_list ',' json_value_expr	{ $$ = lappend($1, $3);}
17191		;
17192
17193json_aggregate_func:
17194			JSON_OBJECTAGG '('
17195				json_name_and_value
17196				json_object_constructor_null_clause_opt
17197				json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt
17198				json_returning_clause_opt
17199			')'
17200				{
17201					JsonObjectAgg *n = makeNode(JsonObjectAgg);
17202
17203					n->arg = (JsonKeyValue *) $3;
17204					n->absent_on_null = $4;
17205					n->unique = $5;
17206					n->constructor = makeNode(JsonAggConstructor);
17207					n->constructor->output = (JsonOutput *) $6;
17208					n->constructor->agg_order = NULL;
17209					n->constructor->location = @1;
17210					$$ = (Node *) n;
17211				}
17212			| JSON_ARRAYAGG '('
17213				json_value_expr
17214				json_array_aggregate_order_by_clause_opt
17215				json_array_constructor_null_clause_opt
17216				json_returning_clause_opt
17217			')'
17218				{
17219					JsonArrayAgg *n = makeNode(JsonArrayAgg);
17220
17221					n->arg = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;
17222					n->absent_on_null = $5;
17223					n->constructor = makeNode(JsonAggConstructor);
17224					n->constructor->agg_order = $4;
17225					n->constructor->output = (JsonOutput *) $6;
17226					n->constructor->location = @1;
17227					$$ = (Node *) n;
17228				}
17229		;
17230
17231json_array_aggregate_order_by_clause_opt:
17232			ORDER BY sortby_list					{ $$ = $3; }
17233			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NIL; }
17234		;
17235
17236/*****************************************************************************
17237 *
17238 *	target list for SELECT
17239 *
17240 *****************************************************************************/
17241
17242opt_target_list: target_list						{ $$ = $1; }
17243			| /* EMPTY */							{ $$ = NIL; }
17244		;
17245
17246target_list:
17247			target_el								{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
17248			| target_list ',' target_el				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
17249		;
17250
17251target_el:	a_expr AS ColLabel
17252				{
17253					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
17254					$$->name = $3;
17255					$$->indirection = NIL;
17256					$$->val = (Node *) $1;
17257					$$->location = @1;
17258				}
17259			| a_expr BareColLabel
17260				{
17261					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
17262					$$->name = $2;
17263					$$->indirection = NIL;
17264					$$->val = (Node *) $1;
17265					$$->location = @1;
17266				}
17267			| a_expr
17268				{
17269					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
17270					$$->name = NULL;
17271					$$->indirection = NIL;
17272					$$->val = (Node *) $1;
17273					$$->location = @1;
17274				}
17275			| '*'
17276				{
17277					ColumnRef  *n = makeNode(ColumnRef);
17278
17279					n->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));
17280					n->location = @1;
17281
17282					$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
17283					$$->name = NULL;
17284					$$->indirection = NIL;
17285					$$->val = (Node *) n;
17286					$$->location = @1;
17287				}
17288		;
17289
17290
17291/*****************************************************************************
17292 *
17293 *	Names and constants
17294 *
17295 *****************************************************************************/
17296
17297qualified_name_list:
17298			qualified_name							{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
17299			| qualified_name_list ',' qualified_name { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
17300		;
17301
17302/*
17303 * The production for a qualified relation name has to exactly match the
17304 * production for a qualified func_name, because in a FROM clause we cannot
17305 * tell which we are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' for a
17306 * func_name, something else for a relation). Therefore we allow 'indirection'
17307 * which may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.
17308 */
17309qualified_name:
17310			ColId
17311				{
17312					$$ = makeRangeVar(NULL, $1, @1);
17313				}
17314			| ColId indirection
17315				{
17316					$$ = makeRangeVarFromQualifiedName($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);
17317				}
17318		;
17319
17320name_list:	name
17321					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
17322			| name_list ',' name
17323					{ $$ = lappend($1, makeString($3)); }
17324		;
17325
17326
17327name:		ColId									{ $$ = $1; };
17328
17329attr_name:	ColLabel								{ $$ = $1; };
17330
17331file_name:	Sconst									{ $$ = $1; };
17332
17333/*
17334 * The production for a qualified func_name has to exactly match the
17335 * production for a qualified columnref, because we cannot tell which we
17336 * are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' or Sconst for a func_name,
17337 * anything else for a columnref).  Therefore we allow 'indirection' which
17338 * may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.  (If we
17339 * ever implement SQL99-like methods, such syntax may actually become legal!)
17340 */
17341func_name:	type_function_name
17342					{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
17343			| ColId indirection
17344					{
17345						$$ = check_func_name(lcons(makeString($1), $2),
17346											 yyscanner);
17347					}
17348		;
17349
17350
17351/*
17352 * Constants
17353 */
17354AexprConst: Iconst
17355				{
17356					$$ = makeIntConst($1, @1);
17357				}
17358			| FCONST
17359				{
17360					$$ = makeFloatConst($1, @1);
17361				}
17362			| Sconst
17363				{
17364					$$ = makeStringConst($1, @1);
17365				}
17366			| BCONST
17367				{
17368					$$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);
17369				}
17370			| XCONST
17371				{
17372					/* This is a bit constant per SQL99:
17373					 * Without Feature F511, "BIT data type",
17374					 * a <general literal> shall not be a
17375					 * <bit string literal> or a <hex string literal>.
17376					 */
17377					$$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);
17378				}
17379			| func_name Sconst
17380				{
17381					/* generic type 'literal' syntax */
17382					TypeName   *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);
17383
17384					t->location = @1;
17385					$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);
17386				}
17387			| func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')' Sconst
17388				{
17389					/* generic syntax with a type modifier */
17390					TypeName   *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);
17391					ListCell   *lc;
17392
17393					/*
17394					 * We must use func_arg_list and opt_sort_clause in the
17395					 * production to avoid reduce/reduce conflicts, but we
17396					 * don't actually wish to allow NamedArgExpr in this
17397					 * context, nor ORDER BY.
17398					 */
17399					foreach(lc, $3)
17400					{
17401						NamedArgExpr *arg = (NamedArgExpr *) lfirst(lc);
17402
17403						if (IsA(arg, NamedArgExpr))
17404							ereport(ERROR,
17405									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
17406									 errmsg("type modifier cannot have parameter name"),
17407									 parser_errposition(arg->location)));
17408					}
17409					if ($4 != NIL)
17410							ereport(ERROR,
17411									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
17412									 errmsg("type modifier cannot have ORDER BY"),
17413									 parser_errposition(@4)));
17414
17415					t->typmods = $3;
17416					t->location = @1;
17417					$$ = makeStringConstCast($6, @6, t);
17418				}
17419			| ConstTypename Sconst
17420				{
17421					$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, $1);
17422				}
17423			| ConstInterval Sconst opt_interval
17424				{
17425					TypeName   *t = $1;
17426
17427					t->typmods = $3;
17428					$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);
17429				}
17430			| ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')' Sconst
17431				{
17432					TypeName   *t = $1;
17433
17434					t->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),
17435											makeIntConst($3, @3));
17436					$$ = makeStringConstCast($5, @5, t);
17437				}
17438			| TRUE_P
17439				{
17440					$$ = makeBoolAConst(true, @1);
17441				}
17442			| FALSE_P
17443				{
17444					$$ = makeBoolAConst(false, @1);
17445				}
17446			| NULL_P
17447				{
17448					$$ = makeNullAConst(@1);
17449				}
17450		;
17451
17452Iconst:		ICONST									{ $$ = $1; };
17453Sconst:		SCONST									{ $$ = $1; };
17454
17455SignedIconst: Iconst								{ $$ = $1; }
17456			| '+' Iconst							{ $$ = + $2; }
17457			| '-' Iconst							{ $$ = - $2; }
17458		;
17459
17460/* Role specifications */
17461RoleId:		RoleSpec
17462				{
17463					RoleSpec   *spc = (RoleSpec *) $1;
17464
17465					switch (spc->roletype)
17466					{
17467						case ROLESPEC_CSTRING:
17468							$$ = spc->rolename;
17469							break;
17470						case ROLESPEC_PUBLIC:
17471							ereport(ERROR,
17472									(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
17473									 errmsg("role name \"%s\" is reserved",
17474											"public"),
17475									 parser_errposition(@1)));
17476							break;
17477						case ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER:
17478							ereport(ERROR,
17479									(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
17480									 errmsg("%s cannot be used as a role name here",
17481											"SESSION_USER"),
17482									 parser_errposition(@1)));
17483							break;
17484						case ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER:
17485							ereport(ERROR,
17486									(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
17487									 errmsg("%s cannot be used as a role name here",
17488											"CURRENT_USER"),
17489									 parser_errposition(@1)));
17490							break;
17491						case ROLESPEC_CURRENT_ROLE:
17492							ereport(ERROR,
17493									(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
17494									 errmsg("%s cannot be used as a role name here",
17495											"CURRENT_ROLE"),
17496									 parser_errposition(@1)));
17497							break;
17498					}
17499				}
17500			;
17501
17502RoleSpec:	NonReservedWord
17503				{
17504					/*
17505					 * "public" and "none" are not keywords, but they must
17506					 * be treated specially here.
17507					 */
17508					RoleSpec   *n;
17509
17510					if (strcmp($1, "public") == 0)
17511					{
17512						n = (RoleSpec *) makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_PUBLIC, @1);
17513						n->roletype = ROLESPEC_PUBLIC;
17514					}
17515					else if (strcmp($1, "none") == 0)
17516					{
17517						ereport(ERROR,
17518								(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
17519								 errmsg("role name \"%s\" is reserved",
17520										"none"),
17521								 parser_errposition(@1)));
17522					}
17523					else
17524					{
17525						n = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CSTRING, @1);
17526						n->rolename = pstrdup($1);
17527					}
17528					$$ = n;
17529				}
17530			| CURRENT_ROLE
17531				{
17532					$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_ROLE, @1);
17533				}
17534			| CURRENT_USER
17535				{
17536					$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER, @1);
17537				}
17538			| SESSION_USER
17539				{
17540					$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER, @1);
17541				}
17542		;
17543
17544role_list:	RoleSpec
17545				{ $$ = list_make1($1); }
17546			| role_list ',' RoleSpec
17547				{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
17548		;
17549
17550
17551/*****************************************************************************
17552 *
17553 * PL/pgSQL extensions
17554 *
17555 * You'd think a PL/pgSQL "expression" should be just an a_expr, but
17556 * historically it can include just about anything that can follow SELECT.
17557 * Therefore the returned struct is a SelectStmt.
17558 *****************************************************************************/
17559
17560PLpgSQL_Expr: opt_distinct_clause opt_target_list
17561			from_clause where_clause
17562			group_clause having_clause window_clause
17563			opt_sort_clause opt_select_limit opt_for_locking_clause
17564				{
17565					SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
17566
17567					n->distinctClause = $1;
17568					n->targetList = $2;
17569					n->fromClause = $3;
17570					n->whereClause = $4;
17571					n->groupClause = ($5)->list;
17572					n->groupDistinct = ($5)->distinct;
17573					n->havingClause = $6;
17574					n->windowClause = $7;
17575					n->sortClause = $8;
17576					if ($9)
17577					{
17578						n->limitOffset = $9->limitOffset;
17579						n->limitCount = $9->limitCount;
17580						if (!n->sortClause &&
17581							$9->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES)
17582							ereport(ERROR,
17583									(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
17584									 errmsg("WITH TIES cannot be specified without ORDER BY clause"),
17585									 parser_errposition($9->optionLoc)));
17586						n->limitOption = $9->limitOption;
17587					}
17588					n->lockingClause = $10;
17589					$$ = (Node *) n;
17590				}
17591		;
17592
17593/*
17594 * PL/pgSQL Assignment statement: name opt_indirection := PLpgSQL_Expr
17595 */
17596
17597PLAssignStmt: plassign_target opt_indirection plassign_equals PLpgSQL_Expr
17598				{
17599					PLAssignStmt *n = makeNode(PLAssignStmt);
17600
17601					n->name = $1;
17602					n->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);
17603					/* nnames will be filled by calling production */
17604					n->val = (SelectStmt *) $4;
17605					n->location = @1;
17606					$$ = (Node *) n;
17607				}
17608		;
17609
17610plassign_target: ColId							{ $$ = $1; }
17611			| PARAM								{ $$ = psprintf("$%d", $1); }
17612		;
17613
17614plassign_equals: COLON_EQUALS
17615			| '='
17616		;
17617
17618
17619/*
17620 * Name classification hierarchy.
17621 *
17622 * IDENT is the lexeme returned by the lexer for identifiers that match
17623 * no known keyword.  In most cases, we can accept certain keywords as
17624 * names, not only IDENTs.	We prefer to accept as many such keywords
17625 * as possible to minimize the impact of "reserved words" on programmers.
17626 * So, we divide names into several possible classes.  The classification
17627 * is chosen in part to make keywords acceptable as names wherever possible.
17628 */
17629
17630/* Column identifier --- names that can be column, table, etc names.
17631 */
17632ColId:		IDENT									{ $$ = $1; }
17633			| unreserved_keyword					{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17634			| col_name_keyword						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17635		;
17636
17637/* Type/function identifier --- names that can be type or function names.
17638 */
17639type_function_name:	IDENT							{ $$ = $1; }
17640			| unreserved_keyword					{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17641			| type_func_name_keyword				{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17642		;
17643
17644/* Any not-fully-reserved word --- these names can be, eg, role names.
17645 */
17646NonReservedWord:	IDENT							{ $$ = $1; }
17647			| unreserved_keyword					{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17648			| col_name_keyword						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17649			| type_func_name_keyword				{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17650		;
17651
17652/* Column label --- allowed labels in "AS" clauses.
17653 * This presently includes *all* Postgres keywords.
17654 */
17655ColLabel:	IDENT									{ $$ = $1; }
17656			| unreserved_keyword					{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17657			| col_name_keyword						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17658			| type_func_name_keyword				{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17659			| reserved_keyword						{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17660		;
17661
17662/* Bare column label --- names that can be column labels without writing "AS".
17663 * This classification is orthogonal to the other keyword categories.
17664 */
17665BareColLabel:	IDENT								{ $$ = $1; }
17666			| bare_label_keyword					{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }
17667		;
17668
17669
17670/*
17671 * Keyword category lists.  Generally, every keyword present in
17672 * the Postgres grammar should appear in exactly one of these lists.
17673 *
17674 * Put a new keyword into the first list that it can go into without causing
17675 * shift or reduce conflicts.  The earlier lists define "less reserved"
17676 * categories of keywords.
17677 *
17678 * Make sure that each keyword's category in kwlist.h matches where
17679 * it is listed here.  (Someday we may be able to generate these lists and
17680 * kwlist.h's table from one source of truth.)
17681 */
17682
17683/* "Unreserved" keywords --- available for use as any kind of name.
17684 */
17685unreserved_keyword:
17686			  ABORT_P
17687			| ABSENT
17688			| ABSOLUTE_P
17689			| ACCESS
17690			| ACTION
17691			| ADD_P
17692			| ADMIN
17693			| AFTER
17694			| AGGREGATE
17695			| ALSO
17696			| ALTER
17697			| ALWAYS
17698			| ASENSITIVE
17699			| ASSERTION
17700			| ASSIGNMENT
17701			| AT
17702			| ATOMIC
17703			| ATTACH
17704			| ATTRIBUTE
17705			| BACKWARD
17706			| BEFORE
17707			| BEGIN_P
17708			| BREADTH
17709			| BY
17710			| CACHE
17711			| CALL
17712			| CALLED
17713			| CASCADE

17716			| CHAIN
17717			| CHARACTERISTICS

17719			| CLASS

17721			| CLUSTER

17723			| COMMENT
17724			| COMMENTS
17725			| COMMIT

17938			| SECOND_P
17939			| SECURITY

17944			| SESSION
17945			| SET

17947			| SHARE
17948			| SHOW
17949			| SIMPLE

17951			| SNAPSHOT

17954			| STABLE
17955			| STANDALONE_P
17956			| START

17960			| STDOUT

17970			| TABLES

17973			| TEMP
17974			| TEMPLATE
17975			| TEMPORARY
17976			| TEXT_P
17977			| TIES
17978			| TRANSACTION
17979			| TRANSFORM
17980			| TRIGGER
17981			| TRUNCATE
17982			| TRUSTED
17983			| TYPE_P
17984			| TYPES_P
17985			| UESCAPE
17986			| UNBOUNDED
17987			| UNCOMMITTED
17988			| UNCONDITIONAL
17989			| UNENCRYPTED
17990			| UNKNOWN
17991			| UNLISTEN
17992			| UNLOGGED
17993			| UNTIL
17994			| UPDATE
17995			| VACUUM
17996			| VALID
17997			| VALIDATE
17998			| VALIDATOR
17999			| VALUE_P
18000			| VARYING
18001			| VERSION_P
18002			| VIEW
18003			| VIEWS
18004			| VIRTUAL
18005			| VOLATILE
18006			| WHITESPACE_P
18007			| WITHIN
18008			| WITHOUT
18009			| WORK
18010			| WRAPPER
18011			| WRITE
18012			| XML_P
18013			| YEAR_P
18014			| YES_P
18015			| ZONE
18016		;
18017
18018/* Column identifier --- keywords that can be column, table, etc names.
18019 *
18020 * Many of these keywords will in fact be recognized as type or function
18021 * names too; but they have special productions for the purpose, and so
18022 * can't be treated as "generic" type or function names.
18023 *
18024 * The type names appearing here are not usable as function names
18025 * because they can be followed by '(' in typename productions, which
18026 * looks too much like a function call for an LR(1) parser.
18027 */
18028col_name_keyword:
18029			  BETWEEN
18030			| BIGINT
18031			| BIT
18032			| BOOLEAN_P
18033			| CHAR_P
18034			| CHARACTER
18035			| COALESCE
18036			| DEC
18037			| DECIMAL_P
18038			| EXISTS
18039			| EXTRACT
18040			| FLOAT_P
18041			| GREATEST
18042			| GROUPING
18043			| INOUT
18044			| INT_P
18045			| INTEGER
18046			| INTERVAL
18047			| JSON
18048			| JSON_ARRAY
18049			| JSON_ARRAYAGG
18050			| JSON_EXISTS
18051			| JSON_OBJECT
18052			| JSON_OBJECTAGG
18053			| JSON_QUERY
18054			| JSON_SCALAR
18055			| JSON_SERIALIZE
18056			| JSON_TABLE
18057			| JSON_VALUE
18058			| LEAST
18059			| MERGE_ACTION
18060			| NATIONAL
18061			| NCHAR
18062			| NONE
18063			| NORMALIZE
18064			| NULLIF
18065			| NUMERIC
18066			| OUT_P
18067			| OVERLAY
18068			| POSITION
18069			| PRECISION
18070			| REAL
18071			| ROW
18072			| SETOF
18073			| SMALLINT
18074			| SUBSTRING
18075			| TIME
18076			| TIMESTAMP
18077			| TREAT
18078			| TRIM
18079			| VALUES
18080			| VARCHAR
18081			| XMLATTRIBUTES
18082			| XMLCONCAT
18083			| XMLELEMENT
18084			| XMLEXISTS
18085			| XMLFOREST
18086			| XMLNAMESPACES
18087			| XMLPARSE
18088			| XMLPI
18089			| XMLROOT
18090			| XMLSERIALIZE
18091			| XMLTABLE
18092		;
18093
18094/* Type/function identifier --- keywords that can be type or function names.
18095 *
18096 * Most of these are keywords that are used as operators in expressions;
18097 * in general such keywords can't be column names because they would be
18098 * ambiguous with variables, but they are unambiguous as function identifiers.
18099 *
18100 * Do not include POSITION, SUBSTRING, etc here since they have explicit
18101 * productions in a_expr to support the goofy SQL9x argument syntax.
18102 * - thomas 2000-11-28
18103 */
18104type_func_name_keyword:
18105			  AUTHORIZATION
18106			| BINARY
18107			| COLLATION
18108			| CONCURRENTLY
18109			| CROSS
18110			| CURRENT_SCHEMA
18111			| FREEZE
18112			| FULL
18113			| ILIKE
18114			| INNER_P
18115			| IS
18116			| ISNULL
18117			| JOIN
18118			| LEFT
18119			| LIKE
18120			| NATURAL
18121			| NOTNULL
18122			| OUTER_P
18123			| OVERLAPS
18124			| RIGHT
18125			| SIMILAR
18126			| TABLESAMPLE
18127			| VERBOSE
18128		;
18129
18130/* Reserved keyword --- these keywords are usable only as a ColLabel.
18131 *
18132 * Keywords appear here if they could not be distinguished from variable,
18133 * type, or function names in some contexts.  Don't put things here unless
18134 * forced to.
18135 */
18136reserved_keyword:
18137			  ALL
18138			| ANALYSE
18139			| ANALYZE
18140			| AND
18141			| ANY
18142			| ARRAY
18143			| AS
18144			| ASC
18145			| ASYMMETRIC
18146			| BOTH
18147			| CASE
18148			| CAST
18149			| CHECK
18150			| COLLATE
18151			| COLUMN
18152			| CONSTRAINT
18153			| CREATE
18154			| CURRENT_CATALOG
18155			| CURRENT_DATE
18156			| CURRENT_ROLE
18157			| CURRENT_TIME
18158			| CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
18159			| CURRENT_USER
18160			| DEFAULT
18161			| DEFERRABLE
18162			| DESC
18163			| DISTINCT
18164			| DO
18165			| ELSE
18166			| END_P
18167			| EXCEPT
18168			| FALSE_P
18169			| FETCH
18170			| FOR
18171			| FOREIGN
18172			| FROM
18173			| GRANT
18174			| GROUP_P
18175			| HAVING
18176			| IN_P
18177			| INITIALLY
18178			| INTERSECT
18179			| INTO
18180			| LATERAL_P
18181			| LEADING
18182			| LIMIT
18183			| LOCALTIME
18184			| LOCALTIMESTAMP
18185			| NOT
18186			| NULL_P
18187			| OFFSET
18188			| ON
18189			| ONLY
18190			| OR
18191			| ORDER
18192			| PLACING
18193			| PRIMARY
18194			| REFERENCES
18195			| RETURNING
18196			| SELECT
18197			| SESSION_USER
18198			| SOME
18199			| SYMMETRIC
18200			| SYSTEM_USER
18201			| TABLE
18202			| THEN
18203			| TO
18204			| TRAILING
18205			| TRUE_P
18206			| UNION
18207			| UNIQUE
18208			| USER
18209			| USING
18210			| VARIADIC
18211			| WHEN
18212			| WHERE
18213			| WINDOW
18214			| WITH
18215		;
18216
18217/*
18218 * While all keywords can be used as column labels when preceded by AS,
18219 * not all of them can be used as a "bare" column label without AS.
18220 * Those that can be used as a bare label must be listed here,
18221 * in addition to appearing in one of the category lists above.
18222 *
18223 * Always add a new keyword to this list if possible.  Mark it BARE_LABEL
18224 * in kwlist.h if it is included here, or AS_LABEL if it is not.
18225 */
18226bare_label_keyword:
18227			  ABORT_P
18228			| ABSENT
18229			| ABSOLUTE_P

18232			| ADD_P

18684%%
18685
18686/*
18687 * The signature of this function is required by bison.  However, we
18688 * ignore the passed yylloc and instead use the last token position
18689 * available from the scanner.
18690 */
18691static void
18692base_yyerror(YYLTYPE *yylloc, core_yyscan_t yyscanner, const char *msg)
18693{
18694	parser_yyerror(msg);
18695}
18696
18697static RawStmt *
18698makeRawStmt(Node *stmt, int stmt_location)
18699{
18700	RawStmt    *rs = makeNode(RawStmt);
18701
18702	rs->stmt = stmt;
18703	rs->stmt_location = stmt_location;
18704	rs->stmt_len = 0;			/* might get changed later */
18705	return rs;
18706}
18707
18708/* Adjust a RawStmt to reflect that it doesn't run to the end of the string */
18709static void
18710updateRawStmtEnd(RawStmt *rs, int end_location)
18711{
18712	/*
18713	 * If we already set the length, don't change it.  This is for situations
18714	 * like "select foo ;; select bar" where the same statement will be last
18715	 * in the string for more than one semicolon.
18716	 */
18717	if (rs->stmt_len > 0)
18718		return;
18719
18720	/* OK, update length of RawStmt */
18721	rs->stmt_len = end_location - rs->stmt_location;
18722}
18723
18724static Node *
18725makeColumnRef(char *colname, List *indirection,
18726			  int location, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
18727{
18728	/*
18729	 * Generate a ColumnRef node, with an A_Indirection node added if there is
18730	 * any subscripting in the specified indirection list.  However, any field
18731	 * selection at the start of the indirection list must be transposed into
18732	 * the "fields" part of the ColumnRef node.
18733	 */
18734	ColumnRef  *c = makeNode(ColumnRef);
18735	int			nfields = 0;
18736	ListCell   *l;
18737
18738	c->location = location;
18739	foreach(l, indirection)
18740	{
18741		if (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Indices))
18742		{
18743			A_Indirection *i = makeNode(A_Indirection);
18744
18745			if (nfields == 0)
18746			{
18747				/* easy case - all indirection goes to A_Indirection */
18748				c->fields = list_make1(makeString(colname));
18749				i->indirection = check_indirection(indirection, yyscanner);
18750			}
18751			else
18752			{
18753				/* got to split the list in two */
18754				i->indirection = check_indirection(list_copy_tail(indirection,
18755																  nfields),
18756												   yyscanner);
18757				indirection = list_truncate(indirection, nfields);
18758				c->fields = lcons(makeString(colname), indirection);
18759			}
18760			i->arg = (Node *) c;
18761			return (Node *) i;
18762		}
18763		else if (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Star))
18764		{
18765			/* We only allow '*' at the end of a ColumnRef */
18766			if (lnext(indirection, l) != NULL)
18767				parser_yyerror("improper use of \"*\"");
18768		}
18769		nfields++;
18770	}
18771	/* No subscripting, so all indirection gets added to field list */
18772	c->fields = lcons(makeString(colname), indirection);
18773	return (Node *) c;
18774}
18775
18776static Node *
18777makeTypeCast(Node *arg, TypeName *typename, int location)
18778{
18779	TypeCast   *n = makeNode(TypeCast);
18780
18781	n->arg = arg;
18782	n->typeName = typename;
18783	n->location = location;
18784	return (Node *) n;
18785}
18786
18787static Node *
18788makeStringConstCast(char *str, int location, TypeName *typename)
18789{
18790	Node	   *s = makeStringConst(str, location);
18791
18792	return makeTypeCast(s, typename, -1);
18793}
18794
18795static Node *
18796makeIntConst(int val, int location)
18797{
18798	A_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);
18799
18800	n->val.ival.type = T_Integer;
18801	n->val.ival.ival = val;
18802	n->location = location;
18803
18804	return (Node *) n;
18805}
18806
18807static Node *
18808makeFloatConst(char *str, int location)
18809{
18810	A_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);
18811
18812	n->val.fval.type = T_Float;
18813	n->val.fval.fval = str;
18814	n->location = location;
18815
18816	return (Node *) n;
18817}
18818
18819static Node *
18820makeBoolAConst(bool state, int location)
18821{
18822	A_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);
18823
18824	n->val.boolval.type = T_Boolean;
18825	n->val.boolval.boolval = state;
18826	n->location = location;
18827
18828	return (Node *) n;
18829}
18830
18831static Node *
18832makeBitStringConst(char *str, int location)
18833{
18834	A_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);
18835
18836	n->val.bsval.type = T_BitString;
18837	n->val.bsval.bsval = str;
18838	n->location = location;
18839
18840	return (Node *) n;
18841}
18842
18843static Node *
18844makeNullAConst(int location)
18845{
18846	A_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);
18847
18848	n->isnull = true;
18849	n->location = location;
18850
18851	return (Node *) n;
18852}
18853
18854static Node *
18855makeAConst(Node *v, int location)
18856{
18857	Node	   *n;
18858
18859	switch (v->type)
18860	{
18861		case T_Float:
18862			n = makeFloatConst(castNode(Float, v)->fval, location);
18863			break;
18864
18865		case T_Integer:
18866			n = makeIntConst(castNode(Integer, v)->ival, location);
18867			break;
18868
18869		default:
18870			/* currently not used */
18871			Assert(false);
18872			n = NULL;
18873	}
18874
18875	return n;
18876}
18877
18878/* makeRoleSpec
18879 * Create a RoleSpec with the given type
18880 */
18881static RoleSpec *
18882makeRoleSpec(RoleSpecType type, int location)
18883{
18884	RoleSpec   *spec = makeNode(RoleSpec);
18885
18886	spec->roletype = type;
18887	spec->location = location;
18888
18889	return spec;
18890}
18891
18892/* check_qualified_name --- check the result of qualified_name production
18893 *
18894 * It's easiest to let the grammar production for qualified_name allow
18895 * subscripts and '*', which we then must reject here.
18896 */
18897static void
18898check_qualified_name(List *names, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
18899{
18900	ListCell   *i;
18901
18902	foreach(i, names)
18903	{
18904		if (!IsA(lfirst(i), String))
18905			parser_yyerror("syntax error");
18906	}
18907}
18908
18909/* check_func_name --- check the result of func_name production
18910 *
18911 * It's easiest to let the grammar production for func_name allow subscripts
18912 * and '*', which we then must reject here.
18913 */
18914static List *
18915check_func_name(List *names, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
18916{
18917	ListCell   *i;
18918
18919	foreach(i, names)
18920	{
18921		if (!IsA(lfirst(i), String))
18922			parser_yyerror("syntax error");
18923	}
18924	return names;
18925}
18926
18927/* check_indirection --- check the result of indirection production
18928 *
18929 * We only allow '*' at the end of the list, but it's hard to enforce that
18930 * in the grammar, so do it here.
18931 */
18932static List *
18933check_indirection(List *indirection, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
18934{
18935	ListCell   *l;
18936
18937	foreach(l, indirection)
18938	{
18939		if (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Star))
18940		{
18941			if (lnext(indirection, l) != NULL)
18942				parser_yyerror("improper use of \"*\"");
18943		}
18944	}
18945	return indirection;
18946}
18947
18948/* extractArgTypes()
18949 * Given a list of FunctionParameter nodes, extract a list of just the
18950 * argument types (TypeNames) for input parameters only.  This is what
18951 * is needed to look up an existing function, which is what is wanted by
18952 * the productions that use this call.
18953 */
18954static List *
18955extractArgTypes(List *parameters)
18956{
18957	List	   *result = NIL;
18958	ListCell   *i;
18959
18960	foreach(i, parameters)
18961	{
18962		FunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) lfirst(i);
18963
18964		if (p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_OUT && p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_TABLE)
18965			result = lappend(result, p->argType);
18966	}
18967	return result;
18968}
18969
18970/* extractAggrArgTypes()
18971 * As above, but work from the output of the aggr_args production.
18972 */
18973static List *
18974extractAggrArgTypes(List *aggrargs)
18975{
18976	Assert(list_length(aggrargs) == 2);
18977	return extractArgTypes((List *) linitial(aggrargs));
18978}
18979
18980/* makeOrderedSetArgs()
18981 * Build the result of the aggr_args production (which see the comments for).
18982 * This handles only the case where both given lists are nonempty, so that
18983 * we have to deal with multiple VARIADIC arguments.
18984 */
18985static List *
18986makeOrderedSetArgs(List *directargs, List *orderedargs,
18987				   core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
18988{
18989	FunctionParameter *lastd = (FunctionParameter *) llast(directargs);
18990	Integer    *ndirectargs;
18991
18992	/* No restriction unless last direct arg is VARIADIC */
18993	if (lastd->mode == FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC)
18994	{
18995		FunctionParameter *firsto = (FunctionParameter *) linitial(orderedargs);
18996
18997		/*
18998		 * We ignore the names, though the aggr_arg production allows them; it
18999		 * doesn't allow default values, so those need not be checked.
19000		 */
19001		if (list_length(orderedargs) != 1 ||
19002			firsto->mode != FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC ||
19003			!equal(lastd->argType, firsto->argType))
19004			ereport(ERROR,
19005					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19006					 errmsg("an ordered-set aggregate with a VARIADIC direct argument must have one VARIADIC aggregated argument of the same data type"),
19007					 parser_errposition(firsto->location)));
19008
19009		/* OK, drop the duplicate VARIADIC argument from the internal form */
19010		orderedargs = NIL;
19011	}
19012
19013	/* don't merge into the next line, as list_concat changes directargs */
19014	ndirectargs = makeInteger(list_length(directargs));
19015
19016	return list_make2(list_concat(directargs, orderedargs),
19017					  ndirectargs);
19018}
19019
19020/* insertSelectOptions()
19021 * Insert ORDER BY, etc into an already-constructed SelectStmt.
19022 *
19023 * This routine is just to avoid duplicating code in SelectStmt productions.
19024 */
19025static void
19026insertSelectOptions(SelectStmt *stmt,
19027					List *sortClause, List *lockingClause,
19028					SelectLimit *limitClause,
19029					WithClause *withClause,
19030					core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19031{
19032	Assert(IsA(stmt, SelectStmt));
19033
19034	/*
19035	 * Tests here are to reject constructs like
19036	 *	(SELECT foo ORDER BY bar) ORDER BY baz
19037	 */
19038	if (sortClause)
19039	{
19040		if (stmt->sortClause)
19041			ereport(ERROR,
19042					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19043					 errmsg("multiple ORDER BY clauses not allowed"),
19044					 parser_errposition(exprLocation((Node *) sortClause))));
19045		stmt->sortClause = sortClause;
19046	}
19047	/* We can handle multiple locking clauses, though */
19048	stmt->lockingClause = list_concat(stmt->lockingClause, lockingClause);
19049	if (limitClause && limitClause->limitOffset)
19050	{
19051		if (stmt->limitOffset)
19052			ereport(ERROR,
19053					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19054					 errmsg("multiple OFFSET clauses not allowed"),
19055					 parser_errposition(limitClause->offsetLoc)));
19056		stmt->limitOffset = limitClause->limitOffset;
19057	}
19058	if (limitClause && limitClause->limitCount)
19059	{
19060		if (stmt->limitCount)
19061			ereport(ERROR,
19062					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19063					 errmsg("multiple LIMIT clauses not allowed"),
19064					 parser_errposition(limitClause->countLoc)));
19065		stmt->limitCount = limitClause->limitCount;
19066	}
19067	if (limitClause)
19068	{
19069		/* If there was a conflict, we must have detected it above */
19070		Assert(!stmt->limitOption);
19071		if (!stmt->sortClause && limitClause->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES)
19072			ereport(ERROR,
19073					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19074					 errmsg("WITH TIES cannot be specified without ORDER BY clause"),
19075					 parser_errposition(limitClause->optionLoc)));
19076		if (limitClause->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES && stmt->lockingClause)
19077		{
19078			ListCell   *lc;
19079
19080			foreach(lc, stmt->lockingClause)
19081			{
19082				LockingClause *lock = lfirst_node(LockingClause, lc);
19083
19084				if (lock->waitPolicy == LockWaitSkip)
19085					ereport(ERROR,
19086							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19087							 errmsg("%s and %s options cannot be used together",
19088									"SKIP LOCKED", "WITH TIES"),
19089							 parser_errposition(limitClause->optionLoc)));
19090			}
19091		}
19092		stmt->limitOption = limitClause->limitOption;
19093	}
19094	if (withClause)
19095	{
19096		if (stmt->withClause)
19097			ereport(ERROR,
19098					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19099					 errmsg("multiple WITH clauses not allowed"),
19100					 parser_errposition(exprLocation((Node *) withClause))));
19101		stmt->withClause = withClause;
19102	}
19103}
19104
19105static Node *
19106makeSetOp(SetOperation op, bool all, Node *larg, Node *rarg)
19107{
19108	SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
19109
19110	n->op = op;
19111	n->all = all;
19112	n->larg = (SelectStmt *) larg;
19113	n->rarg = (SelectStmt *) rarg;
19114	return (Node *) n;
19115}
19116
19117/* SystemFuncName()
19118 * Build a properly-qualified reference to a built-in function.
19119 */
19120List *
19121SystemFuncName(char *name)
19122{
19123	return list_make2(makeString("pg_catalog"), makeString(name));
19124}
19125
19126/* SystemTypeName()
19127 * Build a properly-qualified reference to a built-in type.
19128 *
19129 * typmod is defaulted, but may be changed afterwards by caller.
19130 * Likewise for the location.
19131 */
19132TypeName *
19133SystemTypeName(char *name)
19134{
19135	return makeTypeNameFromNameList(list_make2(makeString("pg_catalog"),
19136											   makeString(name)));
19137}
19138
19139/* doNegate()
19140 * Handle negation of a numeric constant.
19141 *
19142 * Formerly, we did this here because the optimizer couldn't cope with
19143 * indexquals that looked like "var = -4" --- it wants "var = const"
19144 * and a unary minus operator applied to a constant didn't qualify.
19145 * As of Postgres 7.0, that problem doesn't exist anymore because there
19146 * is a constant-subexpression simplifier in the optimizer.  However,
19147 * there's still a good reason for doing this here, which is that we can
19148 * postpone committing to a particular internal representation for simple
19149 * negative constants.	It's better to leave "-123.456" in string form
19150 * until we know what the desired type is.
19151 */
19152static Node *
19153doNegate(Node *n, int location)
19154{
19155	if (IsA(n, A_Const))
19156	{
19157		A_Const    *con = (A_Const *) n;
19158
19159		/* report the constant's location as that of the '-' sign */
19160		con->location = location;
19161
19162		if (IsA(&con->val, Integer))
19163		{
19164			con->val.ival.ival = -con->val.ival.ival;
19165			return n;
19166		}
19167		if (IsA(&con->val, Float))
19168		{
19169			doNegateFloat(&con->val.fval);
19170			return n;
19171		}
19172	}
19173
19174	return (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "-", NULL, n, location);
19175}
19176
19177static void
19178doNegateFloat(Float *v)
19179{
19180	char	   *oldval = v->fval;
19181
19182	if (*oldval == '+')
19183		oldval++;
19184	if (*oldval == '-')
19185		v->fval = oldval + 1;	/* just strip the '-' */
19186	else
19187		v->fval = psprintf("-%s", oldval);
19188}
19189
19190static Node *
19191makeAndExpr(Node *lexpr, Node *rexpr, int location)
19192{
19193	/* Flatten "a AND b AND c ..." to a single BoolExpr on sight */
19194	if (IsA(lexpr, BoolExpr))
19195	{
19196		BoolExpr   *blexpr = (BoolExpr *) lexpr;
19197
19198		if (blexpr->boolop == AND_EXPR)
19199		{
19200			blexpr->args = lappend(blexpr->args, rexpr);
19201			return (Node *) blexpr;
19202		}
19203	}
19204	return (Node *) makeBoolExpr(AND_EXPR, list_make2(lexpr, rexpr), location);
19205}
19206
19207static Node *
19208makeOrExpr(Node *lexpr, Node *rexpr, int location)
19209{
19210	/* Flatten "a OR b OR c ..." to a single BoolExpr on sight */
19211	if (IsA(lexpr, BoolExpr))
19212	{
19213		BoolExpr   *blexpr = (BoolExpr *) lexpr;
19214
19215		if (blexpr->boolop == OR_EXPR)
19216		{
19217			blexpr->args = lappend(blexpr->args, rexpr);
19218			return (Node *) blexpr;
19219		}
19220	}
19221	return (Node *) makeBoolExpr(OR_EXPR, list_make2(lexpr, rexpr), location);
19222}
19223
19224static Node *
19225makeNotExpr(Node *expr, int location)
19226{
19227	return (Node *) makeBoolExpr(NOT_EXPR, list_make1(expr), location);
19228}
19229
19230static Node *
19231makeAArrayExpr(List *elements, int location, int location_end)
19232{
19233	A_ArrayExpr *n = makeNode(A_ArrayExpr);
19234
19235	n->elements = elements;
19236	n->location = location;
19237	n->list_start = location;
19238	n->list_end = location_end;
19239	return (Node *) n;
19240}
19241
19242static Node *
19243makeSQLValueFunction(SQLValueFunctionOp op, int32 typmod, int location)
19244{
19245	SQLValueFunction *svf = makeNode(SQLValueFunction);
19246
19247	svf->op = op;
19248	/* svf->type will be filled during parse analysis */
19249	svf->typmod = typmod;
19250	svf->location = location;
19251	return (Node *) svf;
19252}
19253
19254static Node *
19255makeXmlExpr(XmlExprOp op, char *name, List *named_args, List *args,
19256			int location)
19257{
19258	XmlExpr    *x = makeNode(XmlExpr);
19259
19260	x->op = op;
19261	x->name = name;
19262
19263	/*
19264	 * named_args is a list of ResTarget; it'll be split apart into separate
19265	 * expression and name lists in transformXmlExpr().
19266	 */
19267	x->named_args = named_args;
19268	x->arg_names = NIL;
19269	x->args = args;
19270	/* xmloption, if relevant, must be filled in by caller */
19271	/* type and typmod will be filled in during parse analysis */
19272	x->type = InvalidOid;		/* marks the node as not analyzed */
19273	x->location = location;
19274	return (Node *) x;
19275}
19276
19277/*
19278 * Merge the input and output parameters of a table function.
19279 */
19280static List *
19281mergeTableFuncParameters(List *func_args, List *columns, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19282{
19283	ListCell   *lc;
19284
19285	/* Explicit OUT and INOUT parameters shouldn't be used in this syntax */
19286	foreach(lc, func_args)
19287	{
19288		FunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) lfirst(lc);
19289
19290		if (p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_DEFAULT &&
19291			p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_IN &&
19292			p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC)
19293			ereport(ERROR,
19294					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19295					 errmsg("OUT and INOUT arguments aren't allowed in TABLE functions"),
19296					 parser_errposition(p->location)));
19297	}
19298
19299	return list_concat(func_args, columns);
19300}
19301
19302/*
19303 * Determine return type of a TABLE function.  A single result column
19304 * returns setof that column's type; otherwise return setof record.
19305 */
19306static TypeName *
19307TableFuncTypeName(List *columns)
19308{
19309	TypeName   *result;
19310
19311	if (list_length(columns) == 1)
19312	{
19313		FunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) linitial(columns);
19314
19315		result = copyObject(p->argType);
19316	}
19317	else
19318		result = SystemTypeName("record");
19319
19320	result->setof = true;
19321
19322	return result;
19323}
19324
19325/*
19326 * Convert a list of (dotted) names to a RangeVar (like
19327 * makeRangeVarFromNameList, but with position support).  The
19328 * "AnyName" refers to the any_name production in the grammar.
19329 */
19330static RangeVar *
19331makeRangeVarFromAnyName(List *names, int position, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19332{
19333	RangeVar   *r = makeNode(RangeVar);
19334
19335	switch (list_length(names))
19336	{
19337		case 1:
19338			r->catalogname = NULL;
19339			r->schemaname = NULL;
19340			r->relname = strVal(linitial(names));
19341			break;
19342		case 2:
19343			r->catalogname = NULL;
19344			r->schemaname = strVal(linitial(names));
19345			r->relname = strVal(lsecond(names));
19346			break;
19347		case 3:
19348			r->catalogname = strVal(linitial(names));
19349			r->schemaname = strVal(lsecond(names));
19350			r->relname = strVal(lthird(names));
19351			break;
19352		default:
19353			ereport(ERROR,
19354					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19355					 errmsg("improper qualified name (too many dotted names): %s",
19356							NameListToString(names)),
19357					 parser_errposition(position)));
19358			break;
19359	}
19360
19361	r->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT;
19362	r->location = position;
19363
19364	return r;
19365}
19366
19367/*
19368 * Convert a relation_name with name and namelist to a RangeVar using
19369 * makeRangeVar.
19370 */
19371static RangeVar *
19372makeRangeVarFromQualifiedName(char *name, List *namelist, int location,
19373							  core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19374{
19375	RangeVar   *r;
19376
19377	check_qualified_name(namelist, yyscanner);
19378	r = makeRangeVar(NULL, NULL, location);
19379
19380	switch (list_length(namelist))
19381	{
19382		case 1:
19383			r->catalogname = NULL;
19384			r->schemaname = name;
19385			r->relname = strVal(linitial(namelist));
19386			break;
19387		case 2:
19388			r->catalogname = name;
19389			r->schemaname = strVal(linitial(namelist));
19390			r->relname = strVal(lsecond(namelist));
19391			break;
19392		default:
19393			ereport(ERROR,
19394					errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19395					errmsg("improper qualified name (too many dotted names): %s",
19396						   NameListToString(lcons(makeString(name), namelist))),
19397					parser_errposition(location));
19398			break;
19399	}
19400
19401	return r;
19402}
19403
19404/* Separate Constraint nodes from COLLATE clauses in a ColQualList */
19405static void
19406SplitColQualList(List *qualList,
19407				 List **constraintList, CollateClause **collClause,
19408				 core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19409{
19410	ListCell   *cell;
19411
19412	*collClause = NULL;
19413	foreach(cell, qualList)
19414	{
19415		Node	   *n = (Node *) lfirst(cell);
19416
19417		if (IsA(n, Constraint))
19418		{
19419			/* keep it in list */
19420			continue;
19421		}
19422		if (IsA(n, CollateClause))
19423		{
19424			CollateClause *c = (CollateClause *) n;
19425
19426			if (*collClause)
19427				ereport(ERROR,
19428						(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19429						 errmsg("multiple COLLATE clauses not allowed"),
19430						 parser_errposition(c->location)));
19431			*collClause = c;
19432		}
19433		else
19434			elog(ERROR, "unexpected node type %d", (int) n->type);
19435		/* remove non-Constraint nodes from qualList */
19436		qualList = foreach_delete_current(qualList, cell);
19437	}
19438	*constraintList = qualList;
19439}
19440
19441/*
19442 * Process result of ConstraintAttributeSpec, and set appropriate bool flags
19443 * in the output command node.  Pass NULL for any flags the particular
19444 * command doesn't support.
19445 */
19446static void
19447processCASbits(int cas_bits, int location, const char *constrType,
19448			   bool *deferrable, bool *initdeferred, bool *is_enforced,
19449			   bool *not_valid, bool *no_inherit, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19450{
19451	/* defaults */
19452	if (deferrable)
19453		*deferrable = false;
19454	if (initdeferred)
19455		*initdeferred = false;
19456	if (not_valid)
19457		*not_valid = false;
19458	if (is_enforced)
19459		*is_enforced = true;
19460
19461	if (cas_bits & (CAS_DEFERRABLE | CAS_INITIALLY_DEFERRED))
19462	{
19463		if (deferrable)
19464			*deferrable = true;
19465		else
19466			ereport(ERROR,
19467					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19468			/* translator: %s is CHECK, UNIQUE, or similar */
19469					 errmsg("%s constraints cannot be marked DEFERRABLE",
19470							constrType),
19471					 parser_errposition(location)));
19472	}
19473
19474	if (cas_bits & CAS_INITIALLY_DEFERRED)
19475	{
19476		if (initdeferred)
19477			*initdeferred = true;
19478		else
19479			ereport(ERROR,
19480					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19481			/* translator: %s is CHECK, UNIQUE, or similar */
19482					 errmsg("%s constraints cannot be marked DEFERRABLE",
19483							constrType),
19484					 parser_errposition(location)));
19485	}
19486
19487	if (cas_bits & CAS_NOT_VALID)
19488	{
19489		if (not_valid)
19490			*not_valid = true;
19491		else
19492			ereport(ERROR,
19493					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19494			/* translator: %s is CHECK, UNIQUE, or similar */
19495					 errmsg("%s constraints cannot be marked NOT VALID",
19496							constrType),
19497					 parser_errposition(location)));
19498	}
19499
19500	if (cas_bits & CAS_NO_INHERIT)
19501	{
19502		if (no_inherit)
19503			*no_inherit = true;
19504		else
19505			ereport(ERROR,
19506					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19507			/* translator: %s is CHECK, UNIQUE, or similar */
19508					 errmsg("%s constraints cannot be marked NO INHERIT",
19509							constrType),
19510					 parser_errposition(location)));
19511	}
19512
19513	if (cas_bits & CAS_NOT_ENFORCED)
19514	{
19515		if (is_enforced)
19516			*is_enforced = false;
19517		else
19518			ereport(ERROR,
19519					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19520					 /* translator: %s is CHECK, UNIQUE, or similar */
19521					 errmsg("%s constraints cannot be marked NOT ENFORCED",
19522							constrType),
19523					 parser_errposition(location)));
19524
19525		/*
19526		 * NB: The validated status is irrelevant when the constraint is set to
19527		 * NOT ENFORCED, but for consistency, it should be set accordingly.
19528		 * This ensures that if the constraint is later changed to ENFORCED, it
19529		 * will automatically be in the correct NOT VALIDATED state.
19530		 */
19531		if (not_valid)
19532			*not_valid = true;
19533	}
19534
19535	if (cas_bits & CAS_ENFORCED)
19536	{
19537		if (is_enforced)
19538			*is_enforced = true;
19539		else
19540			ereport(ERROR,
19541					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
19542					 /* translator: %s is CHECK, UNIQUE, or similar */
19543					 errmsg("%s constraints cannot be marked ENFORCED",
19544							constrType),
19545					 parser_errposition(location)));
19546	}
19547}
19548
19549/*
19550 * Parse a user-supplied partition strategy string into parse node
19551 * PartitionStrategy representation, or die trying.
19552 */
19553static PartitionStrategy
19554parsePartitionStrategy(char *strategy, int location, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19555{
19556	if (pg_strcasecmp(strategy, "list") == 0)
19557		return PARTITION_STRATEGY_LIST;
19558	else if (pg_strcasecmp(strategy, "range") == 0)
19559		return PARTITION_STRATEGY_RANGE;
19560	else if (pg_strcasecmp(strategy, "hash") == 0)
19561		return PARTITION_STRATEGY_HASH;
19562
19563	ereport(ERROR,
19564			(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
19565			 errmsg("unrecognized partitioning strategy \"%s\"", strategy),
19566			 parser_errposition(location)));
19567	return PARTITION_STRATEGY_LIST; /* keep compiler quiet */
19568
19569}
19570
19571/*
19572 * Process pubobjspec_list to check for errors in any of the objects and
19573 * convert PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION into appropriate PublicationObjSpecType.
19574 */
19575static void
19576preprocess_pubobj_list(List *pubobjspec_list, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
19577{
19578	ListCell   *cell;
19579	PublicationObjSpec *pubobj;
19580	PublicationObjSpecType prevobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION;
19581
19582	if (!pubobjspec_list)
19583		return;
19584
19585	pubobj = (PublicationObjSpec *) linitial(pubobjspec_list);
19586	if (pubobj->pubobjtype == PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION)
19587		ereport(ERROR,
19588				errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19589				errmsg("invalid publication object list"),
19590				errdetail("One of TABLE or TABLES IN SCHEMA must be specified before a standalone table or schema name."),
19591				parser_errposition(pubobj->location));
19592
19593	foreach(cell, pubobjspec_list)
19594	{
19595		pubobj = (PublicationObjSpec *) lfirst(cell);
19596
19597		if (pubobj->pubobjtype == PUBLICATIONOBJ_CONTINUATION)
19598			pubobj->pubobjtype = prevobjtype;
19599
19600		if (pubobj->pubobjtype == PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLE)
19601		{
19602			/* relation name or pubtable must be set for this type of object */
19603			if (!pubobj->name && !pubobj->pubtable)
19604				ereport(ERROR,
19605						errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19606						errmsg("invalid table name"),
19607						parser_errposition(pubobj->location));
19608
19609			if (pubobj->name)
19610			{
19611				/* convert it to PublicationTable */
19612				PublicationTable *pubtable = makeNode(PublicationTable);
19613
19614				pubtable->relation =
19615					makeRangeVar(NULL, pubobj->name, pubobj->location);
19616				pubobj->pubtable = pubtable;
19617				pubobj->name = NULL;
19618			}
19619		}
19620		else if (pubobj->pubobjtype == PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLES_IN_SCHEMA ||
19621				 pubobj->pubobjtype == PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLES_IN_CUR_SCHEMA)
19622		{
19623			/* WHERE clause is not allowed on a schema object */
19624			if (pubobj->pubtable && pubobj->pubtable->whereClause)
19625				ereport(ERROR,
19626						errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19627						errmsg("WHERE clause not allowed for schema"),
19628						parser_errposition(pubobj->location));
19629
19630			/* Column list is not allowed on a schema object */
19631			if (pubobj->pubtable && pubobj->pubtable->columns)
19632				ereport(ERROR,
19633						errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19634						errmsg("column specification not allowed for schema"),
19635						parser_errposition(pubobj->location));
19636
19637			/*
19638			 * We can distinguish between the different type of schema objects
19639			 * based on whether name and pubtable is set.
19640			 */
19641			if (pubobj->name)
19642				pubobj->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLES_IN_SCHEMA;
19643			else if (!pubobj->name && !pubobj->pubtable)
19644				pubobj->pubobjtype = PUBLICATIONOBJ_TABLES_IN_CUR_SCHEMA;
19645			else
19646				ereport(ERROR,
19647						errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
19648						errmsg("invalid schema name"),
19649						parser_errposition(pubobj->location));
19650		}
19651
19652		prevobjtype = pubobj->pubobjtype;
19653	}
19654}
19655
19656/*----------
19657 * Recursive view transformation
19658 *
19659 * Convert
19660 *
19661 *     CREATE RECURSIVE VIEW relname (aliases) AS query
19662 *
19663 * to
19664 *
19665 *     CREATE VIEW relname (aliases) AS
19666 *         WITH RECURSIVE relname (aliases) AS (query)
19667 *         SELECT aliases FROM relname
19668 *
19669 * Actually, just the WITH ... part, which is then inserted into the original
19670 * view definition as the query.
19671 * ----------
19672 */
19673static Node *
19674makeRecursiveViewSelect(char *relname, List *aliases, Node *query)
19675{
19676	SelectStmt *s = makeNode(SelectStmt);
19677	WithClause *w = makeNode(WithClause);
19678	CommonTableExpr *cte = makeNode(CommonTableExpr);
19679	List	   *tl = NIL;
19680	ListCell   *lc;
19681
19682	/* create common table expression */
19683	cte->ctename = relname;
19684	cte->aliascolnames = aliases;
19685	cte->ctematerialized = CTEMaterializeDefault;
19686	cte->ctequery = query;
19687	cte->location = -1;
19688
19689	/* create WITH clause and attach CTE */
19690	w->recursive = true;
19691	w->ctes = list_make1(cte);
19692	w->location = -1;
19693
19694	/*
19695	 * create target list for the new SELECT from the alias list of the
19696	 * recursive view specification
19697	 */
19698	foreach(lc, aliases)
19699	{
19700		ResTarget  *rt = makeNode(ResTarget);
19701
19702		rt->name = NULL;
19703		rt->indirection = NIL;
19704		rt->val = makeColumnRef(strVal(lfirst(lc)), NIL, -1, 0);
19705		rt->location = -1;
19706
19707		tl = lappend(tl, rt);
19708	}
19709
19710	/*
19711	 * create new SELECT combining WITH clause, target list, and fake FROM
19712	 * clause
19713	 */
19714	s->withClause = w;
19715	s->targetList = tl;
19716	s->fromClause = list_make1(makeRangeVar(NULL, relname, -1));
19717
19718	return (Node *) s;
19719}
19720
19721/* parser_init()
19722 * Initialize to parse one query string
19723 */
19724void
19725parser_init(base_yy_extra_type *yyext)
19726{
19727	yyext->parsetree = NIL;		/* in case grammar forgets to set it */
19728}
